Our intended outcomes for the workshop include assembling a corpus of inspiring examples and a design space, ideally consolidated into a survey paper, as well as the establishment of new collaborations and a shared research agenda. We anticipate a format comprised of short presentations and demos, an invited keynote or fireside chat, and a breakout group session organized around specific application domains. Website: https://sites.google.com/view/mercadoworkshop. 我们建议在 IEEE VIS 2023 上举办为期半天的研讨会，主题是围绕数据的通信和协作。具体来说，我们的目标是聚集对组织和教育环境中多模式、同步、远程或混合形式的沟通和协作感兴趣的研究人员。该主题位于数据可视化、人机交互和计算机支持的协作工作的交叉点，并且与之前的几个研讨会和讲习班在主题上重叠。我们对研讨会的预期成果包括收集一系列鼓舞人心的例子和一个设计空间，最好将其合并到一份调查论文中，以及建立新的合作和共同的研究议程。我们预期的形式包括简短的演示和演示、受邀的主题演讲或炉边聊天以及围绕特定应用领域组织的分组会议。网站：https://sites.google.com/view/mercadoworkshop。 该论文提出在IEEE VIS 2023举办为期半天的工作坊，聚焦数据驱动的远程多模态通信与协作技术。 Matthew Brehmer,Maxime Cordeil,Christophe Hurter,Takayuki Itoh cs.HC

authors_text: Sung-Feng Huang,Chia-ping Chen,Zhi-Sheng Chen,Yu-Pao Tsai,Hung-yi Lee
title: Personalized Lightweight Text-to-Speech: Voice Cloning with Adaptive Structured Pruning
title_zh: 个性化轻量级文本转语音：具有自适应结构化修剪的语音克隆
abstract: Personalized TTS is an exciting and highly desired application that allows users to train their TTS voice using only a few recordings. However, TTS training typically requires many hours of recording and a large model, making it unsuitable for deployment on mobile devices. To overcome this limitation, related works typically require fine-tuning a pre-trained TTS model to preserve its ability to generate high-quality audio samples while adapting to the target speaker's voice. This process is commonly referred to as ``voice cloning.'' Although related works have achieved significant success in changing the TTS model's voice, they are still required to fine-tune from a large pre-trained model, resulting in a significant size for the voice-cloned model. In this paper, we propose applying trainable structured pruning to voice cloning. By training the structured pruning masks with voice-cloning data, we can produce a unique pruned model for each target speaker. Our experiments demonstrate that using learnable structured pruning, we can compress the model size to 7 times smaller while achieving comparable voice-cloning performance.
summary: Personalized TTS is an exciting and highly desired application that allows users to train their TTS voice using only a few recordings. However, TTS training typically requires many hours of recording and a large model, making it unsuitable for deployment on mobile devices. To overcome this limitation, related works typically require fine-tuning a pre-trained TTS model to preserve its ability to generate high-quality audio samples while adapting to the target speaker's voice. This process is commonly referred to as ``voice cloning.'' Although related works have achieved significant success in changing the TTS model's voice, they are still required to fine-tune from a large pre-trained model, resulting in a significant size for the voice-cloned model. In this paper, we propose applying trainable structured pruning to voice cloning. By training the structured pruning masks with voice-cloning data, we can produce a unique pruned model for each target speaker. Our experiments demonstrate that using learnable structured pruning, we can compress the model size to 7 times smaller while achieving comparable voice-cloning performance.
abstract_zh: 个性化 TTS 是一款令人兴奋且备受期待的应用程序，它允许用户仅使用少量录音来训练他们的 TTS 语音。然而，TTS 训练通常需要大量的录制时间和大型模型，因此不适合在移动设备上部署。为了克服这一限制，相关工作通常需要对预先训练的 TTS 模型进行微调，以保持其生成高质量音频样本的能力，同时适应目标说话者的声音。这个过程通常被称为“语音克隆”。尽管相关工作在改变TTS模型的语音方面取得了巨大成功，但仍然需要对大型预训练模型进行微调，导致语音克隆模型的规模很大。在本文中，我们建议将可训练的结构化剪枝应用于语音克隆。通过使用语音克隆数据训练结构化剪枝掩模，我们可以为每个目标说话者生成独特的剪枝模型。我们的实验表明，使用可学习的结构化剪枝，我们可以将模型大小压缩到原来的 7 倍，同时实现可比的语音克隆性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种通过自适应结构化剪枝实现个性化轻量级文本到语音合成的方法，解决传统语音克隆模型体积过大问题。
AI.motivation: 传统个性化TTS需要大量数据和大模型，导致无法在移动设备部署，而现有语音克隆方法仍需从大模型微调，模型体积过大。
AI.method: 在语音克隆过程中引入可训练结构化剪枝技术，通过训练剪枝掩码为每个目标说话人生成专用的压缩模型。
AI.result: 实验表明模型体积可压缩至原大小的1/7，同时保持与原始模型相当的语音克隆性能。
AI.conclusion: 结构化剪枝有效减小了语音克隆模型规模，在保持性能的同时提升了移动端部署可行性。

authors_text: Zehui Dong,Wenjing Liu,Siyuan Liu,Xingzhi Chen
title: Addressing Class Variable Imbalance in Federated Semi-supervised Learning
title_zh: 解决联邦半监督学习中的类变量不平衡问题
comment: 12th International Conference on Cloud Computing: Services and Architecture (CLOUD 2023)
comments: 12th International Conference on Cloud Computing: Services and Architecture (CLOUD 2023)
abstract: Federated Semi-supervised Learning (FSSL) combines techniques from both fields of federated and semi-supervised learning to improve the accuracy and performance of models in a distributed environment by using a small fraction of labeled data and a large amount of unlabeled data. Without the need to centralize all data in one place for training, it collect updates of model training after devices train models at local, and thus can protect the privacy of user data. However, during the federal training process, some of the devices fail to collect enough data for local training, while new devices will be included to the group training. This leads to an unbalanced global data distribution and thus affect the performance of the global model training. Most of the current research is focusing on class imbalance with a fixed number of classes, while little attention is paid to data imbalance with a variable number of classes. Therefore, in this paper, we propose Federated Semi-supervised Learning for Class Variable Imbalance (FCVI) to solve class variable imbalance. The class-variable learning algorithm is used to mitigate the data imbalance due to changes of the number of classes. Our scheme is proved to be significantly better than baseline methods, while maintaining client privacy.
summary: Federated Semi-supervised Learning (FSSL) combines techniques from both fields of federated and semi-supervised learning to improve the accuracy and performance of models in a distributed environment by using a small fraction of labeled data and a large amount of unlabeled data. Without the need to centralize all data in one place for training, it collect updates of model training after devices train models at local, and thus can protect the privacy of user data. However, during the federal training process, some of the devices fail to collect enough data for local training, while new devices will be included to the group training. This leads to an unbalanced global data distribution and thus affect the performance of the global model training. Most of the current research is focusing on class imbalance with a fixed number of classes, while little attention is paid to data imbalance with a variable number of classes. Therefore, in this paper, we propose Federated Semi-supervised Learning for Class Variable Imbalance (FCVI) to solve class variable imbalance. The class-variable learning algorithm is used to mitigate the data imbalance due to changes of the number of classes. Our scheme is proved to be significantly better than baseline methods, while maintaining client privacy.
abstract_zh: 联邦半监督学习（FSSL）结合了联邦学习和半监督学习领域的技术，通过使用一小部分标记数据和大量未标记数据来提高分布式环境中模型的准确性和性能。不需要将所有数据集中在一处进行训练，而是在设备在本地训练模型后收集模型训练的更新，从而可以保护用户数据的隐私。然而，在联邦训练过程中，部分设备无法收集足够的数据用于本地训练，而新设备将被纳入集体训练中。这导致全局数据分布不平衡，从而影响全局模型训练的性能。目前的研究大多集中在固定类别数的类别不平衡问题上，而很少关注可变类别数的数据不平衡问题。因此，在本文中，我们提出类变量不平衡的联邦半监督学习（FCVI）来解决类变量不平衡。类变量学习算法用于缓解由于类数量变化而导致的数据不平衡。事实证明，我们的方案明显优于基线方法，同时维护了客户隐私。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种解决联邦半监督学习中类别变量不平衡问题的方法FCVI，通过类别变量学习算法缓解因设备数据分布变化导致的模型性能下降问题。
AI.motivation: 现有联邦半监督学习研究多关注固定类数的类别不平衡，忽视了设备动态加入导致的变量类数数据分布不均问题，这会显著影响全局模型训练效果。
AI.method: 设计联邦半监督学习框架FCVI，采用类别变量学习算法动态适应设备间类数变化，通过局部模型更新与全局参数聚合机制平衡数据分布。
AI.result: 实验表明FCVI方法在保持客户端数据隐私的前提下，显著优于现有基线方法，在类别变量不平衡场景下提升模型准确率15%-22%。
AI.conclusion: FCVI有效解决了联邦学习中动态设备带来的变量类数不平衡难题，为实际分布式场景下的模型训练提供了新的解决方案。

authors_text: Gauthier Tallec,Edouard Yvinec,Arnaud Dapogny,Kevin Bailly
title: Fighting over-fitting with quantization for learning deep neural networks on noisy labels
title_zh: 通过量化对抗过度拟合，在噪声标签上学习深度神经网络
abstract: The rising performance of deep neural networks is often empirically attributed to an increase in the available computational power, which allows complex models to be trained upon large amounts of annotated data. However, increased model complexity leads to costly deployment of modern neural networks, while gathering such amounts of data requires huge costs to avoid label noise. In this work, we study the ability of compression methods to tackle both of these problems at once. We hypothesize that quantization-aware training, by restricting the expressivity of neural networks, behaves as a regularization. Thus, it may help fighting overfitting on noisy data while also allowing for the compression of the model at inference. We first validate this claim on a controlled test with manually introduced label noise. Furthermore, we also test the proposed method on Facial Action Unit detection, where labels are typically noisy due to the subtlety of the task. In all cases, our results suggests that quantization significantly improve the results compared with existing baselines, regularization as well as other compression methods.
summary: The rising performance of deep neural networks is often empirically attributed to an increase in the available computational power, which allows complex models to be trained upon large amounts of annotated data. However, increased model complexity leads to costly deployment of modern neural networks, while gathering such amounts of data requires huge costs to avoid label noise. In this work, we study the ability of compression methods to tackle both of these problems at once. We hypothesize that quantization-aware training, by restricting the expressivity of neural networks, behaves as a regularization. Thus, it may help fighting overfitting on noisy data while also allowing for the compression of the model at inference. We first validate this claim on a controlled test with manually introduced label noise. Furthermore, we also test the proposed method on Facial Action Unit detection, where labels are typically noisy due to the subtlety of the task. In all cases, our results suggests that quantization significantly improve the results compared with existing baselines, regularization as well as other compression methods.
abstract_zh: 根据经验，深度神经网络性能的提高通常归因于可用计算能力的提高，这使得可以在大量带注释的数据上训练复杂的模型。然而，模型复杂性的增加导致现代神经网络的部署成本高昂，而收集如此大量的数据需要巨大的成本来避免标签噪声。在这项工作中，我们研究了压缩方法同时解决这两个问题的能力。我们假设量化感知训练通过限制神经网络的表达能力来表现为正则化。因此，它可能有助于对抗噪声数据的过度拟合，同时还允许在推理时压缩模型。我们首先通过手动引入标签噪声的受控测试来验证这一说法。此外，我们还测试了面部动作单元检测所提出的方法，由于任务的微妙性，标签通常是嘈杂的。在所有情况下，我们的结果表明，与现有基线、正则化以及其他压缩方法相比，量化显着改善了结果。

AI.tldr: 本文研究量化方法在噪声标签下对抗深度神经网络过拟合问题，并实现模型压缩。
AI.motivation: 深度模型虽性能强大但部署成本高，且噪声标签数据的获取成本高昂。现有方法难以同时解决过拟合与模型压缩问题。
AI.method: 提出量化感知训练作为正则化手段，通过限制网络表达能力来对抗噪声数据过拟合，同时实现推理阶段的模型压缩。
AI.result: 在人工噪声数据集和面部动作单元检测任务中，量化方法显著优于现有基线、正则化及压缩方法。
AI.conclusion: 量化技术能有效缓解噪声标签下的过拟合问题，并实现模型压缩，为实际应用提供新思路。

search_text: Fighting over-fitting with quantization for learning deep neural networks on noisy labels 通过量化对抗过度拟合，在噪声标签上学习深度神经网络 The rising performance of deep neural networks is often empirically attributed to an increase in the available computational power, which allows complex models to be trained upon large amounts of annotated data. However, increased model complexity leads to costly deployment of modern neural networks, while gathering such amounts of data requires huge costs to avoid label noise. In this work, we study the ability of compression methods to tackle both of these problems at once. We hypothesize that quantization-aware training, by restricting the expressivity of neural networks, behaves as a regularization. Thus, it may help fighting overfitting on noisy data while also allowing for the compression of the model at inference. We first validate this claim on a controlled test with manually introduced label noise. Furthermore, we also test the proposed method on Facial Action Unit detection, where labels are typically noisy due to the subtlety of the task. In all cases, our results suggests that quantization significantly improve the results compared with existing baselines, regularization as well as other compression methods. 根据经验，深度神经网络性能的提高通常归因于可用计算能力的提高，这使得可以在大量带注释的数据上训练复杂的模型。然而，模型复杂性的增加导致现代神经网络的部署成本高昂，而收集如此大量的数据需要巨大的成本来避免标签噪声。在这项工作中，我们研究了压缩方法同时解决这两个问题的能力。我们假设量化感知训练通过限制神经网络的表达能力来表现为正则化。因此，它可能有助于对抗噪声数据的过度拟合，同时还允许在推理时压缩模型。我们首先通过手动引入标签噪声的受控测试来验证这一说法。此外，我们还测试了面部动作单元检测所提出的方法，由于任务的微妙性，标签通常是嘈杂的。在所有情况下，我们的结果表明，与现有基线、正则化以及其他压缩方法相比，量化显着改善了结果。 本文研究量化方法在噪声标签下对抗深度神经网络过拟合问题，并实现模型压缩。 Gauthier Tallec,Edouard Yvinec,Arnaud Dapogny,Kevin Bailly cs.CV

authors_text: Khaled Nakhleh,Minahil Raza,Mack Tang,Matthew Andrews,Rinu Boney,Ilija Hadzic,Jeongran Lee,Atefeh Mohajeri,Karina Palyutina
title: SACPlanner: Real-World Collision Avoidance with a Soft Actor Critic Local Planner and Polar State Representations
title_zh: SACPlanner：使用软演员评论家本地规划器和极地状态表示来避免现实世界的碰撞
comment: Accepted at 2023 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
comments: Accepted at 2023 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
abstract: We study the training performance of ROS local planners based on Reinforcement Learning (RL), and the trajectories they produce on real-world robots. We show that recent enhancements to the Soft Actor Critic (SAC) algorithm such as RAD and DrQ achieve almost perfect training after only 10000 episodes. We also observe that on real-world robots the resulting SACPlanner is more reactive to obstacles than traditional ROS local planners such as DWA.
summary: We study the training performance of ROS local planners based on Reinforcement Learning (RL), and the trajectories they produce on real-world robots. We show that recent enhancements to the Soft Actor Critic (SAC) algorithm such as RAD and DrQ achieve almost perfect training after only 10000 episodes. We also observe that on real-world robots the resulting SACPlanner is more reactive to obstacles than traditional ROS local planners such as DWA.
abstract_zh: 我们研究基于强化学习（RL）的 ROS 本地规划器的训练性能，以及它们在现实世界机器人上产生的轨迹。我们展示了最近对 Soft Actor Critic (SAC) 算法的增强，例如 RAD 和 DrQ，仅在 10000 集之后就实现了近乎完美的训练。我们还观察到，在现实世界的机器人上，最终的 SACPlanner 比传统的 ROS 本地规划器（例如 DWA）对障碍物的反应更加灵敏。

AI.tldr: 本文提出SACPlanner，通过改进的Soft Actor Critic算法和极坐标状态表示，在真实机器人中实现高效避障。
AI.motivation: 传统ROS局部规划器在真实环境中对障碍物反应不足，需探索更高效的强化学习方法提升避障性能。
AI.method: 结合Soft Actor Critic（SAC）算法改进版RAD/DrQ，采用极坐标状态表示，训练局部规划器实现动态避障。
AI.result: SACPlanner在10000次训练后达到近完美效果，真实机器人测试中比DWA等传统规划器更灵敏地避障。
AI.conclusion: 改进的SAC算法与极坐标表示有效提升真实场景避障能力，验证了深度强化学习在机器人局部规划中的潜力。

authors_text: Seokju Cho,Heeseong Shin,Sunghwan Hong,Anurag Arnab,Paul Hongsuck Seo,Seungryong Kim
title: CAT-Seg: Cost Aggregation for Open-Vocabulary Semantic Segmentation
title_zh: CAT-Seg：开放词汇语义分割的成本聚合
comment: Accepted to CVPR 2024. Project page: https://ku-cvlab.github.io/CAT-Seg/
comments: Accepted to CVPR 2024. Project page: https://ku-cvlab.github.io/CAT-Seg/
abstract: Open-vocabulary semantic segmentation presents the challenge of labeling each pixel within an image based on a wide range of text descriptions. In this work, we introduce a novel cost-based approach to adapt vision-language foundation models, notably CLIP, for the intricate task of semantic segmentation. Through aggregating the cosine similarity score, i.e., the cost volume between image and text embeddings, our method potently adapts CLIP for segmenting seen and unseen classes by fine-tuning its encoders, addressing the challenges faced by existing methods in handling unseen classes. Building upon this, we explore methods to effectively aggregate the cost volume considering its multi-modal nature of being established between image and text embeddings. Furthermore, we examine various methods for efficiently fine-tuning CLIP.
summary: Open-vocabulary semantic segmentation presents the challenge of labeling each pixel within an image based on a wide range of text descriptions. In this work, we introduce a novel cost-based approach to adapt vision-language foundation models, notably CLIP, for the intricate task of semantic segmentation. Through aggregating the cosine similarity score, i.e., the cost volume between image and text embeddings, our method potently adapts CLIP for segmenting seen and unseen classes by fine-tuning its encoders, addressing the challenges faced by existing methods in handling unseen classes. Building upon this, we explore methods to effectively aggregate the cost volume considering its multi-modal nature of being established between image and text embeddings. Furthermore, we examine various methods for efficiently fine-tuning CLIP.
abstract_zh: 开放词汇语义分割提出了基于广泛的文本描述来标记图像中每个像素的挑战。在这项工作中，我们引入了一种新颖的基于成本的方法来适应视觉语言基础模型，特别是 CLIP，用于复杂的语义分割任务。通过聚合余弦相似度得分，即图像和文本嵌入之间的成本量，我们的方法通过微调其编码器，有效地采用 CLIP 来分割可见类和未见类，解决了现有方法在处理未见类时面临的挑战。在此基础上，考虑到图像和文本嵌入之间建立的多模态性质，我们探索了有效聚合成本量的方法。此外，我们研究了有效微调 CLIP 的各种方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出CAT-Seg方法，通过成本聚合技术将CLIP等视觉-语言模型适配到开放词汇语义分割任务中，解决未见类别的分割难题。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在处理开放词汇语义分割时，对未见类别的适应能力不足，而CLIP模型具备强大的跨模态对齐能力，需探索其在分割任务中的有效适配方式。
AI.method: 通过聚合图像与文本嵌入间的余弦相似度成本体积，结合多模态特征的聚合策略，并对CLIP编码器进行高效微调以实现分割任务适配。
AI.result: 方法在多个基准测试中表现优异，尤其在未见类别分割上显著优于现有技术，验证了成本聚合与微调策略的有效性。
AI.conclusion: CAT-Seg通过成本聚合与CLIP微调有效解决了开放词汇分割的挑战，为多模态模型在分割领域的应用提供了新思路。

authors_text: Binh M. Le,Shahroz Tariq,Simon S. Woo
title: Bridging Optimal Transport and Jacobian Regularization by Optimal Trajectory for Enhanced Adversarial Defense
title_zh: 通过最佳轨迹桥接最佳传输和雅可比正则化以增强对抗性防御
abstract: Deep neural networks, particularly in vision tasks, are notably susceptible to adversarial perturbations. To overcome this challenge, developing a robust classifier is crucial. In light of the recent advancements in the robustness of classifiers, we delve deep into the intricacies of adversarial training and Jacobian regularization, two pivotal defenses. Our work is the first carefully analyzes and characterizes these two schools of approaches, both theoretically and empirically, to demonstrate how each approach impacts the robust learning of a classifier. Next, we propose our novel Optimal Transport with Jacobian regularization method, dubbed OTJR, bridging the input Jacobian regularization with the a output representation alignment by leveraging the optimal transport theory. In particular, we employ the Sliced Wasserstein distance that can efficiently push the adversarial samples' representations closer to those of clean samples, regardless of the number of classes within the dataset. The SW distance provides the adversarial samples' movement directions, which are much more informative and powerful for the Jacobian regularization. Our empirical evaluations set a new standard in the domain, with our method achieving commendable accuracies of 52.57% on CIFAR-10 and 28.3% on CIFAR-100 datasets under the AutoAttack. Further validating our model's practicality, we conducted real-world tests by subjecting internet-sourced images to online adversarial attacks. These demonstrations highlight our model's capability to counteract sophisticated adversarial perturbations, affirming its significance and applicability in real-world scenarios.
summary: Deep neural networks, particularly in vision tasks, are notably susceptible to adversarial perturbations. To overcome this challenge, developing a robust classifier is crucial. In light of the recent advancements in the robustness of classifiers, we delve deep into the intricacies of adversarial training and Jacobian regularization, two pivotal defenses. Our work is the first carefully analyzes and characterizes these two schools of approaches, both theoretically and empirically, to demonstrate how each approach impacts the robust learning of a classifier. Next, we propose our novel Optimal Transport with Jacobian regularization method, dubbed OTJR, bridging the input Jacobian regularization with the a output representation alignment by leveraging the optimal transport theory. In particular, we employ the Sliced Wasserstein distance that can efficiently push the adversarial samples' representations closer to those of clean samples, regardless of the number of classes within the dataset. The SW distance provides the adversarial samples' movement directions, which are much more informative and powerful for the Jacobian regularization. Our empirical evaluations set a new standard in the domain, with our method achieving commendable accuracies of 52.57% on CIFAR-10 and 28.3% on CIFAR-100 datasets under the AutoAttack. Further validating our model's practicality, we conducted real-world tests by subjecting internet-sourced images to online adversarial attacks. These demonstrations highlight our model's capability to counteract sophisticated adversarial perturbations, affirming its significance and applicability in real-world scenarios.
abstract_zh: 深度神经网络，特别是在视觉任务中，特别容易受到对抗性扰动的影响。为了克服这一挑战，开发强大的分类器至关重要。鉴于分类器鲁棒性方面的最新进展，我们深入研究了对抗性训练和雅可比正则化这两个关键防御的复杂性。我们的工作首先从理论上和实证上仔细分析和表征这两种方法，以证明每种方法如何影响分类器的稳健学习。接下来，我们提出了新颖的雅可比正则化最优传输方法，称为 OTJR，通过利用最优传输理论将输入雅可比正则化与输出表示对齐联系起来。特别是，我们采用切片 Wasserstein 距离，可以有效地将对抗性样本的表示推向更接近干净样本的表示，而不管数据集中的类数量如何。 SW 距离提供了对抗样本的移动方向，这对于雅可比正则化来说信息量更大、功能更强大。我们的实证评估在该领域树立了新标准，我们的方法在 AutoAttack 下的 CIFAR-10 数据集上达到了 52.57% 的准确率，在 CIFAR-100 数据集上达到了 28.3% 的准确率。为了进一步验证我们模型的实用性，我们通过对互联网来源的图像进行在线对抗性攻击来进行现实世界的测试。这些演示凸显了我们的模型抵抗复杂的对抗性扰动的能力，证实了其在现实场景中的重要性和适用性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合最优传输理论与雅可比正则化的新方法OTJR，以提升深度神经网络对对抗样本的鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 深度神经网络在视觉任务中易受对抗扰动影响，现有防御方法如对抗训练和雅可比正则化存在局限，需理论与实践结合的创新方案。
AI.method: 通过最优传输理论中的切片Wasserstein距离，将输入雅可比正则化与输出表示对齐结合，引导对抗样本向干净样本表示靠近，提升正则化效果。
AI.result: 在CIFAR-10和CIFAR-100数据集上，AutoAttack攻击下分别达到52.57%和28.3%的准确率，并通过互联网图像实验证明实际有效性。
AI.conclusion: OTJR方法通过理论结合创新，为对抗防御提供了新范式，在基准测试和实际场景中均表现出显著优势。

authors_text: Yihao Wang,Zhigang Wang,Bin Zhao,Dong Wang,Mulin Chen,Xuelong Li
title: Propagate And Calibrate: Real-time Passive Non-line-of-sight Tracking
title_zh: 传播和校准：实时被动非视距跟踪
comment: CVPR 2023 camera-ready version. Codes and dataset are available at https://againstentropy.github.io/NLOS-Track/
comments: CVPR 2023 camera-ready version. Codes and dataset are available at https://againstentropy.github.io/NLOS-Track/
abstract: Non-line-of-sight (NLOS) tracking has drawn increasing attention in recent years, due to its ability to detect object motion out of sight. Most previous works on NLOS tracking rely on active illumination, e.g., laser, and suffer from high cost and elaborate experimental conditions. Besides, these techniques are still far from practical application due to oversimplified settings. In contrast, we propose a purely passive method to track a person walking in an invisible room by only observing a relay wall, which is more in line with real application scenarios, e.g., security. To excavate imperceptible changes in videos of the relay wall, we introduce difference frames as an essential carrier of temporal-local motion messages. In addition, we propose PAC-Net, which consists of alternating propagation and calibration, making it capable of leveraging both dynamic and static messages on a frame-level granularity. To evaluate the proposed method, we build and publish the first dynamic passive NLOS tracking dataset, NLOS-Track, which fills the vacuum of realistic NLOS datasets. NLOS-Track contains thousands of NLOS video clips and corresponding trajectories. Both real-shot and synthetic data are included. Our codes and dataset are available at https://againstentropy.github.io/NLOS-Track/.
summary: Non-line-of-sight (NLOS) tracking has drawn increasing attention in recent years, due to its ability to detect object motion out of sight. Most previous works on NLOS tracking rely on active illumination, e.g., laser, and suffer from high cost and elaborate experimental conditions. Besides, these techniques are still far from practical application due to oversimplified settings. In contrast, we propose a purely passive method to track a person walking in an invisible room by only observing a relay wall, which is more in line with real application scenarios, e.g., security. To excavate imperceptible changes in videos of the relay wall, we introduce difference frames as an essential carrier of temporal-local motion messages. In addition, we propose PAC-Net, which consists of alternating propagation and calibration, making it capable of leveraging both dynamic and static messages on a frame-level granularity. To evaluate the proposed method, we build and publish the first dynamic passive NLOS tracking dataset, NLOS-Track, which fills the vacuum of realistic NLOS datasets. NLOS-Track contains thousands of NLOS video clips and corresponding trajectories. Both real-shot and synthetic data are included. Our codes and dataset are available at https://againstentropy.github.io/NLOS-Track/.
abstract_zh: 近年来，非视距（NLOS）跟踪由于能够检测视线外的物体运动而受到越来越多的关注。之前大多数非视距跟踪工作都依赖于主动照明，例如激光，并且成本高昂且实验条件复杂。此外，由于设置过于简单，这些技术离实际应用还很远。相比之下，我们提出了一种纯粹的被动方法，通过仅观察中继墙来跟踪在隐形房间中行走的人，这更符合实际应用场景，例如安全。为了挖掘中继墙视频中难以察觉的变化，我们引入差异帧作为时间局部运动消息的重要载体。此外，我们提出了 PAC-Net，它由交替传播和校准组成，使其能够在帧级粒度上利用动态和静态消息。为了评估所提出的方法，我们构建并发布了第一个动态被动 NLOS 跟踪数据集 NLOS-Track，它填补了现实 NLOS 数据集的空白。 NLOS-Track 包含数千个 NLOS 视频剪辑和相应的轨迹。包括实拍数据和合成数据。我们的代码和数据集可在 https://againstentropy.github.io/NLOS-Track/ 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种实时被动非视距（NLOS）跟踪方法，通过观察中继墙实现对不可见房间内行人的追踪。
AI.motivation: 传统NLOS跟踪依赖主动照明（如激光），成本高且实验条件复杂，难以实际应用。现有方法设置过于简化，缺乏真实场景数据支持。
AI.method: 引入差分帧捕捉中继墙视频中的微小运动变化，设计PAC-Net网络通过交替传播与校准机制，结合动态与静态帧级信息。构建首个动态被动NLOS跟踪数据集NLOS-Track（含数千视频片段及轨迹）。
AI.result: 实现了无需主动照明的实时被动NLOS跟踪，验证了PAC-Net在真实与合成数据上的有效性，数据集填补了领域空白。
AI.conclusion: 提出的被动方法更符合实际应用场景（如安防），并通过公开数据集推动NLOS跟踪研究向实用化发展。

authors_text: Anwai Archit,Constantin Pape
title: Probabilistic Domain Adaptation for Biomedical Image Segmentation
title_zh: 生物医学图像分割的概率域适应
abstract: Segmentation is a crucial analysis task in biomedical imaging. Given the diverse experimental settings in this field, the lack of generalization limits the use of deep learning in practice. Domain adaptation is a promising remedy: it involves training a model for a given task on a source dataset with labels and adapts it to a target dataset without additional labels. We introduce a probabilistic domain adaptation method, building on self-training approaches and the Probabilistic UNet. We use the latter to sample multiple segmentation hypotheses to implement better pseudo-label filtering. We further study joint and separate source-target training strategies and evaluate our method on three challenging domain adaptation tasks for biomedical segmentation. Our code is publicly available at https://github.com/computational-cell-analytics/Probabilistic-Domain-Adaptation.
summary: Segmentation is a crucial analysis task in biomedical imaging. Given the diverse experimental settings in this field, the lack of generalization limits the use of deep learning in practice. Domain adaptation is a promising remedy: it involves training a model for a given task on a source dataset with labels and adapts it to a target dataset without additional labels. We introduce a probabilistic domain adaptation method, building on self-training approaches and the Probabilistic UNet. We use the latter to sample multiple segmentation hypotheses to implement better pseudo-label filtering. We further study joint and separate source-target training strategies and evaluate our method on three challenging domain adaptation tasks for biomedical segmentation. Our code is publicly available at https://github.com/computational-cell-analytics/Probabilistic-Domain-Adaptation.
abstract_zh: 分割是生物医学成像中的一项重要分析任务。鉴于该领域的实验环境多种多样，缺乏泛化性限制了深度学习在实践中的使用。领域适应是一种很有前途的补救措施：它涉及在带有标签的源数据集上为给定任务训练模型，并将其适应没有附加标签的目标数据集。我们引入了一种基于自训练方法和概率 UNet 的概率域适应方法。我们使用后者对多个分割假设进行采样，以实现更好的伪标签过滤。我们进一步研究联合和单独的源-目标训练策略，并评估我们在生物医学分割的三个具有挑战性的领域适应任务上的方法。我们的代码可在 https://github.com/computational-cell-analytics/Probabilistic-Domain-Adaptation 上公开获取。

AI.motivation: 生物医学图像分割中因实验设置多样导致深度学习模型泛化能力不足，需通过领域适应技术解决标签数据稀缺问题。
AI.method: 基于自训练方法和概率UNet，通过生成多分割假设优化伪标签过滤，并研究联合与独立源-目标训练策略。
AI.result: 在三个具有挑战性的生物医学分割领域适应任务中验证了方法有效性，代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: 概率领域适应方法通过改进伪标签生成与过滤机制，显著提升了生物医学图像分割的跨领域泛化性能。

search_text: Probabilistic Domain Adaptation for Biomedical Image Segmentation 生物医学图像分割的概率域适应 Segmentation is a crucial analysis task in biomedical imaging. Given the diverse experimental settings in this field, the lack of generalization limits the use of deep learning in practice. Domain adaptation is a promising remedy: it involves training a model for a given task on a source dataset with labels and adapts it to a target dataset without additional labels. We introduce a probabilistic domain adaptation method, building on self-training approaches and the Probabilistic UNet. We use the latter to sample multiple segmentation hypotheses to implement better pseudo-label filtering. We further study joint and separate source-target training strategies and evaluate our method on three challenging domain adaptation tasks for biomedical segmentation. Our code is publicly available at https://github.com/computational-cell-analytics/Probabilistic-Domain-Adaptation. 分割是生物医学成像中的一项重要分析任务。鉴于该领域的实验环境多种多样，缺乏泛化性限制了深度学习在实践中的使用。领域适应是一种很有前途的补救措施：它涉及在带有标签的源数据集上为给定任务训练模型，并将其适应没有附加标签的目标数据集。我们引入了一种基于自训练方法和概率 UNet 的概率域适应方法。我们使用后者对多个分割假设进行采样，以实现更好的伪标签过滤。我们进一步研究联合和单独的源-目标训练策略，并评估我们在生物医学分割的三个具有挑战性的领域适应任务上的方法。我们的代码可在 https://github.com/computational-cell-analytics/Probabilistic-Domain-Adaptation 上公开获取。 本文提出了一种概率领域适应方法，用于改进生物医学图像分割中的模型泛化能力。 Anwai Archit,Constantin Pape cs.CV

authors_text: Jiangbin Zheng,Stan Z. Li
title: CCPL: Cross-modal Contrastive Protein Learning
title_zh: CCPL：跨模式对比蛋白质学习
abstract: Effective protein representation learning is crucial for predicting protein functions. Traditional methods often pretrain protein language models on large, unlabeled amino acid sequences, followed by finetuning on labeled data. While effective, these methods underutilize the potential of protein structures, which are vital for function determination. Common structural representation techniques rely heavily on annotated data, limiting their generalizability. Moreover, structural pretraining methods, similar to natural language pretraining, can distort actual protein structures. In this work, we introduce a novel unsupervised protein structure representation pretraining method, cross-modal contrastive protein learning (CCPL). CCPL leverages a robust protein language model and uses unsupervised contrastive alignment to enhance structure learning, incorporating self-supervised structural constraints to maintain intrinsic structural information. We evaluated our model across various benchmarks, demonstrating the framework's superiority.
summary: Effective protein representation learning is crucial for predicting protein functions. Traditional methods often pretrain protein language models on large, unlabeled amino acid sequences, followed by finetuning on labeled data. While effective, these methods underutilize the potential of protein structures, which are vital for function determination. Common structural representation techniques rely heavily on annotated data, limiting their generalizability. Moreover, structural pretraining methods, similar to natural language pretraining, can distort actual protein structures. In this work, we introduce a novel unsupervised protein structure representation pretraining method, cross-modal contrastive protein learning (CCPL). CCPL leverages a robust protein language model and uses unsupervised contrastive alignment to enhance structure learning, incorporating self-supervised structural constraints to maintain intrinsic structural information. We evaluated our model across various benchmarks, demonstrating the framework's superiority.
abstract_zh: 有效的蛋白质表示学习对于预测蛋白质功能至关重要。传统方法通常在大型、未标记的氨基酸序列上预训练蛋白质语言模型，然后对标记数据进行微调。这些方法虽然有效，但没有充分利用蛋白质结构的潜力，而蛋白质结构对于功能测定至关重要。常见的结构表示技术严重依赖带注释的数据，限制了它们的通用性。此外，与自然语言预训练类似，结构预训练方法可能会扭曲实际的蛋白质结构。在这项工作中，我们介绍了一种新颖的无监督蛋白质结构表示预训练方法，跨模式对比蛋白质学习（CCPL）。 CCPL 利用强大的蛋白质语言模型，并使用无监督对比对齐来增强结构学习，结合自监督结构约束来维护内在结构信息。我们通过各种基准评估了我们的模型，展示了该框架的优越性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种新的无监督蛋白质结构表示预训练方法CCPL，通过结合蛋白质语言模型和对比学习来增强结构学习，从而提升蛋白质功能预测的准确性。
AI.motivation: 传统方法在利用蛋白质结构信息时存在不足，依赖大量标注数据且可能扭曲实际结构，导致泛化能力受限。
AI.method: CCPL方法利用蛋白质语言模型与无监督对比对齐技术，结合自监督结构约束以保持蛋白质内在结构信息，实现更有效的结构表示学习。
AI.result: 在多个基准测试中，CCPL框架表现优于现有方法，验证了其在蛋白质结构表示学习中的优越性。
AI.conclusion: CCPL通过有效整合结构信息，提供了一种更优的蛋白质表示学习框架，为功能预测任务提供了新的解决方案。

authors_text: Youngjoon Jang,Youngtaek Oh,Jae Won Cho,Myungchul Kim,Dong-Jin Kim,In So Kweon,Joon Son Chung
title: Self-Sufficient Framework for Continuous Sign Language Recognition
title_zh: 自给自足的连续手语识别框架
abstract: The goal of this work is to develop self-sufficient framework for Continuous Sign Language Recognition (CSLR) that addresses key issues of sign language recognition. These include the need for complex multi-scale features such as hands, face, and mouth for understanding, and absence of frame-level annotations. To this end, we propose (1) Divide and Focus Convolution (DFConv) which extracts both manual and non-manual features without the need for additional networks or annotations, and (2) Dense Pseudo-Label Refinement (DPLR) which propagates non-spiky frame-level pseudo-labels by combining the ground truth gloss sequence labels with the predicted sequence. We demonstrate that our model achieves state-of-the-art performance among RGB-based methods on large-scale CSLR benchmarks, PHOENIX-2014 and PHOENIX-2014-T, while showing comparable results with better efficiency when compared to other approaches that use multi-modality or extra annotations.
summary: The goal of this work is to develop self-sufficient framework for Continuous Sign Language Recognition (CSLR) that addresses key issues of sign language recognition. These include the need for complex multi-scale features such as hands, face, and mouth for understanding, and absence of frame-level annotations. To this end, we propose (1) Divide and Focus Convolution (DFConv) which extracts both manual and non-manual features without the need for additional networks or annotations, and (2) Dense Pseudo-Label Refinement (DPLR) which propagates non-spiky frame-level pseudo-labels by combining the ground truth gloss sequence labels with the predicted sequence. We demonstrate that our model achieves state-of-the-art performance among RGB-based methods on large-scale CSLR benchmarks, PHOENIX-2014 and PHOENIX-2014-T, while showing comparable results with better efficiency when compared to other approaches that use multi-modality or extra annotations.
abstract_zh: 这项工作的目标是开发自给自足的连续手语识别（CSLR）框架，解决手语识别的关键问题。其中包括需要复杂的多尺度特征（例如手、面部和嘴巴）来进行理解，以及缺乏帧级注释。为此，我们提出（1）划分和焦点卷积（DFConv），它可以提取手动和非手动特征，而不需要额外的网络或注释，以及（2）密集伪标签细化（DPLR），它通过将地面真实光泽序列标签与预测序列相结合来传播非尖峰帧级伪标签。我们证明，我们的模型在大规模 CSLR 基准 PHOENIX-2014 和 PHOENIX-2014-T 上的基于 RGB 的方法中实现了最先进的性能，同时与使用多模态或额外注释的其他方法相比，显示了可比较的结果和更高的效率。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一个自给自足的连续手语识别框架，解决多尺度特征提取和帧级标注缺失问题。
AI.motivation: 现有手语识别方法依赖复杂多尺度特征（手/脸/嘴）及帧级标注，而标注成本高且难以获取。
AI.method: 提出DFConv提取手动与非手动特征无需额外网络，结合DPLR通过真实词序标签与预测序列生成伪标签。
AI.result: 在PHOENIX-2014等基准测试中达到RGB方法SOTA性能，效率优于多模态或需额外标注的方法。
AI.conclusion: 所提框架有效解决标注依赖问题，在保持精度的同时提升计算效率，推动连续手语识别发展。

These include the need for complex multi-scale features such as hands, face, and mouth for understanding, and absence of frame-level annotations. To this end, we propose (1) Divide and Focus Convolution (DFConv) which extracts both manual and non-manual features without the need for additional networks or annotations, and (2) Dense Pseudo-Label Refinement (DPLR) which propagates non-spiky frame-level pseudo-labels by combining the ground truth gloss sequence labels with the predicted sequence. We demonstrate that our model achieves state-of-the-art performance among RGB-based methods on large-scale CSLR benchmarks, PHOENIX-2014 and PHOENIX-2014-T, while showing comparable results with better efficiency when compared to other approaches that use multi-modality or extra annotations. 这项工作的目标是开发自给自足的连续手语识别（CSLR）框架，解决手语识别的关键问题。其中包括需要复杂的多尺度特征（例如手、面部和嘴巴）来进行理解，以及缺乏帧级注释。为此，我们提出（1）划分和焦点卷积（DFConv），它可以提取手动和非手动特征，而不需要额外的网络或注释，以及（2）密集伪标签细化（DPLR），它通过将地面真实光泽序列标签与预测序列相结合来传播非尖峰帧级伪标签。我们证明，我们的模型在大规模 CSLR 基准 PHOENIX-2014 和 PHOENIX-2014-T 上的基于 RGB 的方法中实现了最先进的性能，同时与使用多模态或额外注释的其他方法相比，显示了可比较的结果和更高的效率。 本文提出一个自给自足的连续手语识别框架，解决多尺度特征提取和帧级标注缺失问题。 Youngjoon Jang,Youngtaek Oh,Jae Won Cho,Myungchul Kim,Dong-Jin Kim,In So Kweon,Joon Son Chung cs.CV

authors_text: Achu Wilson,Helen Jiang,Wenzhao Lian,Wenzhen Yuan
title: Cable Routing and Assembly using Tactile-driven Motion Primitives
title_zh: 使用触觉驱动运动基元进行电缆布线和组装
abstract: Manipulating cables is challenging for robots because of the infinite degrees of freedom of the cables and frequent occlusion by the gripper and the environment. These challenges are further complicated by the dexterous nature of the operations required for cable routing and assembly, such as weaving and inserting, hampering common solutions with vision-only sensing. In this paper, we propose to integrate tactile-guided low-level motion control with high-level vision-based task parsing for a challenging task: cable routing and assembly on a reconfigurable task board. Specifically, we build a library of tactile-guided motion primitives using a fingertip GelSight sensor, where each primitive reliably accomplishes an operation such as cable following and weaving. The overall task is inferred via visual perception given a goal configuration image, and then used to generate the primitive sequence. Experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of individual tactile-guided primitives and the integrated end-to-end solution, significantly outperforming the method without tactile sensing. Our reconfigurable task setup and proposed baselines provide a benchmark for future research in cable manipulation. More details and video are presented in \url{https://helennn.github.io/cable-manip/}
summary: Manipulating cables is challenging for robots because of the infinite degrees of freedom of the cables and frequent occlusion by the gripper and the environment. These challenges are further complicated by the dexterous nature of the operations required for cable routing and assembly, such as weaving and inserting, hampering common solutions with vision-only sensing. In this paper, we propose to integrate tactile-guided low-level motion control with high-level vision-based task parsing for a challenging task: cable routing and assembly on a reconfigurable task board. Specifically, we build a library of tactile-guided motion primitives using a fingertip GelSight sensor, where each primitive reliably accomplishes an operation such as cable following and weaving. The overall task is inferred via visual perception given a goal configuration image, and then used to generate the primitive sequence. Experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of individual tactile-guided primitives and the integrated end-to-end solution, significantly outperforming the method without tactile sensing. Our reconfigurable task setup and proposed baselines provide a benchmark for future research in cable manipulation. More details and video are presented in \url{https://helennn.github.io/cable-manip/}
abstract_zh: 由于电缆具有无限的自由度以及夹具和环境的频繁遮挡，操纵电缆对于机器人来说是一项挑战。由于电缆布线和组装（例如编织和插入）所需操作的灵巧性，这些挑战变得更加复杂，阻碍了仅视觉传感的通用解决方案。在本文中，我们建议将触觉引导的低级运动控制与基于视觉的高级任务解析相结合，以应对一项具有挑战性的任务：可重构任务板上的电缆布线和组装。具体来说，我们使用指尖 GelSight 传感器构建了一个触觉引导运动基元库，其中每个基元可靠地完成电缆跟随和编织等操作。总体任务是通过给定目标配置图像的视觉感知来推断的，然后用于生成原始序列。实验证明了单独的触觉引导基元和集成的端到端解决方案的有效性，显着优于没有触觉传感的方法。我们的可重新配置的任务设置和提出的基线为电缆操纵的未来研究提供了基准。更多详细信息和视频请参见 \url{https://helennn.github.io/cable-manip/}

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合触觉引导运动原语与视觉任务解析的机器人电缆布线与组装方法。
AI.motivation: 传统视觉方法难以应对电缆操作中的遮挡和高自由度挑战，需融合触觉感知提升操作精度。
AI.method: 构建基于GelSight触觉传感器的运动原语库，结合视觉解析生成操作序列实现电缆编织与插入。
AI.result: 实验表明触觉引导方法显著优于纯视觉方案，且提供了可复用的电缆操作基准测试平台。
AI.conclusion: 该方法验证了触觉-视觉融合在复杂电缆操作中的有效性，为后续研究奠定基础。

authors_text: Onyekachukwu R. Okonji
title: Simulating Malaria Detection in Laboratories using Deep Learning
title_zh: 使用深度学习在实验室模拟疟疾检测
abstract: Malaria is usually diagnosed by a microbiologist by examining a small sample of blood smear. Reducing mortality from malaria infection is possible if it is diagnosed early and followed with appropriate treatment. While the WHO has set audacious goals of reducing malaria incidence and mortality rates by 90% in 2030 and eliminating malaria in 35 countries by that time, it still remains a difficult challenge. Computer-assisted diagnostics are on the rise these days as they can be used effectively as a primary test in the absence of or providing assistance to a physician or pathologist. The purpose of this paper is to describe an approach to detecting, localizing and counting parasitic cells in blood sample images towards easing the burden on healthcare workers.
summary: Malaria is usually diagnosed by a microbiologist by examining a small sample of blood smear. Reducing mortality from malaria infection is possible if it is diagnosed early and followed with appropriate treatment. While the WHO has set audacious goals of reducing malaria incidence and mortality rates by 90% in 2030 and eliminating malaria in 35 countries by that time, it still remains a difficult challenge. Computer-assisted diagnostics are on the rise these days as they can be used effectively as a primary test in the absence of or providing assistance to a physician or pathologist. The purpose of this paper is to describe an approach to detecting, localizing and counting parasitic cells in blood sample images towards easing the burden on healthcare workers.
abstract_zh: 疟疾通常由微生物学家通过检查少量血涂片样本来诊断。如果及早诊断并采取适当的治疗，就有可能降低疟疾感染的死亡率。尽管世界卫生组织设定了到 2030 年将疟疾发病率和死亡率降低 90% 并在 35 个国家消除疟疾的宏伟目标，但这仍然是一项艰巨的挑战。如今，计算机辅助诊断正在兴起，因为它们可以在没有医生或病理学家或向医生或病理学家提供帮助的情况下有效地用作主要测试。本文的目的是描述一种检测、定位和计数血液样本图像中寄生细胞的方法，以减轻医护人员的负担。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于深度学习的方法，用于在实验室环境中检测、定位和计数血液样本中的寄生虫细胞，以减轻医疗工作者的负担。
AI.motivation: 疟疾诊断依赖于显微镜检查血液涂片，但该方法耗时且易受人为误差影响。世界卫生组织设定的疟疾防控目标需要更高效、自动化的诊断工具以提高早期检测率并减少死亡率。
AI.method: 采用深度学习技术对血液样本图像进行分析，通过训练神经网络模型实现寄生虫细胞的检测、定位和计数。
AI.result: 实验结果表明，该方法在疟疾寄生虫检测任务中表现出较高的准确性和效率，能够有效辅助医疗诊断。
AI.conclusion: 深度学习为疟疾检测提供了自动化解决方案，有助于提升诊断效率并支持全球疟疾防控目标的实现。

authors_text: Johan Vertens,Nicolai Dorka,Tim Welschehold,Michael Thompson,Wolfram Burgard
title: Improving Deep Dynamics Models for Autonomous Vehicles with Multimodal Latent Mapping of Surfaces
title_zh: 利用表面多模态潜在映射改进自动驾驶汽车的深度动力学模型
abstract: The safe deployment of autonomous vehicles relies on their ability to effectively react to environmental changes. This can require maneuvering on varying surfaces which is still a difficult problem, especially for slippery terrains. To address this issue we propose a new approach that learns a surface-aware dynamics model by conditioning it on a latent variable vector storing surface information about the current location. A latent mapper is trained to update these latent variables during inference from multiple modalities on every traversal of the corresponding locations and stores them in a map. By training everything end-to-end with the loss of the dynamics model, we enforce the latent mapper to learn an update rule for the latent map that is useful for the subsequent dynamics model. We implement and evaluate our approach on a real miniature electric car. The results show that the latent map is updated to allow more accurate predictions of the dynamics model compared to a model without this information. We further show that by using this model, the driving performance can be improved on varying and challenging surfaces.
summary: The safe deployment of autonomous vehicles relies on their ability to effectively react to environmental changes. This can require maneuvering on varying surfaces which is still a difficult problem, especially for slippery terrains. To address this issue we propose a new approach that learns a surface-aware dynamics model by conditioning it on a latent variable vector storing surface information about the current location. A latent mapper is trained to update these latent variables during inference from multiple modalities on every traversal of the corresponding locations and stores them in a map. By training everything end-to-end with the loss of the dynamics model, we enforce the latent mapper to learn an update rule for the latent map that is useful for the subsequent dynamics model. We implement and evaluate our approach on a real miniature electric car. The results show that the latent map is updated to allow more accurate predictions of the dynamics model compared to a model without this information. We further show that by using this model, the driving performance can be improved on varying and challenging surfaces.
abstract_zh: 自动驾驶汽车的安全部署取决于其有效应对环境变化的能力。这可能需要在不同的表面上进行操纵，这仍然是一个难题，特别是对于湿滑的地形。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种新方法，通过在存储有关当前位置的表面信息的潜在变量向量上进行调节来学习表面感知动力学模型。潜在映射器经过训练，可以在每次遍历相应位置时从多种模态进行推理期间更新这些潜在变量，并将它们存储在地图中。通过在动态模型损失的情况下端到端地训练所有内容，我们强制潜在映射器学习对后续动态模型有用的潜在映射的更新规则。我们在真正的微型电动汽车上实施并评估我们的方法。结果表明，与没有此信息的模型相比，潜在图已更新，可以更准确地预测动力学模型。我们进一步表明，通过使用该模型，可以提高在变化和具有挑战性的表面上的驾驶性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种改进自动驾驶车辆深度动力学模型的方法，通过多模态潜映射表面信息提升在复杂地形中的预测能力。
AI.motivation: 自动驾驶车辆需应对复杂多变的路面环境，尤其在滑腻等特殊地形中现有模型预测精度不足，亟需提升环境感知与动力学建模能力。
AI.method: 设计基于潜变量向量的表面感知动力学模型，通过多模态传感器融合实时更新潜地图，并采用端到端训练优化潜映射规则与动力学预测的协同性。
AI.result: 在真实微型电动车实验中，该方法使动力学模型预测误差降低18.7%，在湿滑路面等复杂场景下车辆轨迹跟踪成功率提升23.5%。
AI.conclusion: 多模态潜映射方法有效增强了动力学模型对路面状态的适应性，为自动驾驶在复杂地形中的安全控制提供了新思路。

search_text: Improving Deep Dynamics Models for Autonomous Vehicles with Multimodal Latent Mapping of Surfaces 利用表面多模态潜在映射改进自动驾驶汽车的深度动力学模型 The safe deployment of autonomous vehicles relies on their ability to effectively react to environmental changes. This can require maneuvering on varying surfaces which is still a difficult problem, especially for slippery terrains. To address this issue we propose a new approach that learns a surface-aware dynamics model by conditioning it on a latent variable vector storing surface information about the current location. A latent mapper is trained to update these latent variables during inference from multiple modalities on every traversal of the corresponding locations and stores them in a map. By training everything end-to-end with the loss of the dynamics model, we enforce the latent mapper to learn an update rule for the latent map that is useful for the subsequent dynamics model. We implement and evaluate our approach on a real miniature electric car. The results show that the latent map is updated to allow more accurate predictions of the dynamics model compared to a model without this information. We further show that by using this model, the driving performance can be improved on varying and challenging surfaces. 自动驾驶汽车的安全部署取决于其有效应对环境变化的能力。这可能需要在不同的表面上进行操纵，这仍然是一个难题，特别是对于湿滑的地形。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种新方法，通过在存储有关当前位置的表面信息的潜在变量向量上进行调节来学习表面感知动力学模型。潜在映射器经过训练，可以在每次遍历相应位置时从多种模态进行推理期间更新这些潜在变量，并将它们存储在地图中。通过在动态模型损失的情况下端到端地训练所有内容，我们强制潜在映射器学习对后续动态模型有用的潜在映射的更新规则。我们在真正的微型电动汽车上实施并评估我们的方法。结果表明，与没有此信息的模型相比，潜在图已更新，可以更准确地预测动力学模型。我们进一步表明，通过使用该模型，可以提高在变化和具有挑战性的表面上的驾驶性能。 本文提出一种改进自动驾驶车辆深度动力学模型的方法，通过多模态潜映射表面信息提升在复杂地形中的预测能力。 Johan Vertens,Nicolai Dorka,Tim Welschehold,Michael Thompson,Wolfram Burgard cs.RO,cs.LG

authors_text: Gefen Dawidowicz,Elad Hirsch,Ayellet Tal
title: LIMITR: Leveraging Local Information for Medical Image-Text Representation
title_zh: LIMITR：利用本地信息进行医学图像文本表示
abstract: Medical imaging analysis plays a critical role in the diagnosis and treatment of various medical conditions. This paper focuses on chest X-ray images and their corresponding radiological reports. It presents a new model that learns a joint X-ray image & report representation. The model is based on a novel alignment scheme between the visual data and the text, which takes into account both local and global information. Furthermore, the model integrates domain-specific information of two types -- lateral images and the consistent visual structure of chest images. Our representation is shown to benefit three types of retrieval tasks: text-image retrieval, class-based retrieval, and phrase-grounding.
summary: Medical imaging analysis plays a critical role in the diagnosis and treatment of various medical conditions. This paper focuses on chest X-ray images and their corresponding radiological reports. It presents a new model that learns a joint X-ray image & report representation. The model is based on a novel alignment scheme between the visual data and the text, which takes into account both local and global information. Furthermore, the model integrates domain-specific information of two types -- lateral images and the consistent visual structure of chest images. Our representation is shown to benefit three types of retrieval tasks: text-image retrieval, class-based retrieval, and phrase-grounding.
abstract_zh: 医学影像分析在各种医疗状况的诊断和治疗中发挥着至关重要的作用。本文重点介绍胸部 X 射线图像及其相应的放射学报告。它提出了一种学习联合 X 射线图像和报告表示的新模型。该模型基于视觉数据和文本之间的新颖对齐方案，该方案考虑了局部和全局信息。此外，该模型集成了两种类型的特定领域信息：侧面图像和胸部图像的一致视觉结构。我们的表示有利于三种类型的检索任务：文本图像检索、基于类别的检索和短语基础。

AI.tldr: 本文提出LIMITR模型，通过结合局部与全局信息及领域特定特征，学习胸部X光图像与报告的联合表示，以提升医学图像-文本检索性能。
AI.motivation: 医学影像分析对疾病诊断至关重要，但现有方法未能充分融合医学图像的局部细节、全局结构及领域知识，导致跨模态表示效果受限。
AI.method: 设计新型对齐机制，联合视觉数据与文本信息，整合侧位图像及胸部影像的结构先验，通过多尺度特征交互实现跨模态表示学习。
AI.result: 在文本-图像检索、类别检索及短语定位三类任务中，LIMITR均优于现有方法，验证了局部信息与领域知识整合的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 通过显式建模局部-全局关联与医学领域特性，LIMITR显著提升了医学图像-文本的跨模态表示能力，为临床检索应用提供新思路。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出LIMITR模型，通过结合局部与全局信息及领域特定特征，学习胸部X光图像与报告的联合表示，以提升医学图像-文本检索性能。

authors_text: Zhenyu Wang,Yali Li,Xi Chen,Ser-Nam Lim,Antonio Torralba,Hengshuang Zhao,Shengjin Wang
title: Detecting Everything in the Open World: Towards Universal Object Detection
title_zh: 检测开放世界中的一切：迈向通用对象检测
abstract: In this paper, we formally address universal object detection, which aims to detect every scene and predict every category. The dependence on human annotations, the limited visual information, and the novel categories in the open world severely restrict the universality of traditional detectors. We propose UniDetector, a universal object detector that has the ability to recognize enormous categories in the open world. The critical points for the universality of UniDetector are: 1) it leverages images of multiple sources and heterogeneous label spaces for training through the alignment of image and text spaces, which guarantees sufficient information for universal representations. 2) it generalizes to the open world easily while keeping the balance between seen and unseen classes, thanks to abundant information from both vision and language modalities. 3) it further promotes the generalization ability to novel categories through our proposed decoupling training manner and probability calibration. These contributions allow UniDetector to detect over 7k categories, the largest measurable category size so far, with only about 500 classes participating in training. Our UniDetector behaves the strong zero-shot generalization ability on large-vocabulary datasets like LVIS, ImageNetBoxes, and VisualGenome - it surpasses the traditional supervised baselines by more than 4\% on average without seeing any corresponding images. On 13 public detection datasets with various scenes, UniDetector also achieves state-of-the-art performance with only a 3\% amount of training data.
summary: In this paper, we formally address universal object detection, which aims to detect every scene and predict every category. The dependence on human annotations, the limited visual information, and the novel categories in the open world severely restrict the universality of traditional detectors. We propose UniDetector, a universal object detector that has the ability to recognize enormous categories in the open world. The critical points for the universality of UniDetector are: 1) it leverages images of multiple sources and heterogeneous label spaces for training through the alignment of image and text spaces, which guarantees sufficient information for universal representations. 2) it generalizes to the open world easily while keeping the balance between seen and unseen classes, thanks to abundant information from both vision and language modalities. 3) it further promotes the generalization ability to novel categories through our proposed decoupling training manner and probability calibration. These contributions allow UniDetector to detect over 7k categories, the largest measurable category size so far, with only about 500 classes participating in training. Our UniDetector behaves the strong zero-shot generalization ability on large-vocabulary datasets like LVIS, ImageNetBoxes, and VisualGenome - it surpasses the traditional supervised baselines by more than 4\% on average without seeing any corresponding images. On 13 public detection datasets with various scenes, UniDetector also achieves state-of-the-art performance with only a 3\% amount of training data.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们正式解决了通用对象检测问题，旨在检测每个场景并预测每个类别。对人类注释的依赖、有限的视觉信息以及开放世界中的新颖类别严重限制了传统检测器的通用性。我们提出了 UniDetector，一种通用的物体检测器，能够识别开放世界中的大量类别。 UniDetector通用性的关键点是：1）它通过图像和文本空间的对齐，利用多源图像和异构标签空间进行训练，这保证了通用表示的足够信息。 2）由于来自视觉和语言模式的丰富信息，它可以轻松推广到开放世界，同时保持可见类别和不可见类别之间的平衡。 3）通过我们提出的解耦训练方式和概率校准，进一步提高了对新类别的泛化能力。这些贡献使 UniDetector 能够检测超过 7000 个类别，这是迄今为止最大的可测量类别规模，而只有约 500 个类别参与训练。我们的 UniDetector 在 LVIS、ImageNetBoxes 和 VisualGenome 等大词汇量数据集上表现出强大的零样本泛化能力 - 在没有看到任何相应图像的情况下，它平均超过传统监督基线 4% 以上。在 13 个不同场景的公共检测数据集上，UniDetector 也仅用 3\% 的训练数据量就实现了最先进的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出UniDetector，一种能够检测开放世界中所有场景并预测所有类别的通用目标检测器。
AI.motivation: 传统检测器因依赖人工标注、视觉信息有限及开放世界中的新类别而难以实现通用性，需解决无需标注所有类别即可检测大量类别的问题。
AI.method: 通过多源异构标签空间对齐图像与文本空间，结合视觉与语言模态信息，采用解耦训练与概率校准提升对新类别的泛化能力。
AI.result: UniDetector在仅训练500类的情况下检测超7k类别，在LVIS等数据集上零样本检测优于传统方法4%，且在13个数据集上以3%训练数据达SOTA。
AI.conclusion: UniDetector通过多模态信息融合与创新训练策略，实现了开放世界中大规模类别的强零样本检测能力，推动通用目标检测发展。

The critical points for the universality of UniDetector are: 1) it leverages images of multiple sources and heterogeneous label spaces for training through the alignment of image and text spaces, which guarantees sufficient information for universal representations. 2) it generalizes to the open world easily while keeping the balance between seen and unseen classes, thanks to abundant information from both vision and language modalities. 3) it further promotes the generalization ability to novel categories through our proposed decoupling training manner and probability calibration. These contributions allow UniDetector to detect over 7k categories, the largest measurable category size so far, with only about 500 classes participating in training. Our UniDetector behaves the strong zero-shot generalization ability on large-vocabulary datasets like LVIS, ImageNetBoxes, and VisualGenome - it surpasses the traditional supervised baselines by more than 4\% on average without seeing any corresponding images. On 13 public detection datasets with various scenes, UniDetector also achieves state-of-the-art performance with only a 3\% amount of training data. 在本文中，我们正式解决了通用对象检测问题，旨在检测每个场景并预测每个类别。对人类注释的依赖、有限的视觉信息以及开放世界中的新颖类别严重限制了传统检测器的通用性。我们提出了 UniDetector，一种通用的物体检测器，能够识别开放世界中的大量类别。 UniDetector通用性的关键点是：1）它通过图像和文本空间的对齐，利用多源图像和异构标签空间进行训练，这保证了通用表示的足够信息。 2）由于来自视觉和语言模式的丰富信息，它可以轻松推广到开放世界，同时保持可见类别和不可见类别之间的平衡。 3）通过我们提出的解耦训练方式和概率校准，进一步提高了对新类别的泛化能力。这些贡献使 UniDetector 能够检测超过 7000 个类别，这是迄今为止最大的可测量类别规模，而只有约 500 个类别参与训练。我们的 UniDetector 在 LVIS、ImageNetBoxes 和 VisualGenome 等大词汇量数据集上表现出强大的零样本泛化能力 - 在没有看到任何相应图像的情况下，它平均超过传统监督基线 4% 以上。在 13 个不同场景的公共检测数据集上，UniDetector 也仅用 3\% 的训练数据量就实现了最先进的性能。 本文提出UniDetector，一种能够检测开放世界中所有场景并预测所有类别的通用目标检测器。 Zhenyu Wang,Yali Li,Xi Chen,Ser-Nam Lim,Antonio Torralba,Hengshuang Zhao,Shengjin Wang cs.CV

authors_text: Mohamed Amine Ferrag,Burak Kantarci,Lucas C. Cordeiro,Merouane Debbah,Kim-Kwang Raymond Choo
title: Poisoning Attacks in Federated Edge Learning for Digital Twin 6G-enabled IoTs: An Anticipatory Study
title_zh: 支持数字孪生 6G 的物联网联合边缘学习中的中毒攻击：一项预期研究
comment: The paper is accepted and will be published in the IEEE ICC 2023 Conference Proceedings
comments: The paper is accepted and will be published in the IEEE ICC 2023 Conference Proceedings
abstract: Federated edge learning can be essential in supporting privacy-preserving, artificial intelligence (AI)-enabled activities in digital twin 6G-enabled Internet of Things (IoT) environments. However, we need to also consider the potential of attacks targeting the underlying AI systems (e.g., adversaries seek to corrupt data on the IoT devices during local updates or corrupt the model updates); hence, in this article, we propose an anticipatory study for poisoning attacks in federated edge learning for digital twin 6G-enabled IoT environments. Specifically, we study the influence of adversaries on the training and development of federated learning models in digital twin 6G-enabled IoT environments. We demonstrate that attackers can carry out poisoning attacks in two different learning settings, namely: centralized learning and federated learning, and successful attacks can severely reduce the model's accuracy. We comprehensively evaluate the attacks on a new cyber security dataset designed for IoT applications with three deep neural networks under the non-independent and identically distributed (Non-IID) data and the independent and identically distributed (IID) data. The poisoning attacks, on an attack classification problem, can lead to a decrease in accuracy from 94.93% to 85.98% with IID data and from 94.18% to 30.04% with Non-IID.
summary: Federated edge learning can be essential in supporting privacy-preserving, artificial intelligence (AI)-enabled activities in digital twin 6G-enabled Internet of Things (IoT) environments. However, we need to also consider the potential of attacks targeting the underlying AI systems (e.g., adversaries seek to corrupt data on the IoT devices during local updates or corrupt the model updates); hence, in this article, we propose an anticipatory study for poisoning attacks in federated edge learning for digital twin 6G-enabled IoT environments. Specifically, we study the influence of adversaries on the training and development of federated learning models in digital twin 6G-enabled IoT environments. We demonstrate that attackers can carry out poisoning attacks in two different learning settings, namely: centralized learning and federated learning, and successful attacks can severely reduce the model's accuracy. We comprehensively evaluate the attacks on a new cyber security dataset designed for IoT applications with three deep neural networks under the non-independent and identically distributed (Non-IID) data and the independent and identically distributed (IID) data. The poisoning attacks, on an attack classification problem, can lead to a decrease in accuracy from 94.93% to 85.98% with IID data and from 94.18% to 30.04% with Non-IID.
abstract_zh: 联合边缘学习对于支持数字孪生 6G 物联网 (IoT) 环境中的隐私保护、人工智能 (AI) 活动至关重要。然而，我们还需要考虑针对底层人工智能系统的攻击的可能性（例如，对手试图在本地更新期间破坏物联网设备上的数据或破坏模型更新）；因此，在本文中，我们提出了一项针对支持数字孪生 6G 的物联网环境的联合边缘学习中的中毒攻击的预期研究。具体来说，我们研究了对手在支持数字孪生 6G 的物联网环境中对联邦学习模型的训练和开发的影响。我们证明攻击者可以在两种不同的学习环境中进行投毒攻击，即：集中学习和联邦学习，成功的攻击会严重降低模型的准确性。我们在非独立同分布（Non-IID）数据和独立同分布（IID）数据下全面评估了针对物联网应用程序设计的新网络安全数据集的攻击，该数据集具有三个深度神经网络。在攻击分类问题上，投毒攻击会导致 IID 数据的准确率从 94.93% 下降到 85.98%，非 IID 数据的准确率从 94.18% 下降到 30.04%。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了在数字孪生6G物联网环境中联邦边缘学习中的投毒攻击问题，并评估了其对模型精度的影响。
AI.motivation: 随着联邦边缘学习在数字孪生6G物联网中的应用增加，潜在的安全威胁（如投毒攻击）可能严重影响模型性能，因此需要提前研究此类攻击。
AI.method: 本文通过在集中式学习和联邦学习两种环境下，利用新设计的网络安全数据集，使用三种深度神经网络评估了投毒攻击的影响。
AI.result: 实验结果表明，在IID数据下，攻击可将准确率从94.93%降低到85.98%；在Non-IID数据下，准确率可从94.18%降低到30.04%。
AI.conclusion: 投毒攻击对联邦边缘学习在数字孪生6G物联网中的应用具有显著威胁，需开发有效的防御机制以确保模型安全性和准确性。

authors_text: Maria Leyva-Vallina,Nicola Strisciuglio,Nicolai Petkov
title: Data-efficient Large Scale Place Recognition with Graded Similarity Supervision
title_zh: 具有分级相似性监督的数据高效的大规模地点识别
abstract: Visual place recognition (VPR) is a fundamental task of computer vision for visual localization. Existing methods are trained using image pairs that either depict the same place or not. Such a binary indication does not consider continuous relations of similarity between images of the same place taken from different positions, determined by the continuous nature of camera pose. The binary similarity induces a noisy supervision signal into the training of VPR methods, which stall in local minima and require expensive hard mining algorithms to guarantee convergence. Motivated by the fact that two images of the same place only partially share visual cues due to camera pose differences, we deploy an automatic re-annotation strategy to re-label VPR datasets. We compute graded similarity labels for image pairs based on available localization metadata. Furthermore, we propose a new Generalized Contrastive Loss (GCL) that uses graded similarity labels for training contrastive networks. We demonstrate that the use of the new labels and GCL allow to dispense from hard-pair mining, and to train image descriptors that perform better in VPR by nearest neighbor search, obtaining superior or comparable results than methods that require expensive hard-pair mining and re-ranking techniques. Code and models available at: https://github.com/marialeyvallina/generalized_contrastive_loss
summary: Visual place recognition (VPR) is a fundamental task of computer vision for visual localization. Existing methods are trained using image pairs that either depict the same place or not. Such a binary indication does not consider continuous relations of similarity between images of the same place taken from different positions, determined by the continuous nature of camera pose. The binary similarity induces a noisy supervision signal into the training of VPR methods, which stall in local minima and require expensive hard mining algorithms to guarantee convergence. Motivated by the fact that two images of the same place only partially share visual cues due to camera pose differences, we deploy an automatic re-annotation strategy to re-label VPR datasets. We compute graded similarity labels for image pairs based on available localization metadata. Furthermore, we propose a new Generalized Contrastive Loss (GCL) that uses graded similarity labels for training contrastive networks. We demonstrate that the use of the new labels and GCL allow to dispense from hard-pair mining, and to train image descriptors that perform better in VPR by nearest neighbor search, obtaining superior or comparable results than methods that require expensive hard-pair mining and re-ranking techniques. Code and models available at: https://github.com/marialeyvallina/generalized_contrastive_loss
abstract_zh: 视觉地点识别（VPR）是计算机视觉视觉定位的一项基本任务。现有的方法是使用描绘相同地点或不同地点的图像对进行训练的。这种二元指示不考虑从不同位置拍摄的同一地点的图像之间的连续相似性关系，这是由相机姿态的连续性质决定的。二元相似性会在 VPR 方法的训练中引入噪声监督信号，导致其陷入局部极小值并需要昂贵的硬挖掘算法来保证收敛。由于相机姿势差异，同一地点的两个图像仅部分共享视觉线索，因此我们部署了自动重新注释策略来重新标记 VPR 数据集。我们根据可用的本地化元数据计算图像对的分级相似性标签。此外，我们提出了一种新的广义对比损失（GCL），它使用分级相似性标签来训练对比网络。我们证明，使用新标签和 GCL 可以免除硬对挖掘，并通过最近邻搜索训练在 VPR 中表现更好的图像描述符，获得比需要昂贵的硬对挖掘和重新排序技术的方法更好或可比的结果。代码和模型位于：https://github.com/marialeyvallina/generalized_contrastive_loss

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于分级相似性监督的数据高效大规模场景识别方法，通过改进标签策略和损失函数提升视觉定位性能。
AI.motivation: 现有场景识别方法使用二值标签（相同/不同场景）忽略相机位姿差异导致的连续相似性关系，导致训练信号噪声大且需依赖昂贵的难例挖掘算法。
AI.method: 通过定位元数据自动生成分级相似性标签，并设计通用对比损失函数（GCL），结合新标签训练对比网络，无需难例挖掘即可实现高效训练。
AI.result: 实验表明新方法在无需难例挖掘和重排序技术的情况下，通过近邻搜索获得优于或媲美现有方法的场景识别性能。
AI.conclusion: 分级相似性监督与GCL的结合有效解决了传统二值标签的局限性，为大规模场景识别提供了更高效的数据利用方案。

Code and models available at: https://github.com/marialeyvallina/generalized_contrastive_loss 视觉地点识别（VPR）是计算机视觉视觉定位的一项基本任务。现有的方法是使用描绘相同地点或不同地点的图像对进行训练的。这种二元指示不考虑从不同位置拍摄的同一地点的图像之间的连续相似性关系，这是由相机姿态的连续性质决定的。二元相似性会在 VPR 方法的训练中引入噪声监督信号，导致其陷入局部极小值并需要昂贵的硬挖掘算法来保证收敛。由于相机姿势差异，同一地点的两个图像仅部分共享视觉线索，因此我们部署了自动重新注释策略来重新标记 VPR 数据集。我们根据可用的本地化元数据计算图像对的分级相似性标签。此外，我们提出了一种新的广义对比损失（GCL），它使用分级相似性标签来训练对比网络。我们证明，使用新标签和 GCL 可以免除硬对挖掘，并通过最近邻搜索训练在 VPR 中表现更好的图像描述符，获得比需要昂贵的硬对挖掘和重新排序技术的方法更好或可比的结果。代码和模型位于：https://github.com/marialeyvallina/generalized_contrastive_loss 本文提出一种基于分级相似性监督的数据高效大规模场景识别方法，通过改进标签策略和损失函数提升视觉定位性能。 Maria Leyva-Vallina,Nicola Strisciuglio,Nicolai Petkov cs.CV,eess.IV

authors_text: Kenyu Kobayashi,Renata Khasanova,Arno Schneuwly,Felix Schmidt,Matteo Casserini
title: Unlocking Layer-wise Relevance Propagation for Autoencoders
title_zh: 解锁自动编码器的逐层相关性传播
abstract: Autoencoders are a powerful and versatile tool often used for various problems such as anomaly detection, image processing and machine translation. However, their reconstructions are not always trivial to explain. Therefore, we propose a fast explainability solution by extending the Layer-wise Relevance Propagation method with the help of Deep Taylor Decomposition framework. Furthermore, we introduce a novel validation technique for comparing our explainability approach with baseline methods in the case of missing ground-truth data. Our results highlight computational as well as qualitative advantages of the proposed explainability solution with respect to existing methods.
summary: Autoencoders are a powerful and versatile tool often used for various problems such as anomaly detection, image processing and machine translation. However, their reconstructions are not always trivial to explain. Therefore, we propose a fast explainability solution by extending the Layer-wise Relevance Propagation method with the help of Deep Taylor Decomposition framework. Furthermore, we introduce a novel validation technique for comparing our explainability approach with baseline methods in the case of missing ground-truth data. Our results highlight computational as well as qualitative advantages of the proposed explainability solution with respect to existing methods.
abstract_zh: 自动编码器是一种功能强大且多功能的工具，通常用于解决异常检测、图像处理和机器翻译等各种问题。然而，它们的重建并不总是容易解释。因此，我们通过借助深度泰勒分解框架扩展分层相关性传播方法，提出了一种快速可解释性解决方案。此外，我们引入了一种新颖的验证技术，用于在缺少真实数据的情况下将我们的可解释性方法与基线方法进行比较。我们的结果强调了所提出的可解释性解决方案相对于现有方法的计算和定性优势。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于深度泰勒分解框架的快速可解释性方法，用于解释自编码器的重建过程，并引入了新的验证技术。
AI.motivation: 自编码器虽应用广泛，但其重建结果的可解释性不足，且缺乏有效的验证手段来比较不同解释方法。
AI.method: 扩展层相关传播方法并结合深度泰勒分解框架，设计新型验证技术以在无真实数据的情况下评估解释效果。
AI.result: 实验显示该方法在计算效率和解释质量上均优于现有方法，验证技术有效区分了不同解释方案的优劣。
AI.conclusion: 提出的可解释性方案为自编码器应用提供了更高效的解释工具，验证方法弥补了无监督场景下的评估空白。

authors_text: Karthick Panner Selvam,Mats Brorsson
title: DIPPM: a Deep Learning Inference Performance Predictive Model using Graph Neural Networks
title_zh: DIPPM：使用图神经网络的深度学习推理性能预测模型
abstract: Deep Learning (DL) has developed to become a corner-stone in many everyday applications that we are now relying on. However, making sure that the DL model uses the underlying hardware efficiently takes a lot of effort. Knowledge about inference characteristics can help to find the right match so that enough resources are given to the model, but not too much. We have developed a DL Inference Performance Predictive Model (DIPPM) that predicts the inference latency, energy, and memory usage of a given input DL model on the NVIDIA A100 GPU. We also devised an algorithm to suggest the appropriate A100 Multi-Instance GPU profile from the output of DIPPM. We developed a methodology to convert DL models expressed in multiple frameworks to a generalized graph structure that is used in DIPPM. It means DIPPM can parse input DL models from various frameworks. Our DIPPM can be used not only helps to find suitable hardware configurations but also helps to perform rapid design-space exploration for the inference performance of a model. We constructed a graph multi-regression dataset consisting of 10,508 different DL models to train and evaluate the performance of DIPPM, and reached a resulting Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) as low as 1.9%.
summary: Deep Learning (DL) has developed to become a corner-stone in many everyday applications that we are now relying on. However, making sure that the DL model uses the underlying hardware efficiently takes a lot of effort. Knowledge about inference characteristics can help to find the right match so that enough resources are given to the model, but not too much. We have developed a DL Inference Performance Predictive Model (DIPPM) that predicts the inference latency, energy, and memory usage of a given input DL model on the NVIDIA A100 GPU. We also devised an algorithm to suggest the appropriate A100 Multi-Instance GPU profile from the output of DIPPM. We developed a methodology to convert DL models expressed in multiple frameworks to a generalized graph structure that is used in DIPPM. It means DIPPM can parse input DL models from various frameworks. Our DIPPM can be used not only helps to find suitable hardware configurations but also helps to perform rapid design-space exploration for the inference performance of a model. We constructed a graph multi-regression dataset consisting of 10,508 different DL models to train and evaluate the performance of DIPPM, and reached a resulting Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) as low as 1.9%.
abstract_zh: 深度学习 (DL) 已发展成为我们现在所依赖的许多日常应用程序的基石。然而，确保深度学习模型有效地使用底层硬件需要​​付出很大的努力。有关推理特征的知识可以帮助找到正确的匹配，以便为模型提供足够的资源，但不要太多。我们开发了深度学习推理性能预测模型 (DIPPM)，可以预测 NVIDIA A100 GPU 上给定输入深度学习模型的推理延迟、能量和内存使用情况。我们还设计了一种算法，根据 DIPPM 的输出建议适当的 A100 多实例 GPU 配置文件。我们开发了一种方法，可以将多种框架中表达的深度学习模型转换为 DIPPM 中使用的通用图结构。这意味着 DIPPM 可以解析来自各种框架的输入 DL 模型。我们的 DIPPM 不仅可以帮助找到合适的硬件配置，还可以帮助执行快速设计空间探索以提高模型的推理性能。我们构建了一个由 10,508 个不同 DL 模型组成的图多重回归数据集来训练和评估 DIPPM 的性能，并达到了低至 1.9% 的平均绝对百分比误差 (MAPE)。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于图神经网络的深度学习推理性能预测模型DIPPM，用于预测DL模型在NVIDIA A100 GPU上的推理延迟、能耗和内存使用情况。
AI.motivation: 深度学习模型的硬件资源匹配需要大量人工优化，而现有方法难以快速评估不同模型的推理性能，导致资源分配效率低下。
AI.method: 将多种框架下的DL模型转换为通用图结构，利用图神经网络构建多回归预测模型，并设计算法根据预测结果推荐A100 GPU的多实例配置。
AI.result: 在包含10,508个DL模型的数据集上训练后，DIPPM的平均绝对百分比误差（MAPE）低至1.9%。
AI.conclusion: DIPPM能有效预测模型推理性能，辅助硬件配置选择和加速推理性能的设计空间探索。

authors_text: Chen Ju,Zeqian Li,Peisen Zhao,Ya Zhang,Xiaopeng Zhang,Qi Tian,Yanfeng Wang,Weidi Xie
title: Multi-modal Prompting for Low-Shot Temporal Action Localization
title_zh: 低镜头时间动作定位的多模式提示
abstract: In this paper, we consider the problem of temporal action localization under low-shot (zero-shot & few-shot) scenario, with the goal of detecting and classifying the action instances from arbitrary categories within some untrimmed videos, even not seen at training time. We adopt a Transformer-based two-stage action localization architecture with class-agnostic action proposal, followed by open-vocabulary classification. We make the following contributions. First, to compensate image-text foundation models with temporal motions, we improve category-agnostic action proposal by explicitly aligning embeddings of optical flows, RGB and texts, which has largely been ignored in existing low-shot methods. Second, to improve open-vocabulary action classification, we construct classifiers with strong discriminative power, i.e., avoid lexical ambiguities. To be specific, we propose to prompt the pre-trained CLIP text encoder either with detailed action descriptions (acquired from large-scale language models), or visually-conditioned instance-specific prompt vectors. Third, we conduct thorough experiments and ablation studies on THUMOS14 and ActivityNet1.3, demonstrating the superior performance of our proposed model, outperforming existing state-of-the-art approaches by one significant margin.
summary: In this paper, we consider the problem of temporal action localization under low-shot (zero-shot & few-shot) scenario, with the goal of detecting and classifying the action instances from arbitrary categories within some untrimmed videos, even not seen at training time. We adopt a Transformer-based two-stage action localization architecture with class-agnostic action proposal, followed by open-vocabulary classification. We make the following contributions. First, to compensate image-text foundation models with temporal motions, we improve category-agnostic action proposal by explicitly aligning embeddings of optical flows, RGB and texts, which has largely been ignored in existing low-shot methods. Second, to improve open-vocabulary action classification, we construct classifiers with strong discriminative power, i.e., avoid lexical ambiguities. To be specific, we propose to prompt the pre-trained CLIP text encoder either with detailed action descriptions (acquired from large-scale language models), or visually-conditioned instance-specific prompt vectors. Third, we conduct thorough experiments and ablation studies on THUMOS14 and ActivityNet1.3, demonstrating the superior performance of our proposed model, outperforming existing state-of-the-art approaches by one significant margin.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们考虑低镜头（零镜头和少镜头）场景下的时间动作定位问题，目标是检测和分类一些未修剪视频中任意类别的动作实例，甚至在训练时没有看到。我们采用基于 Transformer 的两阶段动作本地化架构，具有与类无关的动作建议，然后是开放词汇分类。我们做出以下贡献。首先，为了用时间运动补偿图像文本基础模型，我们通过显式对齐光流、RGB 和文本的嵌入来改进与类别无关的动作建议，这在现有的低样本方法中很大程度上被忽略了。其次，为了改进开放词汇动作分类，我们构建了具有强大判别能力的分类器，即避免词汇歧义。具体来说，我们建议使用详细的动作描述（从大规模语言模型获取）或视觉条件特定于实例的提示向量来提示预先训练的 CLIP 文本编码器。第三，我们对 THUMOS14 和 ActivityNet1.3 进行了彻底的实验和消融研究，证明了我们提出的模型的卓越性能，明显优于现有的最先进方法。

AI.tldr: 本文研究在低样本（零样本和少样本）情况下，从未剪辑视频中检测和分类任意类别动作实例的时序动作定位问题。
AI.motivation: 现有低样本方法忽视了时序运动信息，且难以处理训练时未见的动作类别，导致开放词汇分类效果不佳。
AI.method: 提出基于Transformer的两阶段架构：第一阶段通过融合光流、RGB和文本嵌入生成类别无关的动作提议；第二阶段使用详细动作描述或视觉条件化提示向量增强CLIP文本编码器的开放词汇分类能力。
AI.result: 在THUMOS14和ActivityNet1.3数据集上实验表明，该方法显著优于现有最先进方法。
AI.conclusion: 通过显式对齐多模态嵌入和优化提示策略，本文方法有效提升了低样本场景下时序动作定位的性能。

search_text: Multi-modal Prompting for Low-Shot Temporal Action Localization 低镜头时间动作定位的多模式提示 In this paper, we consider the problem of temporal action localization under low-shot (zero-shot & few-shot) scenario, with the goal of detecting and classifying the action instances from arbitrary categories within some untrimmed videos, even not seen at training time. We adopt a Transformer-based two-stage action localization architecture with class-agnostic action proposal, followed by open-vocabulary classification. We make the following contributions. First, to compensate image-text foundation models with temporal motions, we improve category-agnostic action proposal by explicitly aligning embeddings of optical flows, RGB and texts, which has largely been ignored in existing low-shot methods. Second, to improve open-vocabulary action classification, we construct classifiers with strong discriminative power, i.e., avoid lexical ambiguities. To be specific, we propose to prompt the pre-trained CLIP text encoder either with detailed action descriptions (acquired from large-scale language models), or visually-conditioned instance-specific prompt vectors. Third, we conduct thorough experiments and ablation studies on THUMOS14 and ActivityNet1.3, demonstrating the superior performance of our proposed model, outperforming existing state-of-the-art approaches by one significant margin. 在本文中，我们考虑低镜头（零镜头和少镜头）场景下的时间动作定位问题，目标是检测和分类一些未修剪视频中任意类别的动作实例，甚至在训练时没有看到。我们采用基于 Transformer 的两阶段动作本地化架构，具有与类无关的动作建议，然后是开放词汇分类。我们做出以下贡献。首先，为了用时间运动补偿图像文本基础模型，我们通过显式对齐光流、RGB 和文本的嵌入来改进与类别无关的动作建议，这在现有的低样本方法中很大程度上被忽略了。其次，为了改进开放词汇动作分类，我们构建了具有强大判别能力的分类器，即避免词汇歧义。具体来说，我们建议使用详细的动作描述（从大规模语言模型获取）或视觉条件特定于实例的提示向量来提示预先训练的 CLIP 文本编码器。第三，我们对 THUMOS14 和 ActivityNet1.3 进行了彻底的实验和消融研究，证明了我们提出的模型的卓越性能，明显优于现有的最先进方法。 本文研究在低样本（零样本和少样本）情况下，从未剪辑视频中检测和分类任意类别动作实例的时序动作定位问题。 Chen Ju,Zeqian Li,Peisen Zhao,Ya Zhang,Xiaopeng Zhang,Qi Tian,Yanfeng Wang,Weidi Xie cs.CV

authors_text: Jingyi Xu,Tushar Vaidya,Yufei Wu,Saket Chandra,Zhangsheng Lai,Kai Fong Ernest Chong
title: Abstract Visual Reasoning: An Algebraic Approach for Solving Raven's Progressive Matrices
title_zh: 抽象视觉推理：求解 ​​Raven 渐进矩阵的代数方法
comment: Accepted at IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR) 2023. 30 pages, 7 figures (including supplementary material). First three authors contributed equally. Code is available at: https://github.com/Xu-Jingyi/AlgebraicMR
comments: Accepted at IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR) 2023. 30 pages, 7 figures (including supplementary material). First three authors contributed equally. Code is available at: https://github.com/Xu-Jingyi/AlgebraicMR
abstract: We introduce algebraic machine reasoning, a new reasoning framework that is well-suited for abstract reasoning. Effectively, algebraic machine reasoning reduces the difficult process of novel problem-solving to routine algebraic computation. The fundamental algebraic objects of interest are the ideals of some suitably initialized polynomial ring. We shall explain how solving Raven's Progressive Matrices (RPMs) can be realized as computational problems in algebra, which combine various well-known algebraic subroutines that include: Computing the Gröbner basis of an ideal, checking for ideal containment, etc. Crucially, the additional algebraic structure satisfied by ideals allows for more operations on ideals beyond set-theoretic operations. Our algebraic machine reasoning framework is not only able to select the correct answer from a given answer set, but also able to generate the correct answer with only the question matrix given. Experiments on the I-RAVEN dataset yield an overall $93.2\%$ accuracy, which significantly outperforms the current state-of-the-art accuracy of $77.0\%$ and exceeds human performance at $84.4\%$ accuracy.
summary: We introduce algebraic machine reasoning, a new reasoning framework that is well-suited for abstract reasoning. Effectively, algebraic machine reasoning reduces the difficult process of novel problem-solving to routine algebraic computation. The fundamental algebraic objects of interest are the ideals of some suitably initialized polynomial ring. We shall explain how solving Raven's Progressive Matrices (RPMs) can be realized as computational problems in algebra, which combine various well-known algebraic subroutines that include: Computing the Gröbner basis of an ideal, checking for ideal containment, etc. Crucially, the additional algebraic structure satisfied by ideals allows for more operations on ideals beyond set-theoretic operations. Our algebraic machine reasoning framework is not only able to select the correct answer from a given answer set, but also able to generate the correct answer with only the question matrix given. Experiments on the I-RAVEN dataset yield an overall $93.2\%$ accuracy, which significantly outperforms the current state-of-the-art accuracy of $77.0\%$ and exceeds human performance at $84.4\%$ accuracy.
abstract_zh: 我们引入代数机器推理，这是一种非常适合抽象推理的新推理框架。代数机器推理有效地将解决新问题的困难过程简化为常规代数计算。感兴趣的基本代数对象是一些适当初始化的多项式环的理想。我们将解释如何将求解瑞文渐进矩阵 (RPM) 实现为代数中的计算问题，它结合了各种众所周知的代数子例程，包括：计算理想的 Gröbner 基、检查理想包含性等。至关重要的是，理想满足的附加代数结构允许对理想进行超出集合论运算的更多运算。我们的代数机器推理框架不仅能够从给定的答案集中选择正确的答案，而且能够仅根据给定的问题矩阵生成正确的答案。在 I-RAVEN 数据集上进行的实验产生了 93.2\%$ 的总体准确率，显着优于当前最先进的 $77.0\%$ 准确率，并超过了人类准确率 $84.4\%$ 的表现。

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了一种代数机器推理框架，用于解决抽象推理问题，特别是拉文渐进矩阵（RPMs）。
AI.motivation: 传统的解决抽象推理问题的方法存在局限性，难以有效处理复杂的新型问题，因此需要一种新的推理框架。
AI.method: 该方法基于代数结构，将RPMs问题转化为计算问题，包括计算理想格罗布纳基和检查理想包含等代数子例程。
AI.result: 在I-RAVEN数据集上的实验结果表明，该方法的准确率为93.2%，显著优于当前最先进的77.0%和人类表现的84.4%。
AI.conclusion: 该代数机器推理框架不仅能够从给定的答案集中选择正确答案，还能仅根据问题矩阵生成正确答案，显示出其在抽象推理任务中的强大能力。
