authors_text: Javier Gamazo Tejero,Martin S. Zinkernagel,Sebastian Wolf,Raphael Sznitman,Pablo Márquez Neila
title: Full or Weak annotations? An adaptive strategy for budget-constrained annotation campaigns
title_zh: 完整注释还是弱注释？针对预算有限的注释活动的自适应策略
abstract: Annotating new datasets for machine learning tasks is tedious, time-consuming, and costly. For segmentation applications, the burden is particularly high as manual delineations of relevant image content are often extremely expensive or can only be done by experts with domain-specific knowledge. Thanks to developments in transfer learning and training with weak supervision, segmentation models can now also greatly benefit from annotations of different kinds. However, for any new domain application looking to use weak supervision, the dataset builder still needs to define a strategy to distribute full segmentation and other weak annotations. Doing so is challenging, however, as it is a priori unknown how to distribute an annotation budget for a given new dataset. To this end, we propose a novel approach to determine annotation strategies for segmentation datasets, whereby estimating what proportion of segmentation and classification annotations should be collected given a fixed budget. To do so, our method sequentially determines proportions of segmentation and classification annotations to collect for budget-fractions by modeling the expected improvement of the final segmentation model. We show in our experiments that our approach yields annotations that perform very close to the optimal for a number of different annotation budgets and datasets.
summary: Annotating new datasets for machine learning tasks is tedious, time-consuming, and costly. For segmentation applications, the burden is particularly high as manual delineations of relevant image content are often extremely expensive or can only be done by experts with domain-specific knowledge. Thanks to developments in transfer learning and training with weak supervision, segmentation models can now also greatly benefit from annotations of different kinds. However, for any new domain application looking to use weak supervision, the dataset builder still needs to define a strategy to distribute full segmentation and other weak annotations. Doing so is challenging, however, as it is a priori unknown how to distribute an annotation budget for a given new dataset. To this end, we propose a novel approach to determine annotation strategies for segmentation datasets, whereby estimating what proportion of segmentation and classification annotations should be collected given a fixed budget. To do so, our method sequentially determines proportions of segmentation and classification annotations to collect for budget-fractions by modeling the expected improvement of the final segmentation model. We show in our experiments that our approach yields annotations that perform very close to the optimal for a number of different annotation budgets and datasets.
abstract_zh: 为机器学习任务注释新数据集非常乏味、耗时且成本高昂。对于分割应用程序来说，负担特别高，因为相关图像内容的手动描绘通常非常昂贵，或者只能由具有特定领域知识的专家来完成。由于迁移学习和弱监督训练的发展，分割模型现在也可以从不同类型的注释中受益匪浅。然而，对于任何希望使用弱监督的新领域应用程序，数据集构建者仍然需要定义一种策略来分发完整分割和其他弱注释。然而，这样做具有挑战性，因为先验未知如何为给定的新数据集分配注释预算。为此，我们提出了一种新方法来确定分割数据集的注释策略，从而估计在给定固定预算的情况下应收集的分割和分类注释的比例。为此，我们的方法通过对最终分割模型的预期改进进行建模，依次确定分割和分类注释的比例，以收集预算分数。我们在实验中表明，我们的方法产生的注释对于许多不同的注释预算和数据集来说，其性能非常接近最佳值。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种在预算受限情况下，为分割任务数据集分配全标注与弱标注比例的自适应策略。
AI.motivation: 全标注成本高昂且耗时，而弱标注虽成本低但精度有限，如何在有限预算下平衡两者比例是分割模型训练的关键挑战。
AI.method: 通过建模最终分割模型的预期改进，依次确定不同预算比例下应收集的分割标注与分类标注比例，实现动态分配。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法在多种预算分配和数据集上表现接近最优方案，显著提升标注效率与模型性能。
AI.conclusion: 该策略为预算受限的分割任务提供了高效的标注分配方案，可在成本与精度间取得良好平衡。

authors_text: Tina Yao,Nicole St. Clair,Gabriel F. Miller,Adam L. Dorfman,Mark A. Fogel,Sunil Ghelani,Rajesh Krishnamurthy,Christopher Z. Lam,Joshua D. Robinson,David Schidlow,Timothy C. Slesnick,Justin Weigand,Michael Quail,Rahul Rathod,Jennifer A. Steeden,Vivek Muthurangu
title: Deep Learning Pipeline for Preprocessing and Segmenting Cardiac Magnetic Resonance of Single Ventricle Patients from an Image Registry
title_zh: 用于从图像注册表中预处理和分割单心室患者的心脏磁共振的深度学习管道
abstract: Purpose: To develop and evaluate an end-to-end deep learning pipeline for segmentation and analysis of cardiac magnetic resonance images to provide core-lab processing for a multi-centre registry of Fontan patients. Materials and Methods: This retrospective study used training (n = 175), validation (n = 25) and testing (n = 50) cardiac magnetic resonance image exams collected from 13 institutions in the UK, US and Canada. The data was used to train and evaluate a pipeline containing three deep-learning models. The pipeline's performance was assessed on the Dice and IoU score between the automated and reference standard manual segmentation. Cardiac function values were calculated from both the automated and manual segmentation and evaluated using Bland-Altman analysis and paired t-tests. The overall pipeline was further evaluated qualitatively on 475 unseen patient exams. Results: For the 50 testing dataset, the pipeline achieved a median Dice score of 0.91 (0.89-0.94) for end-diastolic volume, 0.86 (0.82-0.89) for end-systolic volume, and 0.74 (0.70-0.77) for myocardial mass. The deep learning-derived end-diastolic volume, end-systolic volume, myocardial mass, stroke volume and ejection fraction had no statistical difference compared to the same values derived from manual segmentation with p values all greater than 0.05. For the 475 unseen patient exams, the pipeline achieved 68% adequate segmentation in both systole and diastole, 26% needed minor adjustments in either systole or diastole, 5% needed major adjustments, and the cropping model only failed in 0.4%. Conclusion: Deep learning pipeline can provide standardised 'core-lab' segmentation for Fontan patients. This pipeline can now be applied to the >4500 cardiac magnetic resonance exams currently in the FORCE registry as well as any new patients that are recruited.

For the 475 unseen patient exams, the pipeline achieved 68% adequate segmentation in both systole and diastole, 26% needed minor adjustments in either systole or diastole, 5% needed major adjustments, and the cropping model only failed in 0.4%. abstract_zh: 目的：开发和评估用于心脏磁共振图像分割和分析的端到端深度学习管道，为 Fontan 患者的多中心登记提供核心实验室处理。材料和方法：这项回顾性研究使用了从英国、美国和加拿大 13 个机构收集的训练 (n = 175)、验证 (n = 25) 和测试 (n = 50) 心脏磁共振图像检查。这些数据用于训练和评估包含三个深度学习模型的管道。管道的性能根据自动分割和参考标准手动分割之间的 Dice 和 IoU 分数进行评估。通过自动和手动分割计算心脏功能值，并使用 Bland-Altman 分析和配对 t 检验进行评估。在 475 名未见过的患者检查中进一步对整个流程进行了定性评估。结果：对于 50 个测试数据集，管道的舒张末期容积中值 Dice 得分为 0.91 (0.89-0.94)，收缩末期容积为 0.86 (0.82-0.89)，心肌质量为 0.74 (0.70-0.77)。深度学习得出的舒张末期容积、收缩末期容积、心肌质量、每搏输出量和射血分数与手动分割得出的相同值相比没有统计学差异，p值均大于0.05。对于 475 名未见过的患者检查，管道在收缩期和舒张期实现了 68% 的充分分割，26% 需要在收缩期或舒张期进行小幅调整，5% 需要进行重大调整，而裁剪模型仅在 0.4% 的情况下失败。结论：深度学习流程可以为 Fontan 患者提供标准化的“核心实验室”分割。该管道现在可应用于 FORCE 注册中心目前超过 4500 例心脏磁共振检查以及招募的任何新患者。

AI.tldr: 开发用于单心室患者心脏磁共振图像预处理和分割的端到端深度学习流程，以支持多中心注册库的核心实验室分析。
AI.motivation: 手动分割心脏磁共振图像耗时且结果可能不一致，需自动化方法提高效率并标准化多中心数据处理。
AI.method: 基于来自13家机构的175例训练数据、25例验证数据和50例测试数据，构建包含三个深度学习模型的流水线，通过Dice/IoU分数、Bland-Altman分析及配对t检验评估分割性能，并在475例未见数据上进行定性验证。
AI.result: 测试集分割Dice中位数达0.91（舒张末期容积）、0.86（收缩末期容积）和0.74（心肌质量），自动分割与手动结果无统计学差异（p>0.05）；475例未见数据中68%分割合格，仅0.4%裁剪失败。
AI.conclusion: 该深度学习流水线可为Fontan患者提供标准化核心实验室分割，适用于当前4500+例FORCE注册数据及新患者影像分析。

authors_text: Yongkang Cheng,Shaoli Huang,Jifeng Ning,Ying Shan
title: BoPR: Body-aware Part Regressor for Human Shape and Pose Estimation
title_zh: BoPR：用于人体形状和姿势估计的身体感知部分回归器
abstract: This paper presents a novel approach for estimating human body shape and pose from monocular images that effectively addresses the challenges of occlusions and depth ambiguity. Our proposed method BoPR, the Body-aware Part Regressor, first extracts features of both the body and part regions using an attention-guided mechanism. We then utilize these features to encode extra part-body dependency for per-part regression, with part features as queries and body feature as a reference. This allows our network to infer the spatial relationship of occluded parts with the body by leveraging visible parts and body reference information. Our method outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods on two benchmark datasets, and our experiments show that it significantly surpasses existing methods in terms of depth ambiguity and occlusion handling. These results provide strong evidence of the effectiveness of our approach.The code and data are available for research purposes at https://github.com/cyk990422/BoPR.
summary: This paper presents a novel approach for estimating human body shape and pose from monocular images that effectively addresses the challenges of occlusions and depth ambiguity. Our proposed method BoPR, the Body-aware Part Regressor, first extracts features of both the body and part regions using an attention-guided mechanism. We then utilize these features to encode extra part-body dependency for per-part regression, with part features as queries and body feature as a reference. This allows our network to infer the spatial relationship of occluded parts with the body by leveraging visible parts and body reference information. Our method outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods on two benchmark datasets, and our experiments show that it significantly surpasses existing methods in terms of depth ambiguity and occlusion handling. These results provide strong evidence of the effectiveness of our approach.The code and data are available for research purposes at https://github.com/cyk990422/BoPR.
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种从单目图像估计人体形状和姿势的新方法，有效解决了遮挡和深度模糊的挑战。我们提出的方法 BoPR（身体感知零件回归器）首先使用注意力引导机制提取身体和零件区域的特征。然后，我们利用这些特征来编码额外的部位-身体依赖性，以进行每个部位的回归，以部位特征作为查询，以身体特征作为参考。这使得我们的网络能够利用可见部分和身体参考信息来推断被遮挡部分与身体的空间关系。我们的方法在两个基准数据集上优于现有的最先进方法，并且我们的实验表明，它在深度模糊性和遮挡处理方面显着超越了现有方法。这些结果为我们方法的有效性提供了有力的证据。代码和数据可用于研究目的：https://github.com/cyk990422/BoPR。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种从单目图像中估计人体形状和姿态的新方法，有效解决遮挡和深度模糊的挑战。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在处理人体姿态估计时面临遮挡和深度模糊的挑战，导致估计精度下降。
AI.method: 提出BoPR方法，通过注意力机制提取身体及部件特征，并利用部件特征作为查询、身体特征作为参考，建立部件与身体的依赖关系以推断被遮挡部位的空间关系。
AI.result: 在两个基准数据集上显著优于现有方法，尤其在深度模糊和遮挡场景下表现突出。
AI.conclusion: BoPR方法通过引入身体感知的部件回归机制，有效提升了人体形状和姿态估计的准确性，代码和数据已公开共享。

authors_text: Jintao Guo,Na Wang,Lei Qi,Yinghuan Shi
title: ALOFT: A Lightweight MLP-like Architecture with Dynamic Low-frequency Transform for Domain Generalization
title_zh: ALOFT：一种轻量级的类 MLP 架构，具有用于域泛化的动态低频变换
comment: Accepted by CVPR2023. The code is available at https://github.com/lingeringlight/ALOFT/
comments: Accepted by CVPR2023. The code is available at https://github.com/lingeringlight/ALOFT/
abstract: Domain generalization (DG) aims to learn a model that generalizes well to unseen target domains utilizing multiple source domains without re-training. Most existing DG works are based on convolutional neural networks (CNNs). However, the local operation of the convolution kernel makes the model focus too much on local representations (e.g., texture), which inherently causes the model more prone to overfit to the source domains and hampers its generalization ability. Recently, several MLP-based methods have achieved promising results in supervised learning tasks by learning global interactions among different patches of the image. Inspired by this, in this paper, we first analyze the difference between CNN and MLP methods in DG and find that MLP methods exhibit a better generalization ability because they can better capture the global representations (e.g., structure) than CNN methods. Then, based on a recent lightweight MLP method, we obtain a strong baseline that outperforms most state-of-the-art CNN-based methods. The baseline can learn global structure representations with a filter to suppress structure irrelevant information in the frequency space. Moreover, we propose a dynAmic LOw-Frequency spectrum Transform (ALOFT) that can perturb local texture features while preserving global structure features, thus enabling the filter to remove structure-irrelevant information sufficiently. Extensive experiments on four benchmarks have demonstrated that our method can achieve great performance improvement with a small number of parameters compared to SOTA CNN-based DG methods. Our code is available at https://github.com/lingeringlight/ALOFT/.
summary: Domain generalization (DG) aims to learn a model that generalizes well to unseen target domains utilizing multiple source domains without re-training. Most existing DG works are based on convolutional neural networks (CNNs). However, the local operation of the convolution kernel makes the model focus too much on local representations (e.g., texture), which inherently causes the model more prone to overfit to the source domains and hampers its generalization ability. Recently, several MLP-based methods have achieved promising results in supervised learning tasks by learning global interactions among different patches of the image. Inspired by this, in this paper, we first analyze the difference between CNN and MLP methods in DG and find that MLP methods exhibit a better generalization ability because they can better capture the global representations (e.g., structure) than CNN methods. Then, based on a recent lightweight MLP method, we obtain a strong baseline that outperforms most state-of-the-art CNN-based methods. The baseline can learn global structure representations with a filter to suppress structure irrelevant information in the frequency space. Moreover, we propose a dynAmic LOw-Frequency spectrum Transform (ALOFT) that can perturb local texture features while preserving global structure features, thus enabling the filter to remove structure-irrelevant information sufficiently. Extensive experiments on four benchmarks have demonstrated that our method can achieve great performance improvement with a small number of parameters compared to SOTA CNN-based DG methods. Our code is available at https://github.com/lingeringlight/ALOFT/.
abstract_zh: 域泛化（DG）旨在学习一种模型，该模型可以利用多个源域很好地泛化到未见过的目标域，而无需重新训练。大多数现有的 DG 工作都是基于卷积神经网络（CNN）的。然而，卷积核的局部操作使得模型过于关注局部表示（例如纹理），这本质上导致模型更容易过度拟合源域并阻碍其泛化能力。最近，一些基于 MLP 的方法通过学习图像不同块之间的全局交互，在监督学习任务中取得了有希望的结果。受此启发，在本文中，我们首先分析了 DG 中 CNN 和 MLP 方法的差异，发现 MLP 方法表现出更好的泛化能力，因为它们比 CNN 方法可以更好地捕获全局表示（例如结构）。然后，基于最近的轻量级 MLP 方法，我们获得了优于大多数最先进的基于 CNN 的方法的强大基线。基线可以使用滤波器学习全局结构表示，以抑制频率空间中的结构无关信息。此外，我们提出了一种动态低频频谱变换（ALOFT），它可以扰乱局部纹理特征，同时保留全局结构特征，从而使滤波器能够充分去除与结构无关的信息。对四个基准的大量实验表明，与基于 SOTA CNN 的 DG 方法相比，我们的方法可以用少量参数实现巨大的性能提升。我们的代码可在 https://github.com/lingeringlight/ALOFT/ 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种轻量级MLP-like架构ALOFT，通过动态低频变换提升领域泛化能力。
AI.motivation: 传统CNN因过度关注局部纹理特征导致领域泛化能力不足，而MLP能更好捕捉全局结构表示，但现有方法参数量大且未充分抑制无关结构信息。
AI.method: 基于轻量MLP构建强基线，设计频域滤波器抑制无关结构信息，并提出动态低频变换模块（ALOFT）扰动局部纹理特征以保留全局结构。
AI.result: 在四个基准数据集上，ALOFT以更少参数显著优于SOTA CNN领域泛化方法，代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: ALOFT通过全局结构感知与动态频域扰动有效提升领域泛化能力，为轻量领域泛化模型提供新思路。

search_text: ALOFT: A Lightweight MLP-like Architecture with Dynamic Low-frequency Transform for Domain Generalization ALOFT：一种轻量级的类 MLP 架构，具有用于域泛化的动态低频变换 Domain generalization (DG) aims to learn a model that generalizes well to unseen target domains utilizing multiple source domains without re-training. Most existing DG works are based on convolutional neural networks (CNNs). However, the local operation of the convolution kernel makes the model focus too much on local representations (e.g., texture), which inherently causes the model more prone to overfit to the source domains and hampers its generalization ability. Recently, several MLP-based methods have achieved promising results in supervised learning tasks by learning global interactions among different patches of the image. Inspired by this, in this paper, we first analyze the difference between CNN and MLP methods in DG and find that MLP methods exhibit a better generalization ability because they can better capture the global representations (e.g., structure) than CNN methods. Then, based on a recent lightweight MLP method, we obtain a strong baseline that outperforms most state-of-the-art CNN-based methods. The baseline can learn global structure representations with a filter to suppress structure irrelevant information in the frequency space. Moreover, we propose a dynAmic LOw-Frequency spectrum Transform (ALOFT) that can perturb local texture features while preserving global structure features, thus enabling the filter to remove structure-irrelevant information sufficiently. Extensive experiments on four benchmarks have demonstrated that our method can achieve great performance improvement with a small number of parameters compared to SOTA CNN-based DG methods. authors_text: Jing Zhang,Chuanwen Li,Jianzgong Qi,Jiayuan He
title: A Survey on Class Imbalance in Federated Learning
title_zh: 联邦学习中类不平衡的调查
abstract: Federated learning, which allows multiple client devices in a network to jointly train a machine learning model without direct exposure of clients' data, is an emerging distributed learning technique due to its nature of privacy preservation. However, it has been found that models trained with federated learning usually have worse performance than their counterparts trained in the standard centralized learning mode, especially when the training data is imbalanced. In the context of federated learning, data imbalance may occur either locally one one client device, or globally across many devices. The complexity of different types of data imbalance has posed challenges to the development of federated learning technique, especially considering the need of relieving data imbalance issue and preserving data privacy at the same time. Therefore, in the literature, many attempts have been made to handle class imbalance in federated learning. In this paper, we present a detailed review of recent advancements along this line. We first introduce various types of class imbalance in federated learning, after which we review existing methods for estimating the extent of class imbalance without the need of knowing the actual data to preserve data privacy. After that, we discuss existing methods for handling class imbalance in FL, where the advantages and disadvantages of the these approaches are discussed. We also summarize common evaluation metrics for class imbalanced tasks, and point out potential future directions.
summary: Federated learning, which allows multiple client devices in a network to jointly train a machine learning model without direct exposure of clients' data, is an emerging distributed learning technique due to its nature of privacy preservation. However, it has been found that models trained with federated learning usually have worse performance than their counterparts trained in the standard centralized learning mode, especially when the training data is imbalanced. In the context of federated learning, data imbalance may occur either locally one one client device, or globally across many devices. The complexity of different types of data imbalance has posed challenges to the development of federated learning technique, especially considering the need of relieving data imbalance issue and preserving data privacy at the same time. Therefore, in the literature, many attempts have been made to handle class imbalance in federated learning. In this paper, we present a detailed review of recent advancements along this line. We first introduce various types of class imbalance in federated learning, after which we review existing methods for estimating the extent of class imbalance without the need of knowing the actual data to preserve data privacy. After that, we discuss existing methods for handling class imbalance in FL, where the advantages and disadvantages of the these approaches are discussed. We also summarize common evaluation metrics for class imbalanced tasks, and point out potential future directions.
abstract_zh: 联邦学习允许网络中的多个客户端设备联合训练机器学习模型，而无需直接暴露客户端数据，由于其隐私保护的性质，它是一种新兴的分布式学习技术。然而，人们发现，使用联邦学习训练的模型通常比使用标准集中学习模式训练的模型性能更差，特别是当训练数据不平衡时。在联邦学习的背景下，数据不平衡可能发生在一台客户端设备本地，也可能发生在多个设备上。不同类型数据不平衡的复杂性对联邦学习技术的发展提出了挑战，特别是考虑到同时缓解数据不平衡问题和保护数据隐私的需要。因此，在文献中，人们做出了许多尝试来解决联邦学习中的类别不平衡问题。在本文中，我们详细回顾了这方面的最新进展。我们首先介绍联邦学习中的各种类型的类别不平衡，然后回顾现有的用于估计类别不平衡程度的方法，而无需了解实际数据以保护数据隐私。之后，我们讨论现有的 FL 中处理类不平衡的方法，并讨论这些方法的优点和缺点。我们还总结了类别不平衡任务的常见评估指标，并指出了未来潜在的方向。

AI.tldr: 本文综述了联邦学习中类别不平衡问题的最新研究进展，分析了其挑战并总结了现有解决方案。
AI.motivation: 联邦学习在数据不平衡场景下性能劣于集中式学习，且需同时解决数据不平衡与隐私保护的矛盾，亟需系统性研究。
AI.method: 通过分类讨论联邦学习中的数据不平衡类型，回顾无需直接访问数据的不平衡度评估方法，并分析现有类别不平衡处理技术的优缺点。
AI.result: 总结了类别不平衡任务的常用评估指标，归纳了当前方法的局限性，并指出了隐私保护与模型性能平衡的未来研究方向。
AI.conclusion: 现有方法在处理联邦学习中的类别不平衡问题上仍存在挑战，需进一步探索兼顾隐私保护与模型泛化的创新技术路径。

authors_text: Yongjin Jeon,Youngtack Oh,Doyoung Jeong,Hyunguk Choi,Junsik Kim
title: Focus or Not: A Baseline for Anomaly Event Detection On the Open Public Places with Satellite Images
title_zh: 聚焦与否：利用卫星图像检测开放公共场所异常事件的基线
comment: I am withdrawing my submission due to issues with content modification
comments: I am withdrawing my submission due to issues with content modification
abstract: In recent years, monitoring the world wide area with satellite images has been emerged as an important issue. Site monitoring task can be divided into two independent tasks; 1) Change Detection and 2) Anomaly Event Detection. Unlike to change detection research is actively conducted based on the numerous datasets(\eg LEVIR-CD, WHU-CD, S2Looking, xView2 and etc...) to meet up the expectations of industries or governments, research on AI models for detecting anomaly events is passively and rarely conducted. In this paper, we introduce a novel satellite imagery dataset(AED-RS) for detecting anomaly events on the open public places. AED-RS Dataset contains satellite images of normal and abnormal situations of 8 open public places from all over the world. Each places are labeled with different criteria based on the difference of characteristics of each places. With this dataset, we introduce a baseline model for our dataset TB-FLOW, which can be trained in weakly-supervised manner and shows reasonable performance on the AED-RS Dataset compared with the other NF(Normalizing-Flow) based anomaly detection models. Our dataset and code will be publicly open in \url{https://github.com/SIAnalytics/RS_AnomalyDetection.git}.

Site monitoring task can be divided into two independent tasks; 1) Change Detection and 2) Anomaly Event Detection. Unlike to change detection research is actively conducted based on the numerous datasets(\eg LEVIR-CD, WHU-CD, S2Looking, xView2 and etc...) to meet up the expectations of industries or governments, research on AI models for detecting anomaly events is passively and rarely conducted. In this paper, we introduce a novel satellite imagery dataset(AED-RS) for detecting anomaly events on the open public places. AED-RS Dataset contains satellite images of normal and abnormal situations of 8 open public places from all over the world. Each places are labeled with different criteria based on the difference of characteristics of each places. With this dataset, we introduce a baseline model for our dataset TB-FLOW, which can be trained in weakly-supervised manner and shows reasonable performance on the AED-RS Dataset compared with the other NF(Normalizing-Flow) based anomaly detection models. Our dataset and code will be publicly open in \url{https://github.com/SIAnalytics/RS_AnomalyDetection.git}.
abstract_zh: 近年来，利用卫星图像监测全球范围已成为一个重要问题。现场监控任务可分为两个独立的任务； 1) 变更检测和 2) 异常事件检测。与基于众多数据集（例如 LEVIR-CD、WHU-CD、S2Looking、xView2 等）积极开展变化检测研究以满足行业或政府的期望不同，用于检测异常事件的 AI 模型的研究是被动且很少进行的。在本文中，我们介绍了一种新颖的卫星图像数据集（AED-RS），用于检测开放公共场所的异常事件。 AED-RS数据集包含来自世界各地的8个开放公共场所的正常和异常情况的卫星图像。根据每个地方的特点差异，每个地方都有不同的标准。借助该数据集，我们为数据集 TB-FLOW 引入了一个基线模型，该模型可以以弱监督方式进行训练，并且与其他基于 NF（归一化流）的异常检测模型相比，它在 AED-RS 数据集上显示出合理的性能。我们的数据集和代码将在 \url{https://github.com/SIAnalytics/RS_AnomalyDetection.git} 中公开开放。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一个名为AED-RS的新卫星图像数据集和基线模型TB-FLOW，用于检测开放公共区域的异常事件。
AI.motivation: 尽管变化检测领域已有丰富数据集，但异常事件检测研究因数据缺乏而进展缓慢，亟需建立专门数据集和基准模型。
AI.method: 构建包含全球8个开放场所正常/异常场景的AED-RS数据集，设计基于弱监督学习的TB-FLOW模型（归一化流方法）进行异常检测。
AI.result: TB-FLOW在AED-RS数据集上相较其他归一化流模型表现出合理性能，且数据集和代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: 本研究为卫星图像异常事件检测领域提供了首个基准数据集和有效方法，推动了该方向的后续研究。

search_text: Focus or Not: A Baseline for Anomaly Event Detection On the Open Public Places with Satellite Images 聚焦与否：利用卫星图像检测开放公共场所异常事件的基线 In recent years, monitoring the world wide area with satellite images has been emerged as an important issue. Site monitoring task can be divided into two independent tasks; 1) Change Detection and 2) Anomaly Event Detection. Unlike to change detection research is actively conducted based on the numerous datasets(\eg LEVIR-CD, WHU-CD, S2Looking, xView2 and etc...) to meet up the expectations of industries or governments, research on AI models for detecting anomaly events is passively and rarely conducted. In this paper, we introduce a novel satellite imagery dataset(AED-RS) for detecting anomaly events on the open public places. AED-RS Dataset contains satellite images of normal and abnormal situations of 8 open public places from all over the world. Each places are labeled with different criteria based on the difference of characteristics of each places. With this dataset, we introduce a baseline model for our dataset TB-FLOW, which can be trained in weakly-supervised manner and shows reasonable performance on the AED-RS Dataset compared with the other NF(Normalizing-Flow) based anomaly detection models. Our dataset and code will be publicly open in \url{https://github.com/SIAnalytics/RS_AnomalyDetection.git}. 近年来，利用卫星图像监测全球范围已成为一个重要问题。现场监控任务可分为两个独立的任务； 1) 变更检测和 2) 异常事件检测。与基于众多数据集（例如 LEVIR-CD、WHU-CD、S2Looking、xView2 等）积极开展变化检测研究以满足行业或政府的期望不同，用于检测异常事件的 AI 模型的研究是被动且很少进行的。在本文中，我们介绍了一种新颖的卫星图像数据集（AED-RS），用于检测开放公共场所的异常事件。 AED-RS数据集包含来自世界各地的8个开放公共场所的正常和异常情况的卫星图像。根据每个地方的特点差异，每个地方都有不同的标准。借助该数据集，我们为数据集 TB-FLOW 引入了一个基线模型，该模型可以以弱监督方式进行训练，并且与其他基于 NF（归一化流）的异常检测模型相比，它在 AED-RS 数据集上显示出合理的性能。我们的数据集和代码将在 \url{https://github.com/SIAnalytics/RS_AnomalyDetection.git} 中公开开放。 本文提出一个名为AED-RS的新卫星图像数据集和基线模型TB-FLOW，用于检测开放公共区域的异常事件。 Yongjin Jeon,Youngtack Oh,Doyoung Jeong,Hyunguk Choi,Junsik Kim cs.CV

authors_text: Fang Hu,Xuexue Sun,Ke Qing,Fenxi Xiao,Zhi Wang,Xiaolu Fan
title: Advanced Multi-Microscopic Views Cell Semi-supervised Segmentation
title_zh: 先进的多显微视图细胞半监督分割
abstract: Although deep learning (DL) shows powerful potential in cell segmentation tasks, it suffers from poor generalization as DL-based methods originally simplified cell segmentation in detecting cell membrane boundary, lacking prominent cellular structures to position overall differentiating. Moreover, the scarcity of annotated cell images limits the performance of DL models. Segmentation limitations of a single category of cell make massive practice difficult, much less, with varied modalities. In this paper, we introduce a novel semi-supervised cell segmentation method called Multi-Microscopic-view Cell semi-supervised Segmentation (MMCS), which can train cell segmentation models utilizing less labeled multi-posture cell images with different microscopy well. Technically, MMCS consists of Nucleus-assisted global recognition, Self-adaptive diameter filter, and Temporal-ensembling models. Nucleus-assisted global recognition adds additional cell nucleus channel to improve the global distinguishing performance of fuzzy cell membrane boundaries even when cells aggregate. Besides, self-adapted cell diameter filter can help separate multi-resolution cells with different morphology properly. It further leverages the temporal-ensembling models to improve the semi-supervised training process, achieving effective training with less labeled data. Additionally, optimizing the weight of unlabeled loss contributed to total loss also improve the model performance. Evaluated on the Tuning Set of NeurIPS 2022 Cell Segmentation Challenge (NeurIPS CellSeg), MMCS achieves an F1-score of 0.8239 and the running time for all cases is within the time tolerance.
summary: Although deep learning (DL) shows powerful potential in cell segmentation tasks, it suffers from poor generalization as DL-based methods originally simplified cell segmentation in detecting cell membrane boundary, lacking prominent cellular structures to position overall differentiating. Moreover, the scarcity of annotated cell images limits the performance of DL models. Segmentation limitations of a single category of cell make massive practice difficult, much less, with varied modalities. In this paper, we introduce a novel semi-supervised cell segmentation method called Multi-Microscopic-view Cell semi-supervised Segmentation (MMCS), which can train cell segmentation models utilizing less labeled multi-posture cell images with different microscopy well. Technically, MMCS consists of Nucleus-assisted global recognition, Self-adaptive diameter filter, and Temporal-ensembling models. Nucleus-assisted global recognition adds additional cell nucleus channel to improve the global distinguishing performance of fuzzy cell membrane boundaries even when cells aggregate. Besides, self-adapted cell diameter filter can help separate multi-resolution cells with different morphology properly. It further leverages the temporal-ensembling models to improve the semi-supervised training process, achieving effective training with less labeled data. Additionally, optimizing the weight of unlabeled loss contributed to total loss also improve the model performance. Evaluated on the Tuning Set of NeurIPS 2022 Cell Segmentation Challenge (NeurIPS CellSeg), MMCS achieves an F1-score of 0.8239 and the running time for all cases is within the time tolerance.
abstract_zh: 尽管深度学习（DL）在细胞分割任务中显示出强大的潜力，但其泛化性较差，因为基于深度学习的方法最初简化了检测细胞膜边界的细胞分割，缺乏突出的细胞结构来定位整体分化。此外，带注释的细胞图像的稀缺限制了深度学习模型的性能。单一类别细胞的分割限制使得大规模实践变得困难，更不用说采用不同的模式了。在本文中，我们介绍了一种新颖的半监督细胞分割方法，称为多显微镜视图细胞半监督分割（MMCS），该方法可以很好地利用不同显微镜下标记较少的多姿态细胞图像来训练细胞分割模型。从技术上讲，MMCS由Nucleus辅助全局识别、自适应直径滤波器和时间集成模型组成。细胞核辅助全局识别增加了额外的细胞核通道，即使在细胞聚集时，也能提高模糊细胞膜边界的全局区分性能。此外，自适应细胞直径过滤器可以帮助正确分离不同形态的多分辨率细胞。它进一步利用时间集成模型来改进半监督训练过程，以更少的标记数据实现有效的训练。此外，优化未标记损失对总损失的权重也可以提高模型性能。在 NeurIPS 2022 细胞分割挑战赛 (NeurIPS CellSeg) 的调优集上进行评估，MMCS 的 F1 分数为 0.8239，所有情况的运行时间都在时间容差范围内。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于多显微镜视角的半监督细胞分割方法MMCS，以解决标注数据不足和模型泛化能力差的问题。
AI.motivation: 深度学习在细胞分割中因标注数据稀缺和单类别分割局限性导致泛化能力不足，需利用多模态数据提升模型性能。
AI.method: MMCS包含细胞核辅助全局识别、自适应直径滤波和时间集成模型，通过优化未标注数据损失权重提升半监督训练效果。
AI.result: 在NeurIPS 2022细胞分割挑战赛测试集上达到F1-score 0.8239，且所有案例运行时间均在允许范围内。
AI.conclusion: MMCS通过多视角半监督学习和组件协同优化，有效提升了细胞分割的准确率与鲁棒性，为数据稀缺场景提供新思路。

authors_text: Usman Nazir,Murtaza Taj,Momin Uppal,Sara Khalid
title: Mitigating climate and health impact of small-scale kiln industry using multi-spectral classifier and deep learning
title_zh: 使用多光谱分类器和深度学习减轻小型窑炉行业的气候和​​健康影响
comment: Tackling Climate Change with Machine Learning workshop at ICLR 2023
comments: Tackling Climate Change with Machine Learning workshop at ICLR 2023
abstract: Industrial air pollution has a direct health impact and is a major contributor to climate change. Small scale industries particularly bull-trench brick kilns are one of the key sources of air pollution in South Asia often creating hazardous levels of smog that is injurious to human health. To mitigate the climate and health impact of the kiln industry, fine-grained kiln localization at different geographic locations is needed. Kiln localization using multi-spectral remote sensing data such as vegetation indices can result in a noisy estimates whereas relying solely on high-resolution imagery is infeasible due to cost and compute complexities. This paper proposes a fusion of spatio-temporal multi-spectral data with high-resolution imagery for detection of brick kilns within the "Brick-Kiln-Belt" of South Asia. We first perform classification using low-resolution spatio-temporal multi-spectral data from Sentinel-2 imagery by combining vegetation, burn, build up and moisture indices. Next, orientation aware object detector YOLOv3 (with theta value) is implemented for removal of false detections and fine-grained localization. Our proposed technique, when compared with other benchmarks, results in a 21 times improvement in speed with comparable or higher accuracy when tested over multiple countries.
summary: Industrial air pollution has a direct health impact and is a major contributor to climate change. Small scale industries particularly bull-trench brick kilns are one of the key sources of air pollution in South Asia often creating hazardous levels of smog that is injurious to human health. To mitigate the climate and health impact of the kiln industry, fine-grained kiln localization at different geographic locations is needed. Kiln localization using multi-spectral remote sensing data such as vegetation indices can result in a noisy estimates whereas relying solely on high-resolution imagery is infeasible due to cost and compute complexities. This paper proposes a fusion of spatio-temporal multi-spectral data with high-resolution imagery for detection of brick kilns within the "Brick-Kiln-Belt" of South Asia. We first perform classification using low-resolution spatio-temporal multi-spectral data from Sentinel-2 imagery by combining vegetation, burn, build up and moisture indices. Next, orientation aware object detector YOLOv3 (with theta value) is implemented for removal of false detections and fine-grained localization. Our proposed technique, when compared with other benchmarks, results in a 21 times improvement in speed with comparable or higher accuracy when tested over multiple countries.
abstract_zh: 工业空气污染对健康有直接影响，也是气候变化的主要原因。小型工业，特别是牛沟砖窑，是南亚空气污染的主要来源之一，经常产生有害人体健康的危险水平的烟雾。为了减轻窑炉行业对气候和健康的影响，需要在不同地理位置进行细粒度的窑炉定位。使用植被指数等多光谱遥感数据进行窑炉定位可能会导致估计结果存在噪声，而由于成本和计算复杂性，仅依靠高分辨率图像是不可行的。本文提出了时空多光谱数据与高分辨率图像的融合，用于检测南亚“砖窑带”内的砖窑。我们首先通过结合植被、燃烧、堆积和湿度指数，使用来自 Sentinel-2 图像的低分辨率时空多光谱数据进行分类。接下来，实现方向感知对象检测器 YOLOv3（具有 theta 值），以消除错误检测和细粒度定位。与其他基准相比，我们提出的技术在多个国家/地区进行测试时，速度提高了 21 倍，且准确度相当或更高。

AI.tldr: 本文提出结合多光谱数据与高分辨率影像的融合方法，用于南亚砖窑带的小规模砖窑精准定位，以减少其对气候和健康的影响。
AI.motivation: 小规模砖窑是南亚主要空气污染源，现有方法依赖多光谱数据易产生噪声，而高分辨率影像因成本和计算复杂度难以普及，需更高效准确的检测方案。
AI.method: 首先利用Sentinel-2多光谱数据的植被、燃烧、建筑和湿度指数进行分类，再结合带方向感知的YOLOv3检测器（含theta参数）消除误检并实现精细定位。
AI.result: 相比其他基准方法，该技术在多国测试中速度提升21倍，同时保持相当或更高的检测精度。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效平衡了检测速度与精度，为南亚砖窑污染监测提供了可扩展的解决方案，有助于缓解气候与健康问题。

search_text: Mitigating climate and health impact of small-scale kiln industry using multi-spectral classifier and deep learning 使用多光谱分类器和深度学习减轻小型窑炉行业的气候和​​健康影响 Industrial air pollution has a direct health impact and is a major contributor to climate change. Small scale industries particularly bull-trench brick kilns are one of the key sources of air pollution in South Asia often creating hazardous levels of smog that is injurious to human health. To mitigate the climate and health impact of the kiln industry, fine-grained kiln localization at different geographic locations is needed. Kiln localization using multi-spectral remote sensing data such as vegetation indices can result in a noisy estimates whereas relying solely on high-resolution imagery is infeasible due to cost and compute complexities. This paper proposes a fusion of spatio-temporal multi-spectral data with high-resolution imagery for detection of brick kilns within the "Brick-Kiln-Belt" of South Asia. We first perform classification using low-resolution spatio-temporal multi-spectral data from Sentinel-2 imagery by combining vegetation, burn, build up and moisture indices. Next, orientation aware object detector YOLOv3 (with theta value) is implemented for removal of false detections and fine-grained localization. Our proposed technique, when compared with other benchmarks, results in a 21 times improvement in speed with comparable or higher accuracy when tested over multiple countries. 工业空气污染对健康有直接影响，也是气候变化的主要原因。小型工业，特别是牛沟砖窑，是南亚空气污染的主要来源之一，经常产生有害人体健康的危险水平的烟雾。为了减轻窑炉行业对气候和健康的影响，需要在不同地理位置进行细粒度的窑炉定位。使用植被指数等多光谱遥感数据进行窑炉定位可能会导致估计结果存在噪声，而由于成本和计算复杂性，仅依靠高分辨率图像是不可行的。本文提出了时空多光谱数据与高分辨率图像的融合，用于检测南亚“砖窑带”内的砖窑。我们首先通过结合植被、燃烧、堆积和湿度指数，使用来自 Sentinel-2 图像的低分辨率时空多光谱数据进行分类。接下来，实现方向感知对象检测器 YOLOv3（具有 theta 值），以消除错误检测和细粒度定位。与其他基准相比，我们提出的技术在多个国家/地区进行测试时，速度提高了 21 倍，且准确度相当或更高。 本文提出结合多光谱数据与高分辨率影像的融合方法，用于南亚砖窑带的小规模砖窑精准定位，以减少其对气候和健康的影响。 Usman Nazir,Murtaza Taj,Momin Uppal,Sara Khalid cs.CV

authors_text: Yang Zhao,Jianwen Xie,Ping Li
title: CoopInit: Initializing Generative Adversarial Networks via Cooperative Learning
title_zh: CoopInit：通过合作学习初始化生成对抗网络
comment: 9 pages of main text, 2 pages of references
comments: 9 pages of main text, 2 pages of references
abstract: Numerous research efforts have been made to stabilize the training of the Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), such as through regularization and architecture design. However, we identify the instability can also arise from the fragile balance at the early stage of adversarial learning. This paper proposes the CoopInit, a simple yet effective cooperative learning-based initialization strategy that can quickly learn a good starting point for GANs, with a very small computation overhead during training. The proposed algorithm consists of two learning stages: (i) Cooperative initialization stage: The discriminator of GAN is treated as an energy-based model (EBM) and is optimized via maximum likelihood estimation (MLE), with the help of the GAN's generator to provide synthetic data to approximate the learning gradients. The EBM also guides the MLE learning of the generator via MCMC teaching; (ii) Adversarial finalization stage: After a few iterations of initialization, the algorithm seamlessly transits to the regular mini-max adversarial training until convergence. The motivation is that the MLE-based initialization stage drives the model towards mode coverage, which is helpful in alleviating the issue of mode dropping during the adversarial learning stage. We demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach on image generation and one-sided unpaired image-to-image translation tasks through extensive experiments.
summary: Numerous research efforts have been made to stabilize the training of the Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), such as through regularization and architecture design. However, we identify the instability can also arise from the fragile balance at the early stage of adversarial learning. This paper proposes the CoopInit, a simple yet effective cooperative learning-based initialization strategy that can quickly learn a good starting point for GANs, with a very small computation overhead during training. The proposed algorithm consists of two learning stages: (i) Cooperative initialization stage: The discriminator of GAN is treated as an energy-based model (EBM) and is optimized via maximum likelihood estimation (MLE), with the help of the GAN's generator to provide synthetic data to approximate the learning gradients. The EBM also guides the MLE learning of the generator via MCMC teaching; (ii) Adversarial finalization stage: After a few iterations of initialization, the algorithm seamlessly transits to the regular mini-max adversarial training until convergence. The motivation is that the MLE-based initialization stage drives the model towards mode coverage, which is helpful in alleviating the issue of mode dropping during the adversarial learning stage. We demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach on image generation and one-sided unpaired image-to-image translation tasks through extensive experiments.
abstract_zh: 为了稳定生成对抗网络（GAN）的训练，人们已经做出了大量的研究工作，例如通过正则化和架构设计。然而，我们发现，对抗性学习早期阶段的脆弱平衡也可能导致不稳定。本文提出了 CoopInit，一种简单而有效的基于协作学习的初始化策略，可以快速学习 GAN 的良好起点，并且在训练期间的计算开销非常小。该算法由两个学习阶段组成：（i）合作初始化阶段：GAN 的判别器被视为基于能量的模型（EBM），并通过最大似然估计（MLE）进行优化，借助 GAN 的生成器提供合成数据来近似学习梯度。 EBM还通过MCMC教学指导生成器的MLE学习； (ii) 对抗最终阶段：经过几次初始化迭代后，算法无缝过渡到常规的最小-最大对抗训练，直到收敛。其动机是基于 MLE 的初始化阶段将模型推向模式覆盖，这有助于缓解对抗性学习阶段的模式丢失问题。我们通过大量的实验证明了所提出的方法在图像生成和单侧不配对图像到图像翻译任务方面的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出CoopInit方法，通过合作学习策略初始化生成对抗网络（GAN），以解决训练初期的不稳定性问题。
AI.motivation: 传统GAN训练不稳定主要源于对抗学习初期的脆弱平衡，而现有方法（如正则化、架构设计）未能有效解决此问题。通过合作学习初始化可提升模式覆盖，缓解模式崩溃。
AI.method: 分两阶段：1) 合作初始化阶段：将判别器视为能量模型，通过最大似然估计优化，并利用生成器生成数据近似梯度；2) 对抗最终阶段：初始化后转为常规对抗训练直至收敛。
AI.result: 在图像生成和单边非配对图像翻译任务中，CoopInit显著提升生成质量，实验验证其有效性。
AI.conclusion: CoopInit通过合作学习有效稳定GAN训练过程，提升生成效果且计算开销低。

search_text: CoopInit: Initializing Generative Adversarial Networks via Cooperative Learning CoopInit：通过合作学习初始化生成对抗网络 Numerous research efforts have been made to stabilize the training of the Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), such as through regularization and architecture design. However, we identify the instability can also arise from the fragile balance at the early stage of adversarial learning. This paper proposes the CoopInit, a simple yet effective cooperative learning-based initialization strategy that can quickly learn a good starting point for GANs, with a very small computation overhead during training. The proposed algorithm consists of two learning stages: (i) Cooperative initialization stage: The discriminator of GAN is treated as an energy-based model (EBM) and is optimized via maximum likelihood estimation (MLE), with the help of the GAN's generator to provide synthetic data to approximate the learning gradients. The EBM also guides the MLE learning of the generator via MCMC teaching; (ii) Adversarial finalization stage: After a few iterations of initialization, the algorithm seamlessly transits to the regular mini-max adversarial training until convergence. The motivation is that the MLE-based initialization stage drives the model towards mode coverage, which is helpful in alleviating the issue of mode dropping during the adversarial learning stage. We demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach on image generation and one-sided unpaired image-to-image translation tasks through extensive experiments. 为了稳定生成对抗网络（GAN）的训练，人们已经做出了大量的研究工作，例如通过正则化和架构设计。然而，我们发现，对抗性学习早期阶段的脆弱平衡也可能导致不稳定。本文提出了 CoopInit，一种简单而有效的基于协作学习的初始化策略，可以快速学习 GAN 的良好起点，并且在训练期间的计算开销非常小。该算法由两个学习阶段组成：（i）合作初始化阶段：GAN 的判别器被视为基于能量的模型（EBM），并通过最大似然估计（MLE）进行优化，借助 GAN 的生成器提供合成数据来近似学习梯度。 EBM还通过MCMC教学指导生成器的MLE学习； (ii) 对抗最终阶段：经过几次初始化迭代后，算法无缝过渡到常规的最小-最大对抗训练，直到收敛。其动机是基于 MLE 的初始化阶段将模型推向模式覆盖，这有助于缓解对抗性学习阶段的模式丢失问题。我们通过大量的实验证明了所提出的方法在图像生成和单侧不配对图像到图像翻译任务方面的有效性。 本文提出CoopInit方法，通过合作学习策略初始化生成对抗网络（GAN），以解决训练初期的不稳定性问题。 Yang Zhao,Jianwen Xie,Ping Li cs.CV,eess.IV

authors_text: Yulong Tian,Fnu Suya,Anshuman Suri,Fengyuan Xu,David Evans
title: Manipulating Transfer Learning for Property Inference
title_zh: 操纵迁移学习进行属性推理
abstract: Transfer learning is a popular method for tuning pretrained (upstream) models for different downstream tasks using limited data and computational resources. We study how an adversary with control over an upstream model used in transfer learning can conduct property inference attacks on a victim's tuned downstream model. For example, to infer the presence of images of a specific individual in the downstream training set. We demonstrate attacks in which an adversary can manipulate the upstream model to conduct highly effective and specific property inference attacks (AUC score $> 0.9$), without incurring significant performance loss on the main task. The main idea of the manipulation is to make the upstream model generate activations (intermediate features) with different distributions for samples with and without a target property, thus enabling the adversary to distinguish easily between downstream models trained with and without training examples that have the target property. Our code is available at https://github.com/yulongt23/Transfer-Inference.
summary: Transfer learning is a popular method for tuning pretrained (upstream) models for different downstream tasks using limited data and computational resources. We study how an adversary with control over an upstream model used in transfer learning can conduct property inference attacks on a victim's tuned downstream model. For example, to infer the presence of images of a specific individual in the downstream training set. We demonstrate attacks in which an adversary can manipulate the upstream model to conduct highly effective and specific property inference attacks (AUC score $> 0.9$), without incurring significant performance loss on the main task. The main idea of the manipulation is to make the upstream model generate activations (intermediate features) with different distributions for samples with and without a target property, thus enabling the adversary to distinguish easily between downstream models trained with and without training examples that have the target property. Our code is available at https://github.com/yulongt23/Transfer-Inference.
abstract_zh: 迁移学习是一种流行的方法，使用有限的数据和计算资源针对不同的下游任务调整预训练（上游）模型。我们研究控制迁移学习中使用的上游模型的对手如何对受害者调整的下游模型进行属性推理攻击。例如，推断下游训练集中特定个体​​的图像的存在。我们演示了攻击者可以操纵上游模型来进行高效且特定的属性推断攻击（AUC 得分 > 0.9$），而不会在主要任务上造成显着的性能损失。该操作的主要思想是使上游模型为具有和不具有目标属性的样本生成不同分布的激活（中间特征），从而使攻击者能够轻松地区分使用和不使用具有目标属性的训练样本训练的下游模型。我们的代码可在 https://github.com/yulongt23/Transfer-Inference 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文研究如何通过操控预训练模型（上游模型）在迁移学习过程中对下游模型进行属性推断攻击，以推断下游训练数据中是否存在特定属性。
AI.motivation: 迁移学习广泛应用于资源受限场景，但现有研究未充分关注上游模型被恶意操控后可能引发的隐私泄露风险，需揭示此类攻击的可行性与危害性。
AI.method: 通过修改上游模型的激活分布，使包含目标属性的样本与不包含样本在中间特征层产生显著差异，从而让攻击者通过分析下游模型区分训练数据是否包含目标属性。
AI.result: 实验表明攻击可实现AUC得分超过0.9的高精度属性推断，且对主任务性能影响极小。
AI.conclusion: 迁移学习框架存在潜在安全漏洞，需在模型设计阶段考虑对抗性攻击防御机制。

ai_content_json.method: 通过修改上游模型的激活分布，使包含目标属性的样本与不包含样本在中间特征层产生显著差异，从而让攻击者通过分析下游模型区分训练数据是否包含目标属性。
ai_content_json.result: 实验表明攻击可实现AUC得分超过0.9的高精度属性推断，且对主任务性能影响极小。
ai_content_json.conclusion: 迁移学习框架存在潜在安全漏洞，需在模型设计阶段考虑对抗性攻击防御机制。

tldr: 本文研究如何通过操控预训练模型（上游模型）在迁移学习过程中对下游模型进行属性推断攻击，以推断下游训练数据中是否存在特定属性。
motivation: 迁移学习广泛应用于资源受限场景，但现有研究未充分关注上游模型被恶意操控后可能引发的隐私泄露风险，需揭示此类攻击的可行性与危害性。
method: 通过修改上游模型的激活分布，使包含目标属性的样本与不包含样本在中间特征层产生显著差异，从而让攻击者通过分析下游模型区分训练数据是否包含目标属性。
result: 实验表明攻击可实现AUC得分超过0.9的高精度属性推断，且对主任务性能影响极小。
conclusion: 迁移学习框架存在潜在安全漏洞，需在模型设计阶段考虑对抗性攻击防御机制。
search_text: Manipulating Transfer Learning for Property Inference 操纵迁移学习进行属性推理 Transfer learning is a popular method for tuning pretrained (upstream) models for different downstream tasks using limited data and computational resources. We study how an adversary with control over an upstream model used in transfer learning can conduct property inference attacks on a victim's tuned downstream model. For example, to infer the presence of images of a specific individual in the downstream training set. We demonstrate attacks in which an adversary can manipulate the upstream model to conduct highly effective and specific property inference attacks (AUC score $> 0.9$), without incurring significant performance loss on the main task. The main idea of the manipulation is to make the upstream model generate activations (intermediate features) with different distributions for samples with and without a target property, thus enabling the adversary to distinguish easily between downstream models trained with and without training examples that have the target property. Our code is available at https://github.com/yulongt23/Transfer-Inference. 迁移学习是一种流行的方法，使用有限的数据和计算资源针对不同的下游任务调整预训练（上游）模型。我们研究控制迁移学习中使用的上游模型的对手如何对受害者调整的下游模型进行属性推理攻击。例如，推断下游训练集中特定个体​​的图像的存在。我们演示了攻击者可以操纵上游模型来进行高效且特定的属性推断攻击（AUC 得分 > 0.9$），而不会在主要任务上造成显着的性能损失。该操作的主要思想是使上游模型为具有和不具有目标属性的样本生成不同分布的激活（中间特征），从而使攻击者能够轻松地区分使用和不使用具有目标属性的训练样本训练的下游模型。我们的代码可在 https://github.com/yulongt23/Transfer-Inference 获取。 本文研究如何通过操控预训练模型（上游模型）在迁移学习过程中对下游模型进行属性推断攻击，以推断下游训练数据中是否存在特定属性。 Yulong Tian,Fnu Suya,Anshuman Suri,Fengyuan Xu,David Evans cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.CR

authors_text: Muyao Niu,Zhuoxiao Li,Zhihang Zhong,Yinqiang Zheng
title: Visibility Constrained Wide-band Illumination Spectrum Design for Seeing-in-the-Dark
title_zh: 用于黑暗中视物的可见度约束宽带照明光谱设计
abstract: Seeing-in-the-dark is one of the most important and challenging computer vision tasks due to its wide applications and extreme complexities of in-the-wild scenarios. Existing arts can be mainly divided into two threads: 1) RGB-dependent methods restore information using degraded RGB inputs only (\eg, low-light enhancement), 2) RGB-independent methods translate images captured under auxiliary near-infrared (NIR) illuminants into RGB domain (\eg, NIR2RGB translation). The latter is very attractive since it works in complete darkness and the illuminants are visually friendly to naked eyes, but tends to be unstable due to its intrinsic ambiguities. In this paper, we try to robustify NIR2RGB translation by designing the optimal spectrum of auxiliary illumination in the wide-band VIS-NIR range, while keeping visual friendliness. Our core idea is to quantify the visibility constraint implied by the human vision system and incorporate it into the design pipeline. By modeling the formation process of images in the VIS-NIR range, the optimal multiplexing of a wide range of LEDs is automatically designed in a fully differentiable manner, within the feasible region defined by the visibility constraint. We also collect a substantially expanded VIS-NIR hyperspectral image dataset for experiments by using a customized 50-band filter wheel. Experimental results show that the task can be significantly improved by using the optimized wide-band illumination than using NIR only. Codes Available: https://github.com/MyNiuuu/VCSD.
summary: Seeing-in-the-dark is one of the most important and challenging computer vision tasks due to its wide applications and extreme complexities of in-the-wild scenarios. Existing arts can be mainly divided into two threads: 1) RGB-dependent methods restore information using degraded RGB inputs only (\eg, low-light enhancement), 2) RGB-independent methods translate images captured under auxiliary near-infrared (NIR) illuminants into RGB domain (\eg, NIR2RGB translation). The latter is very attractive since it works in complete darkness and the illuminants are visually friendly to naked eyes, but tends to be unstable due to its intrinsic ambiguities. In this paper, we try to robustify NIR2RGB translation by designing the optimal spectrum of auxiliary illumination in the wide-band VIS-NIR range, while keeping visual friendliness. Our core idea is to quantify the visibility constraint implied by the human vision system and incorporate it into the design pipeline. By modeling the formation process of images in the VIS-NIR range, the optimal multiplexing of a wide range of LEDs is automatically designed in a fully differentiable manner, within the feasible region defined by the visibility constraint. We also collect a substantially expanded VIS-NIR hyperspectral image dataset for experiments by using a customized 50-band filter wheel. Experimental results show that the task can be significantly improved by using the optimized wide-band illumination than using NIR only. Codes Available: https://github.com/MyNiuuu/VCSD.
abstract_zh: 由于其广泛的应用和野外场景的极端复杂性，黑暗中的视觉是最重要和最具挑战性的计算机视觉任务之一。现有技术可主要分为两个线程：1）依赖于 RGB 的方法仅使用降级的 RGB 输入来恢复信息（例如，低光增强），2）独立于 RGB 的方法将在辅助近红外（NIR）光源下捕获的图像转换为 RGB 域（例如，NIR2RGB 转换）。后者非常有吸引力，因为它可以在完全黑暗的环境中工作，并且光源对肉眼来说在视觉上是友好的，但由于其固有的模糊性而往往不稳定。在本文中，我们尝试通过设计宽带 VIS-NIR 范围内的辅助照明的最佳光谱来增强 NIR2RGB 转换，同时保持视觉友好性。我们的核心思想是量化人类视觉系统隐含的可见性约束，并将其纳入设计流程中。通过对 VIS-NIR 范围内的图像形成过程进行建模，在可见性约束定义的可行区域内，以完全可微分的方式自动设计各种 LED 的最佳复用。我们还通过使用定制的 50 波段滤光轮收集了大幅扩展的 VIS-NIR 高光谱图像数据集用于实验。实验结果表明，与仅使用 NIR 相比，使用优化的宽带照明可以显着改善任务。可用代码：https://github.com/MyNiuuu/VCSD。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种在可见光-近红外（VIS-NIR）宽波段范围内设计辅助照明光谱的方法，以提升低光环境下的视觉感知能力。
AI.motivation: 现有基于近红外（NIR）的NIR2RGB图像转换方法在完全黑暗环境中不稳定，需通过优化照明光谱提升鲁棒性同时保持视觉友好性。
AI.method: 量化人类视觉系统可见性约束，建模VIS-NIR成像过程，通过可微分优化自动设计LED光谱组合，并构建50波段滤光轮采集的高光谱数据集验证方法。
AI.result: 实验表明，优化后的宽波段照明相比单一NIR显著提升了低光场景的图像重建质量与稳定性。
AI.conclusion: 通过融合人类视觉约束的宽波段照明设计，可有效增强NIR2RGB转换的鲁棒性，为低光视觉任务提供新思路。

authors_text: Zigang Geng,Chunyu Wang,Yixuan Wei,Ze Liu,Houqiang Li,Han Hu
title: Human Pose as Compositional Tokens
title_zh: 作为构图标记的人体姿势
abstract: Human pose is typically represented by a coordinate vector of body joints or their heatmap embeddings. While easy for data processing, unrealistic pose estimates are admitted due to the lack of dependency modeling between the body joints. In this paper, we present a structured representation, named Pose as Compositional Tokens (PCT), to explore the joint dependency. It represents a pose by M discrete tokens with each characterizing a sub-structure with several interdependent joints. The compositional design enables it to achieve a small reconstruction error at a low cost. Then we cast pose estimation as a classification task. In particular, we learn a classifier to predict the categories of the M tokens from an image. A pre-learned decoder network is used to recover the pose from the tokens without further post-processing. We show that it achieves better or comparable pose estimation results as the existing methods in general scenarios, yet continues to work well when occlusion occurs, which is ubiquitous in practice. The code and models are publicly available at https://github.com/Gengzigang/PCT.
summary: Human pose is typically represented by a coordinate vector of body joints or their heatmap embeddings. While easy for data processing, unrealistic pose estimates are admitted due to the lack of dependency modeling between the body joints. In this paper, we present a structured representation, named Pose as Compositional Tokens (PCT), to explore the joint dependency. It represents a pose by M discrete tokens with each characterizing a sub-structure with several interdependent joints. The compositional design enables it to achieve a small reconstruction error at a low cost. Then we cast pose estimation as a classification task. In particular, we learn a classifier to predict the categories of the M tokens from an image. A pre-learned decoder network is used to recover the pose from the tokens without further post-processing. We show that it achieves better or comparable pose estimation results as the existing methods in general scenarios, yet continues to work well when occlusion occurs, which is ubiquitous in practice. The code and models are publicly available at https://github.com/Gengzigang/PCT.
abstract_zh: 人体姿势通常由身体关节的坐标向量或其热图嵌入表示。虽然数据处理很容易，但由于身体关节之间缺乏依赖性建模，不切实际的姿势估计被承认。在本文中，我们提出了一种结构化表示，称为姿势组合令牌（PCT），以探索联合依赖性。它由 M 个离散标记表示一个姿势，每个标记都表征具有多个相互依赖的关节的子结构。组合设计使其能够以较低的成本实现较小的重建误差。然后我们将姿态估计作为分类任务。特别是，我们学习一个分类器来预测图像中 M 个标记的类别。使用预先学习的解码器网络从令牌中恢复姿势，而无需进一步的后处理。我们表明，它在一般场景下取得了与现有方法更好或相当的姿态估计结果，但在发生遮挡时仍然表现良好，这在实践中是普遍存在的。代码和模型可在 https://github.com/Gengzigang/PCT 上公开获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为Pose as Compositional Tokens（PCT）的结构化表示方法，用于改进人体姿态估计任务。
AI.motivation: 传统方法通过坐标向量或热图嵌入表示人体姿态，但因未建模关节间的依赖关系，导致估计结果不真实，尤其在遮挡场景下表现不佳。
AI.method: 将姿态分解为M个离散的结构化token，每个token表征多个关联关节。通过分类任务预测token类别，并利用预训练解码器直接恢复姿态，无需后处理。
AI.result: 在常规场景和遮挡场景下均取得优于或媲美现有方法的姿态估计效果，代码和模型已开源。
AI.conclusion: PCT通过结构化token建模关节依赖关系，为鲁棒的姿态估计提供了新思路，尤其适用于复杂遮挡场景。

search_text: Human Pose as Compositional Tokens 作为构图标记的人体姿势 Human pose is typically represented by a coordinate vector of body joints or their heatmap embeddings. While easy for data processing, unrealistic pose estimates are admitted due to the lack of dependency modeling between the body joints. In this paper, we present a structured representation, named Pose as Compositional Tokens (PCT), to explore the joint dependency. It represents a pose by M discrete tokens with each characterizing a sub-structure with several interdependent joints. The compositional design enables it to achieve a small reconstruction error at a low cost. Then we cast pose estimation as a classification task. In particular, we learn a classifier to predict the categories of the M tokens from an image. A pre-learned decoder network is used to recover the pose from the tokens without further post-processing. We show that it achieves better or comparable pose estimation results as the existing methods in general scenarios, yet continues to work well when occlusion occurs, which is ubiquitous in practice. The code and models are publicly available at https://github.com/Gengzigang/PCT. 人体姿势通常由身体关节的坐标向量或其热图嵌入表示。虽然数据处理很容易，但由于身体关节之间缺乏依赖性建模，不切实际的姿势估计被承认。在本文中，我们提出了一种结构化表示，称为姿势组合令牌（PCT），以探索联合依赖性。它由 M 个离散标记表示一个姿势，每个标记都表征具有多个相互依赖的关节的子结构。组合设计使其能够以较低的成本实现较小的重建误差。然后我们将姿态估计作为分类任务。特别是，我们学习一个分类器来预测图像中 M 个标记的类别。使用预先学习的解码器网络从令牌中恢复姿势，而无需进一步的后处理。我们表明，它在一般场景下取得了与现有方法更好或相当的姿态估计结果，但在发生遮挡时仍然表现良好，这在实践中是普遍存在的。代码和模型可在 https://github.com/Gengzigang/PCT 上公开获取。 本文提出了一种名为Pose as Compositional Tokens（PCT）的结构化表示方法，用于改进人体姿态估计任务。 Zigang Geng,Chunyu Wang,Yixuan Wei,Ze Liu,Houqiang Li,Han Hu cs.CV

authors_text: Yang Shen,Xuhao Sun,Xiu-Shen Wei
title: Equiangular Basis Vectors
title_zh: 等角基向量
abstract: We propose Equiangular Basis Vectors (EBVs) for classification tasks. In deep neural networks, models usually end with a k-way fully connected layer with softmax to handle different classification tasks. The learning objective of these methods can be summarized as mapping the learned feature representations to the samples' label space. While in metric learning approaches, the main objective is to learn a transformation function that maps training data points from the original space to a new space where similar points are closer while dissimilar points become farther apart. Different from previous methods, our EBVs generate normalized vector embeddings as "predefined classifiers" which are required to not only be with the equal status between each other, but also be as orthogonal as possible. By minimizing the spherical distance of the embedding of an input between its categorical EBV in training, the predictions can be obtained by identifying the categorical EBV with the smallest distance during inference. Various experiments on the ImageNet-1K dataset and other downstream tasks demonstrate that our method outperforms the general fully connected classifier while it does not introduce huge additional computation compared with classical metric learning methods. Our EBVs won the first place in the 2022 DIGIX Global AI Challenge, and our code is open-source and available at https://github.com/NJUST-VIPGroup/Equiangular-Basis-Vectors.
summary: We propose Equiangular Basis Vectors (EBVs) for classification tasks. In deep neural networks, models usually end with a k-way fully connected layer with softmax to handle different classification tasks. The learning objective of these methods can be summarized as mapping the learned feature representations to the samples' label space. While in metric learning approaches, the main objective is to learn a transformation function that maps training data points from the original space to a new space where similar points are closer while dissimilar points become farther apart. Different from previous methods, our EBVs generate normalized vector embeddings as "predefined classifiers" which are required to not only be with the equal status between each other, but also be as orthogonal as possible. By minimizing the spherical distance of the embedding of an input between its categorical EBV in training, the predictions can be obtained by identifying the categorical EBV with the smallest distance during inference. Various experiments on the ImageNet-1K dataset and other downstream tasks demonstrate that our method outperforms the general fully connected classifier while it does not introduce huge additional computation compared with classical metric learning methods. Our EBVs won the first place in the 2022 DIGIX Global AI Challenge, and our code is open-source and available at https://github.com/NJUST-VIPGroup/Equiangular-Basis-Vectors.
abstract_zh: 我们提出用于分类任务的等角基向量（EBV）。在深度神经网络中，模型通常以带有 softmax 的 k 路全连接层结尾，以处理不同的分类任务。这些方法的学习目标可以概括为将学习到的特征表示映射到样本的标签空间。在度量学习方法中，主要目标是学习一个变换函数，将训练数据点从原始空间映射到新空间，其中相似点更接近，而不相似点变得更远。与以前的方法不同，我们的 EBV 生成归一化向量嵌入作为“预定义分类器”，这些分类器不仅要求彼此之间具有平等的地位，而且还尽可能正交。通过在训练中最小化输入在其分类 EBV 之间嵌入的球形距离，可以通过在推理过程中识别具有最小距离的分类 EBV 来获得预测。在 ImageNet-1K 数据集和其他下游任务上的各种实验表明，我们的方法优于一般的全连接分类器，同时与经典的度量学习方法相比，它没有引入大量的额外计算。我们的 EBV 在 2022 年 DIGIX 全球人工智能挑战赛中获得第一名，我们的代码是开源的，可在 https://github.com/NJUST-VIPGroup/Equiangular-Basis-Vectors 上获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为等角基向量（EBVs）的新方法，用于改进深度神经网络中的分类任务。
AI.motivation: 传统全连接分类器和度量学习方法在计算效率或分类性能上存在不足，本文旨在设计一种既保持向量等角性又尽可能正交的预定义分类器，以提升分类效果并降低计算开销。
AI.method: 通过生成归一化的等角基向量作为预定义分类器，在训练阶段最小化输入嵌入与对应类别EBV之间的球面距离，推理时通过寻找最近邻EBV实现预测。
AI.result: 在ImageNet-1K等数据集上优于传统全连接分类器，计算开销接近经典度量学习方法，并在2022年DIGIX全球AI挑战赛中夺冠。
AI.conclusion: EBVs为分类任务提供了高效且性能优越的替代方案，其开源实现验证了方法的可行性与广泛适用性。

In deep neural networks, models usually end with a k-way fully connected layer with softmax to handle different classification tasks. authors_text: Max Peter Ronecker,Yuan Zhu
title: Deep Q-Network Based Decision Making for Autonomous Driving
title_zh: 基于深度 Q 网络的自动驾驶决策
comment: Accepted at 2019 International Conference on Robotics and Automation Sciences (ICRAS)
comments: Accepted at 2019 International Conference on Robotics and Automation Sciences (ICRAS)
abstract: Currently decision making is one of the biggest challenges in autonomous driving. This paper introduces a method for safely navigating an autonomous vehicle in highway scenarios by combining deep Q-Networks and insight from control theory. A Deep Q-Network is trained in simulation to serve as a central decision-making unit by proposing targets for a trajectory planner. The generated trajectories in combination with a controller for longitudinal movement are used to execute lane change maneuvers. In order to prove the functionality of this approach it is evaluated on two different highway traffic scenarios. Furthermore, the impact of different state representations on the performance and training process is analyzed. The results show that the proposed system can produce efficient and safe driving behavior.
summary: Currently decision making is one of the biggest challenges in autonomous driving. This paper introduces a method for safely navigating an autonomous vehicle in highway scenarios by combining deep Q-Networks and insight from control theory. A Deep Q-Network is trained in simulation to serve as a central decision-making unit by proposing targets for a trajectory planner. The generated trajectories in combination with a controller for longitudinal movement are used to execute lane change maneuvers. In order to prove the functionality of this approach it is evaluated on two different highway traffic scenarios. Furthermore, the impact of different state representations on the performance and training process is analyzed. The results show that the proposed system can produce efficient and safe driving behavior.
abstract_zh: 目前决策是自动驾驶面临的最大挑战之一。本文介绍了一种结合深度 Q 网络和控制理论的见解，在高速公路场景中安全驾驶自动驾驶车辆的方法。深度 Q 网络经过模拟训练，通过为轨迹规划器提出目标来充当中央决策单元。生成的轨迹与纵向运动控制器相结合，用于执行变道操作。为了证明该方法的功能，在两种不同的高速公路交通场景下对其进行了评估。此外，还分析了不同状态表示对性能和训练过程的影响。结果表明，所提出的系统可以产生高效、安全的驾驶行为。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合深度Q网络与控制理论的自动驾驶决策方法，用于高速公路场景中的安全导航。
AI.motivation: 自动驾驶中的决策制定是核心挑战，现有方法在复杂交通场景中存在安全性与效率不足的问题。
AI.method: 通过模拟训练深度Q网络作为决策中枢，结合轨迹规划器与纵向控制控制器实现变道操作，并分析不同状态表示的影响。
AI.result: 系统在两种高速公路场景中展现出高效且安全的驾驶行为，状态表示显著影响训练效果与性能表现。
AI.conclusion: 该方法验证了深度强化学习与控制理论结合在自动驾驶决策中的有效性，为未来研究提供新思路。

authors_text: Zhuotao Tian,Jiequan Cui,Li Jiang,Xiaojuan Qi,Xin Lai,Yixin Chen,Shu Liu,Jiaya Jia
title: Learning Context-aware Classifier for Semantic Segmentation
title_zh: 学习用于语义分割的上下文感知分类器
comment: AAAI 2023. Code and models are available at https://github.com/tianzhuotao/CAC
comments: AAAI 2023. Code and models are available at https://github.com/tianzhuotao/CAC
abstract: Semantic segmentation is still a challenging task for parsing diverse contexts in different scenes, thus the fixed classifier might not be able to well address varying feature distributions during testing. Different from the mainstream literature where the efficacy of strong backbones and effective decoder heads has been well studied, in this paper, additional contextual hints are instead exploited via learning a context-aware classifier whose content is data-conditioned, decently adapting to different latent distributions. Since only the classifier is dynamically altered, our method is model-agnostic and can be easily applied to generic segmentation models. Notably, with only negligible additional parameters and +2\% inference time, decent performance gain has been achieved on both small and large models with challenging benchmarks, manifesting substantial practical merits brought by our simple yet effective method. The implementation is available at \url{https://github.com/tianzhuotao/CAC}.
summary: Semantic segmentation is still a challenging task for parsing diverse contexts in different scenes, thus the fixed classifier might not be able to well address varying feature distributions during testing. Different from the mainstream literature where the efficacy of strong backbones and effective decoder heads has been well studied, in this paper, additional contextual hints are instead exploited via learning a context-aware classifier whose content is data-conditioned, decently adapting to different latent distributions. Since only the classifier is dynamically altered, our method is model-agnostic and can be easily applied to generic segmentation models. Notably, with only negligible additional parameters and +2\% inference time, decent performance gain has been achieved on both small and large models with challenging benchmarks, manifesting substantial practical merits brought by our simple yet effective method. The implementation is available at \url{https://github.com/tianzhuotao/CAC}.
abstract_zh: 语义分割对于解析不同场景中的不同上下文仍然是一项具有挑战性的任务，因此固定分类器可能无法很好地解决测试过程中变化的特征分布。与主流文献不同，主流文献充分研究了强大的主干和有效的解码器头的功效，在本文中，通过学习上下文感知分类器来利用额外的上下文提示，该分类器的内容是数据调节的，可以很好地适应不同的潜在分布。由于仅动态改变分类器，因此我们的方法与模型无关，并且可以轻松应用于通用分割模型。值得注意的是，仅用可忽略的附加参数和+2%的推理时间，在具有挑战性基准的小型和大型模型上都取得了不错的性能增益，体现了我们简单而有效的方法带来的巨大实际优点。该实现可在 \url{https://github.com/tianzhuotao/CAC} 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种上下文感知分类器，用于解决语义分割中因场景多样性导致的特征分布变化问题。
AI.motivation: 传统固定分类器难以适应测试时变化的特征分布，而现有研究过度依赖主干网络和解码器优化，忽视了分类器的动态调整潜力。
AI.method: 设计数据条件化的上下文感知分类器，通过动态调整分类器参数适应不同潜在分布，保持模型无关性且仅增加少量参数与计算开销。
AI.result: 在多个基准测试中，该方法在小型和大型模型上均实现性能提升，参数增加可忽略，推理时间仅增加2%。
AI.conclusion: 提出的简单有效方法显著提升分割性能，具有广泛适用性，为模型优化提供新思路。

search_text: Learning Context-aware Classifier for Semantic Segmentation 学习用于语义分割的上下文感知分类器 Semantic segmentation is still a challenging task for parsing diverse contexts in different scenes, thus the fixed classifier might not be able to well address varying feature distributions during testing. Different from the mainstream literature where the efficacy of strong backbones and effective decoder heads has been well studied, in this paper, additional contextual hints are instead exploited via learning a context-aware classifier whose content is data-conditioned, decently adapting to different latent distributions. Since only the classifier is dynamically altered, our method is model-agnostic and can be easily applied to generic segmentation models. Notably, with only negligible additional parameters and +2\% inference time, decent performance gain has been achieved on both small and large models with challenging benchmarks, manifesting substantial practical merits brought by our simple yet effective method. The implementation is available at \url{https://github.com/tianzhuotao/CAC}. 语义分割对于解析不同场景中的不同上下文仍然是一项具有挑战性的任务，因此固定分类器可能无法很好地解决测试过程中变化的特征分布。与主流文献不同，主流文献充分研究了强大的主干和有效的解码器头的功效，在本文中，通过学习上下文感知分类器来利用额外的上下文提示，该分类器的内容是数据调节的，可以很好地适应不同的潜在分布。由于仅动态改变分类器，因此我们的方法与模型无关，并且可以轻松应用于通用分割模型。值得注意的是，仅用可忽略的附加参数和+2%的推理时间，在具有挑战性基准的小型和大型模型上都取得了不错的性能增益，体现了我们简单而有效的方法带来的巨大实际优点。该实现可在 \url{https://github.com/tianzhuotao/CAC} 获取。 本文提出一种上下文感知分类器，用于解决语义分割中因场景多样性导致的特征分布变化问题。 Zhuotao Tian,Jiequan Cui,Li Jiang,Xiaojuan Qi,Xin Lai,Yixin Chen,Shu Liu,Jiaya Jia cs.CV

authors_text: Nitish Shukla
title: An Embarrassingly Simple Approach for Wafer Feature Extraction and Defect Pattern Recognition
title_zh: 晶圆特征提取和缺陷模式识别的极其简单的方法
abstract: Identifying defect patterns in a wafer map during manufacturing is crucial to find the root cause of the underlying issue and provides valuable insights on improving yield in the foundry. Currently used methods use deep neural networks to identify the defects. These methods are generally very huge and have significant inference time. They also require GPU support to efficiently operate. All these issues make these models not fit for on-line prediction in the manufacturing foundry. In this paper, we propose an extremely simple yet effective technique to extract features from wafer images. The proposed method is extremely fast, intuitive, and non-parametric while being explainable. The experiment results show that the proposed pipeline outperforms conventional deep learning models. Our feature extraction requires no training or fine-tuning while preserving the relative shape and location of data points as revealed by our interpretability analysis.
summary: Identifying defect patterns in a wafer map during manufacturing is crucial to find the root cause of the underlying issue and provides valuable insights on improving yield in the foundry. Currently used methods use deep neural networks to identify the defects. These methods are generally very huge and have significant inference time. They also require GPU support to efficiently operate. All these issues make these models not fit for on-line prediction in the manufacturing foundry. In this paper, we propose an extremely simple yet effective technique to extract features from wafer images. The proposed method is extremely fast, intuitive, and non-parametric while being explainable. The experiment results show that the proposed pipeline outperforms conventional deep learning models. Our feature extraction requires no training or fine-tuning while preserving the relative shape and location of data points as revealed by our interpretability analysis.
abstract_zh: 在制造过程中识别晶圆图中的缺陷模式对于找到根本问题的根本原因至关重要，并为提高铸造厂的产量提供了宝贵的见解。目前使用的方法使用深度神经网络来识别缺陷。这些方法通常非常庞大并且具有大量的推理时间。它们还需要 GPU 支持才能高效运行。所有这些问题使得这些模型不适合制造铸造厂的在线预测。在本文中，我们提出了一种极其简单但有效的技术来从晶圆图像中提取特征。所提出的方法非常快速、直观、非参数，同时易于解释。实验结果表明，所提出的管道优于传统的深度学习模型。我们的特征提取不需要训练或微调，同时保留我们的可解释性分析所揭示的数据点的相对形状和位置。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种简单有效的晶圆特征提取与缺陷模式识别方法，解决现有深度学习方法在制造场景中的不足。
AI.motivation: 现有深度学习方法模型庞大、推理时间长且依赖GPU，难以满足制造现场在线预测需求，需更高效可解释的解决方案。
AI.method: 采用无需训练的非参数方法提取晶圆特征，通过保留数据点相对形状与位置实现缺陷模式识别。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法在性能上超越传统深度学习模型，且具备快速推理与可解释性优势。
AI.conclusion: 提出的简单方法为晶圆缺陷检测提供高效替代方案，可直接应用于制造场景提升良率分析效率。

authors_text: Rui Yang,Lin Song,Yixiao Ge,Xiu Li
title: BoxSnake: Polygonal Instance Segmentation with Box Supervision
title_zh: BoxSnake：具有框监督的多边形实例分割
abstract: Box-supervised instance segmentation has gained much attention as it requires only simple box annotations instead of costly mask or polygon annotations. However, existing box-supervised instance segmentation models mainly focus on mask-based frameworks. We propose a new end-to-end training technique, termed BoxSnake, to achieve effective polygonal instance segmentation using only box annotations for the first time. Our method consists of two loss functions: (1) a point-based unary loss that constrains the bounding box of predicted polygons to achieve coarse-grained segmentation; and (2) a distance-aware pairwise loss that encourages the predicted polygons to fit the object boundaries. Compared with the mask-based weakly-supervised methods, BoxSnake further reduces the performance gap between the predicted segmentation and the bounding box, and shows significant superiority on the Cityscapes dataset. The code has been available publicly.
summary: Box-supervised instance segmentation has gained much attention as it requires only simple box annotations instead of costly mask or polygon annotations. However, existing box-supervised instance segmentation models mainly focus on mask-based frameworks. We propose a new end-to-end training technique, termed BoxSnake, to achieve effective polygonal instance segmentation using only box annotations for the first time. Our method consists of two loss functions: (1) a point-based unary loss that constrains the bounding box of predicted polygons to achieve coarse-grained segmentation; and (2) a distance-aware pairwise loss that encourages the predicted polygons to fit the object boundaries. Compared with the mask-based weakly-supervised methods, BoxSnake further reduces the performance gap between the predicted segmentation and the bounding box, and shows significant superiority on the Cityscapes dataset. The code has been available publicly.
abstract_zh: 框监督实例分割受到了广泛关注，因为它只需要简单的框注释，而不是昂贵的掩模或多边形注释。然而，现有的盒监督实例分割模型主要集中在基于掩模的框架上。我们提出了一种新的端到端训练技术，称为 BoxSnake，首次仅使用框注释来实现有效的多边形实例分割。我们的方法由两个损失函数组成：（1）基于点的一元损失，它约束预测多边形的边界框以实现粗粒度分割； (2) 距离感知的成对损失，鼓励预测的多边形适合对象边界。与基于掩模的弱监督方法相比，BoxSnake 进一步缩小了预测分割与边界框之间的性能差距，并在 Cityscapes 数据集上显示出显着的优越性。该代码已公开。

AI.tldr: 本文提出BoxSnake方法，首次仅使用框标注实现多边形实例分割。
AI.motivation: 现有框监督实例分割方法主要基于掩码框架，难以直接生成精确多边形结果，且需昂贵的标注成本。
AI.method: 设计两点损失函数：基于点的单目损失约束预测多边形框实现粗分割，距离感知的成对损失优化边界拟合。
AI.result: 在Cityscapes数据集上显著优于基于掩码的弱监督方法，缩小分割结果与框标注的性能差距。
AI.conclusion: BoxSnake为仅依赖框标注的多边形实例分割提供了高效可行的新方案。

authors_text: Haotian Liu,Guang Chen,Sanqing Qu,Yanping Zhang,Zhijun Li,Alois Knoll,Changjun Jiang
title: TMA: Temporal Motion Aggregation for Event-based Optical Flow
title_zh: TMA：基于事件的光流的时间运动聚合
abstract: Event cameras have the ability to record continuous and detailed trajectories of objects with high temporal resolution, thereby providing intuitive motion cues for optical flow estimation. Nevertheless, most existing learning-based approaches for event optical flow estimation directly remould the paradigm of conventional images by representing the consecutive event stream as static frames, ignoring the inherent temporal continuity of event data. In this paper, we argue that temporal continuity is a vital element of event-based optical flow and propose a novel Temporal Motion Aggregation (TMA) approach to unlock its potential. Technically, TMA comprises three components: an event splitting strategy to incorporate intermediate motion information underlying the temporal context, a linear lookup strategy to align temporally fine-grained motion features and a novel motion pattern aggregation module to emphasize consistent patterns for motion feature enhancement. By incorporating temporally fine-grained motion information, TMA can derive better flow estimates than existing methods at early stages, which not only enables TMA to obtain more accurate final predictions, but also greatly reduces the demand for a number of refinements. Extensive experiments on DSEC-Flow and MVSEC datasets verify the effectiveness and superiority of our TMA. Remarkably, compared to E-RAFT, TMA achieves a 6\% improvement in accuracy and a 40\% reduction in inference time on DSEC-Flow. Code will be available at \url{https://github.com/ispc-lab/TMA}.
summary: Event cameras have the ability to record continuous and detailed trajectories of objects with high temporal resolution, thereby providing intuitive motion cues for optical flow estimation. Nevertheless, most existing learning-based approaches for event optical flow estimation directly remould the paradigm of conventional images by representing the consecutive event stream as static frames, ignoring the inherent temporal continuity of event data. In this paper, we argue that temporal continuity is a vital element of event-based optical flow and propose a novel Temporal Motion Aggregation (TMA) approach to unlock its potential. Technically, TMA comprises three components: an event splitting strategy to incorporate intermediate motion information underlying the temporal context, a linear lookup strategy to align temporally fine-grained motion features and a novel motion pattern aggregation module to emphasize consistent patterns for motion feature enhancement. By incorporating temporally fine-grained motion information, TMA can derive better flow estimates than existing methods at early stages, which not only enables TMA to obtain more accurate final predictions, but also greatly reduces the demand for a number of refinements. Extensive experiments on DSEC-Flow and MVSEC datasets verify the effectiveness and superiority of our TMA. Remarkably, compared to E-RAFT, TMA achieves a 6\% improvement in accuracy and a 40\% reduction in inference time on DSEC-Flow. Code will be available at \url{https://github.com/ispc-lab/TMA}.
abstract_zh: 事件摄像机能够以高时间分辨率记录连续且详细的物体轨迹，从而为光流估计提供直观的运动线索。然而，大多数现有的基于学习的事件光流估计方法通过将连续事件流表示为静态帧，直接重塑了传统图像的范式，忽略了事件数据固有的时间连续性。在本文中，我们认为时间连续性是基于事件的光流的重要元素，并提出了一种新颖的时间运动聚合（TMA）方法来释放其潜力。从技术上讲，TMA 包含三个组件：事件分割策略，用于合并时间上下文下的中间运动信息；线性查找策略，用于对齐时间细粒度运动特征；新颖的运动模式聚合模块，用于强调运动特征增强的一致模式。通过结合时间上的细粒度运动信息，TMA可以在早期阶段得到比现有方法更好的流量估计，这不仅使TMA能够获得更准确的最终预测，而且大大减少了对许多细化的需求。在 DSEC-Flow 和 MVSEC 数据集上进行的大量实验验证了我们的 TMA 的有效性和优越性。值得注意的是，与 E-RAFT 相比，TMA 在 DSEC-Flow 上的准确率提高了 6%，推理时间缩短了 40%。代码可在 \url{https://github.com/ispc-lab/TMA} 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于事件相机的时序运动聚合（TMA）方法，用于改进事件驱动的光流估计。
AI.motivation: 现有基于学习的事件光流估计方法将事件流视为静态帧，忽略了事件数据固有的时序连续性，导致精度和效率不足。
AI.method: TMA包含三个组件：事件分割策略以整合时序上下文中的运动信息、线性查找策略对齐细粒度运动特征，以及运动模式聚合模块强化一致模式以提升特征。
AI.result: 在DSEC-Flow和MVSEC数据集上，TMA相比E-RAFT方法在精度提升6%，推理时间减少40%。
AI.conclusion: TMA通过有效利用事件数据的时序连续性，显著提升了事件光流估计的性能和效率。

search_text: TMA: Temporal Motion Aggregation for Event-based Optical Flow TMA：基于事件的光流的时间运动聚合 Event cameras have the ability to record continuous and detailed trajectories of objects with high temporal resolution, thereby providing intuitive motion cues for optical flow estimation. Nevertheless, most existing learning-based approaches for event optical flow estimation directly remould the paradigm of conventional images by representing the consecutive event stream as static frames, ignoring the inherent temporal continuity of event data. In this paper, we argue that temporal continuity is a vital element of event-based optical flow and propose a novel Temporal Motion Aggregation (TMA) approach to unlock its potential. Technically, TMA comprises three components: an event splitting strategy to incorporate intermediate motion information underlying the temporal context, a linear lookup strategy to align temporally fine-grained motion features and a novel motion pattern aggregation module to emphasize consistent patterns for motion feature enhancement. By incorporating temporally fine-grained motion information, TMA can derive better flow estimates than existing methods at early stages, which not only enables TMA to obtain more accurate final predictions, but also greatly reduces the demand for a number of refinements. Extensive experiments on DSEC-Flow and MVSEC datasets verify the effectiveness and superiority of our TMA. Remarkably, compared to E-RAFT, TMA achieves a 6\% improvement in accuracy and a 40\% reduction in inference time on DSEC-Flow. Code will be available at \url{https://github.com/ispc-lab/TMA}. 事件摄像机能够以高时间分辨率记录连续且详细的物体轨迹，从而为光流估计提供直观的运动线索。然而，大多数现有的基于学习的事件光流估计方法通过将连续事件流表示为静态帧，直接重塑了传统图像的范式，忽略了事件数据固有的时间连续性。在本文中，我们认为时间连续性是基于事件的光流的重要元素，并提出了一种新颖的时间运动聚合（TMA）方法来释放其潜力。从技术上讲，TMA 包含三个组件：事件分割策略，用于合并时间上下文下的中间运动信息；线性查找策略，用于对齐时间细粒度运动特征；新颖的运动模式聚合模块，用于强调运动特征增强的一致模式。通过结合时间上的细粒度运动信息，TMA可以在早期阶段得到比现有方法更好的流量估计，这不仅使TMA能够获得更准确的最终预测，而且大大减少了对许多细化的需求。在 DSEC-Flow 和 MVSEC 数据集上进行的大量实验验证了我们的 TMA 的有效性和优越性。值得注意的是，与 E-RAFT 相比，TMA 在 DSEC-Flow 上的准确率提高了 6%，推理时间缩短了 40%。代码可在 \url{https://github.com/ispc-lab/TMA} 获取。 本文提出了一种基于事件相机的时序运动聚合（TMA）方法，用于改进事件驱动的光流估计。 Haotian Liu,Guang Chen,Sanqing Qu,Yanping Zhang,Zhijun Li,Alois Knoll,Changjun Jiang cs.CV

authors_text: Yao Zhu,Yuefeng Chen,Xiaodan Li,Rong Zhang,Xiang Tian,Bolun Zheng,Yaowu Chen
title: Information-containing Adversarial Perturbation for Combating Facial Manipulation Systems
title_zh: 用于对抗面部操纵系统的包含信息的对抗性扰动
