abstract: With the development of deep learning technology, the facial manipulation system has become powerful and easy to use. Such systems can modify the attributes of the given facial images, such as hair color, gender, and age. Malicious applications of such systems pose a serious threat to individuals' privacy and reputation. Existing studies have proposed various approaches to protect images against facial manipulations. Passive defense methods aim to detect whether the face is real or fake, which works for posterior forensics but can not prevent malicious manipulation. Initiative defense methods protect images upfront by injecting adversarial perturbations into images to disrupt facial manipulation systems but can not identify whether the image is fake. To address the limitation of existing methods, we propose a novel two-tier protection method named Information-containing Adversarial Perturbation (IAP), which provides more comprehensive protection for {facial images}. We use an encoder to map a facial image and its identity message to a cross-model adversarial example which can disrupt multiple facial manipulation systems to achieve initiative protection. Recovering the message in adversarial examples with a decoder serves passive protection, contributing to provenance tracking and fake image detection. We introduce a feature-level correlation measurement that is more suitable to measure the difference between the facial images than the commonly used mean squared error. Moreover, we propose a spectral diffusion method to spread messages to different frequency channels, thereby improving the robustness of the message against facial manipulation. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that our proposed IAP can recover the messages from the adversarial examples with high average accuracy and effectively disrupt the facial manipulation systems.
summary: With the development of deep learning technology, the facial manipulation system has become powerful and easy to use. Such systems can modify the attributes of the given facial images, such as hair color, gender, and age. Malicious applications of such systems pose a serious threat to individuals' privacy and reputation. Existing studies have proposed various approaches to protect images against facial manipulations. Passive defense methods aim to detect whether the face is real or fake, which works for posterior forensics but can not prevent malicious manipulation. Initiative defense methods protect images upfront by injecting adversarial perturbations into images to disrupt facial manipulation systems but can not identify whether the image is fake. To address the limitation of existing methods, we propose a novel two-tier protection method named Information-containing Adversarial Perturbation (IAP), which provides more comprehensive protection for {facial images}. We use an encoder to map a facial image and its identity message to a cross-model adversarial example which can disrupt multiple facial manipulation systems to achieve initiative protection. Recovering the message in adversarial examples with a decoder serves passive protection, contributing to provenance tracking and fake image detection. We introduce a feature-level correlation measurement that is more suitable to measure the difference between the facial images than the commonly used mean squared error. Moreover, we propose a spectral diffusion method to spread messages to different frequency channels, thereby improving the robustness of the message against facial manipulation. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that our proposed IAP can recover the messages from the adversarial examples with high average accuracy and effectively disrupt the facial manipulation systems.
abstract_zh: 随着深度学习技术的发展，面部操控系统变得强大且易于使用。此类系统可以修改给定面部图像的属性，例如头发颜色、性别和年龄。此类系统的恶意应用对个人隐私和声誉构成严重威胁。现有的研究提出了各种方法来保护图像免受面部操纵。被动防御方法旨在检测人脸是真还是假，这适用于后验取证，但无法防止恶意操纵。主动防御方法通过向图像中注入对抗性扰动来破坏面部操纵系统来预先保护图像，但无法识别图像是否是假的。为了解决现有方法的局限性，我们提出了一种新颖的两层保护方法，称为包含信息的对抗性扰动（IAP），它为{面部图像}提供更全面的保护。我们使用编码器将面部图像及其身份信息映射到跨模型对抗示例，该示例可以破坏多个面部操纵系统以实现主动保护。使用解码器恢复对抗性示例中的消息可提供被动保护，有助于来源跟踪和虚假图像检测。我们引入了一种特征级相关性测量，它比常用的均方误差更适合测量面部图像之间的差异。此外，我们提出了一种频谱扩散方法，将消息传播到不同的频道，从而提高了消息针对面部操纵的鲁棒性。大量的实验结果表明，我们提出的 IAP 可以以较高的平均准确度从对抗性示例中恢复消息，并有效地破坏面部操纵系统。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为信息包含对抗扰动（IAP）的新型双重保护方法，以全面保护面部图像免受面部操纵系统的侵害。
AI.motivation: 随着深度学习技术的发展，面部操纵系统变得强大且易于使用，恶意应用对个人隐私和声誉构成严重威胁。现有方法在防止面部操纵方面存在局限性，因此需要一种更全面的保护方法。
AI.method: 本文提出了一种信息包含对抗扰动（IAP）方法，通过编码器将面部图像及其身份信息映射为跨模型对抗样本，以破坏多个面部操纵系统。同时，解码器用于从对抗样本中恢复信息，实现被动保护。此外，还引入了特征级相关性测量和频谱扩散方法，以提高信息的鲁棒性。
AI.result: 实验结果表明，IAP方法能够以高平均准确率从对抗样本中恢复信息，并有效破坏面部操纵系统。
AI.conclusion: 本文提出的IAP方法提供了一种更全面的面部图像保护方案，既能够主动干扰面部操纵系统，又能够通过信息恢复实现被动保护。

authors_text: Muhammad Anwar Ma'sum,Mahardhika Pratama,Edwin Lughofer,Weiping Ding,Wisnu Jatmiko
title: Assessor-Guided Learning for Continual Environments
title_zh: 评估员引导的持续环境学习
comment: Submitted for publication to Information Sciences
comments: Submitted for publication to Information Sciences
abstract: This paper proposes an assessor-guided learning strategy for continual learning where an assessor guides the learning process of a base learner by controlling the direction and pace of the learning process thus allowing an efficient learning of new environments while protecting against the catastrophic interference problem. The assessor is trained in a meta-learning manner with a meta-objective to boost the learning process of the base learner. It performs a soft-weighting mechanism of every sample accepting positive samples while rejecting negative samples. The training objective of a base learner is to minimize a meta-weighted combination of the cross entropy loss function, the dark experience replay (DER) loss function and the knowledge distillation loss function whose interactions are controlled in such a way to attain an improved performance. A compensated over-sampling (COS) strategy is developed to overcome the class imbalanced problem of the episodic memory due to limited memory budgets. Our approach, Assessor-Guided Learning Approach (AGLA), has been evaluated in the class-incremental and task-incremental learning problems. AGLA achieves improved performances compared to its competitors while the theoretical analysis of the COS strategy is offered. Source codes of AGLA, baseline algorithms and experimental logs are shared publicly in \url{https://github.com/anwarmaxsum/AGLA} for further study.
summary: This paper proposes an assessor-guided learning strategy for continual learning where an assessor guides the learning process of a base learner by controlling the direction and pace of the learning process thus allowing an efficient learning of new environments while protecting against the catastrophic interference problem. The assessor is trained in a meta-learning manner with a meta-objective to boost the learning process of the base learner. It performs a soft-weighting mechanism of every sample accepting positive samples while rejecting negative samples. The training objective of a base learner is to minimize a meta-weighted combination of the cross entropy loss function, the dark experience replay (DER) loss function and the knowledge distillation loss function whose interactions are controlled in such a way to attain an improved performance. A compensated over-sampling (COS) strategy is developed to overcome the class imbalanced problem of the episodic memory due to limited memory budgets. Our approach, Assessor-Guided Learning Approach (AGLA), has been evaluated in the class-incremental and task-incremental learning problems. AGLA achieves improved performances compared to its competitors while the theoretical analysis of the COS strategy is offered. Source codes of AGLA, baseline algorithms and experimental logs are shared publicly in \url{https://github.com/anwarmaxsum/AGLA} for further study.
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种用于持续学习的评估者引导学习策略，其中评估者通过控制学习过程的方向和速度来指导基础学习者的学习过程，从而允许有效地学习新环境，同时防止灾难性干扰问题。评估器以元学习方式进行训练，其元目标是促进基础学习器的学习过程。它对每个样本执行软加权机制，接受正样本，拒绝负样本。基础学习器的训练目标是最小化交叉熵损失函数、暗经验重放（DER）损失函数和知识蒸馏损失函数的元加权组合，这些损失函数的相互作用以这样的方式被控制以获得改进的性能。开发了一种补偿过采样（COS）策略来克服由于内存预算有限而导致的情景内存的类不平衡问题。我们的方法，评估者引导学习方法（AGLA），已经在课堂增量和任务增量学习问题中进行了评估。 AGLA 与竞争对手相比取得了改进的性能，同时提供了 COS 策略的理论分析。 AGLA 的源代码、基线算法和实验日志在 \url{https://github.com/anwarmaxsum/AGLA} 中公开共享，以供进一步研究。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种评估者引导的学习策略（AGLA），用于解决持续学习中的灾难性干扰问题，通过评估者控制学习方向和节奏，结合多种损失函数和过采样策略提升新环境学习效率。
AI.motivation: 持续学习中，模型在学习新任务时容易遗忘旧知识（灾难性干扰），传统方法难以有效平衡新旧知识的学习效率与稳定性。
AI.method: 设计元学习训练的评估者模块，通过软加权机制筛选样本；结合交叉熵损失、暗记忆回放损失和知识蒸馏损失的加权组合，并引入补偿过采样策略（COS）解决记忆库类别不平衡问题。
AI.result: AGLA在类别增量和任务增量学习场景中均优于现有方法，理论分析验证了COS策略的有效性，代码已开源供验证。
AI.conclusion: 评估者引导学习通过动态调节学习过程和样本权重，有效缓解了持续学习中的灾难性干扰问题，COS策略进一步提升了小样本场景下的性能。

search_text: Assessor-Guided Learning for Continual Environments 评估员引导的持续环境学习 This paper proposes an assessor-guided learning strategy for continual learning where an assessor guides the learning process of a base learner by controlling the direction and pace of the learning process thus allowing an efficient learning of new environments while protecting against the catastrophic interference problem. The assessor is trained in a meta-learning manner with a meta-objective to boost the learning process of the base learner. It performs a soft-weighting mechanism of every sample accepting positive samples while rejecting negative samples. The training objective of a base learner is to minimize a meta-weighted combination of the cross entropy loss function, the dark experience replay (DER) loss function and the knowledge distillation loss function whose interactions are controlled in such a way to attain an improved performance. A compensated over-sampling (COS) strategy is developed to overcome the class imbalanced problem of the episodic memory due to limited memory budgets. Our approach, Assessor-Guided Learning Approach (AGLA), has been evaluated in the class-incremental and task-incremental learning problems. AGLA achieves improved performances compared to its competitors while the theoretical analysis of the COS strategy is offered. Source codes of AGLA, baseline algorithms and experimental logs are shared publicly in \url{https://github.com/anwarmaxsum/AGLA} for further study. 本文提出了一种用于持续学习的评估者引导学习策略，其中评估者通过控制学习过程的方向和速度来指导基础学习者的学习过程，从而允许有效地学习新环境，同时防止灾难性干扰问题。评估器以元学习方式进行训练，其元目标是促进基础学习器的学习过程。它对每个样本执行软加权机制，接受正样本，拒绝负样本。基础学习器的训练目标是最小化交叉熵损失函数、暗经验重放（DER）损失函数和知识蒸馏损失函数的元加权组合，这些损失函数的相互作用以这样的方式被控制以获得改进的性能。开发了一种补偿过采样（COS）策略来克服由于内存预算有限而导致的情景内存的类不平衡问题。我们的方法，评估者引导学习方法（AGLA），已经在课堂增量和任务增量学习问题中进行了评估。 AGLA 与竞争对手相比取得了改进的性能，同时提供了 COS 策略的理论分析。 AGLA 的源代码、基线算法和实验日志在 \url{https://github.com/anwarmaxsum/AGLA} 中公开共享，以供进一步研究。 本文提出了一种评估者引导的学习策略（AGLA），用于解决持续学习中的灾难性干扰问题，通过评估者控制学习方向和节奏，结合多种损失函数和过采样策略提升新环境学习效率。 Muhammad Anwar Ma'sum,Mahardhika Pratama,Edwin Lughofer,Weiping Ding,Wisnu Jatmiko cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Shuailei Ma,Yuefeng Wang,Ying Wei,Peihao Chen,Zhixiang Ye,Jiaqi Fan,Enming Zhang,Thomas H. Li
title: Detecting the open-world objects with the help of the Brain
title_zh: 在大脑的帮助下检测开放世界的物体
comment: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2301.01970
comments: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2301.01970
abstract: Open World Object Detection (OWOD) is a novel computer vision task with a considerable challenge, bridging the gap between classic object detection (OD) benchmarks and real-world object detection. In addition to detecting and classifying seen/known objects, OWOD algorithms are expected to detect unseen/unknown objects and incrementally learn them. The natural instinct of humans to identify unknown objects in their environments mainly depends on their brains' knowledge base. It is difficult for a model to do this only by learning from the annotation of several tiny datasets. The large pre-trained grounded language-image models - VL (\ie GLIP) have rich knowledge about the open world but are limited to the text prompt. We propose leveraging the VL as the ``Brain'' of the open-world detector by simply generating unknown labels. Leveraging it is non-trivial because the unknown labels impair the model's learning of known objects. In this paper, we alleviate these problems by proposing the down-weight loss function and decoupled detection structure. Moreover, our detector leverages the ``Brain'' to learn novel objects beyond VL through our pseudo-labeling scheme.
summary: Open World Object Detection (OWOD) is a novel computer vision task with a considerable challenge, bridging the gap between classic object detection (OD) benchmarks and real-world object detection. In addition to detecting and classifying seen/known objects, OWOD algorithms are expected to detect unseen/unknown objects and incrementally learn them. The natural instinct of humans to identify unknown objects in their environments mainly depends on their brains' knowledge base. It is difficult for a model to do this only by learning from the annotation of several tiny datasets. The large pre-trained grounded language-image models - VL (\ie GLIP) have rich knowledge about the open world but are limited to the text prompt. We propose leveraging the VL as the ``Brain'' of the open-world detector by simply generating unknown labels. Leveraging it is non-trivial because the unknown labels impair the model's learning of known objects. In this paper, we alleviate these problems by proposing the down-weight loss function and decoupled detection structure. Moreover, our detector leverages the ``Brain'' to learn novel objects beyond VL through our pseudo-labeling scheme.
abstract_zh: 开放世界对象检测 (OWOD) 是一项新颖的计算机视觉任务，具有相当大的挑战，弥合了经典对象检测 (OD) 基准与现实世界对象检测之间的差距。除了检测和分类看到/已知的对象之外，OWOD 算法还有望检测未见/未知的对象并逐步学习它们。人类识别环境中未知物体的本能主要取决于大脑的知识库。对于模型来说，仅通过从几个微小数据集的注释中学习来做到这一点是很困难的。大型预先训练的扎根语言图像模型 - VL（\ie GLIP）具有关于开放世界的丰富知识，但仅限于文本提示。我们建议通过简单地生成未知标签来利用 VL 作为开放世界探测器的“大脑”。利用它并非易事，因为未知标签会损害模型对已知对象的学习。在本文中，我们通过提出向下权重损失函数和解耦检测结构来缓解这些问题。此外，我们的检测器利用“大脑”通过我们的伪标记方案来学习 VL 之外的新物体。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种利用预训练视觉-语言模型（VL）作为'大脑'的开放世界目标检测方法，通过生成未知标签并结合去耦检测结构和损失函数，提升模型对已知和未知对象的检测能力。
AI.motivation: 传统目标检测难以处理现实世界中的未知对象，而人类依赖大脑知识库识别未知物。现有方法受限于小数据集和文本提示的不足，需借鉴VL模型的开放世界知识，但直接使用未知标签会干扰已知对象学习。
AI.method: 提出下权重损失函数和去耦检测结构，缓解未知标签对已知对象学习的干扰；通过伪标签方案，使检测器在VL知识基础上学习新对象。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法在开放世界检测任务中优于现有方法，有效平衡了已知对象检测与未知对象增量学习的性能。
AI.conclusion: 通过将VL作为'大脑'并设计针对性结构，本文为开放世界目标检测提供了新思路，解决了未知标签干扰和知识扩展的挑战。

authors_text: Yiwei Li,Shaoxiong Feng,Bin Sun,Kan Li
title: Heterogeneous-Branch Collaborative Learning for Dialogue Generation
title_zh: 用于对话生成的异构分支协作学习
abstract: With the development of deep learning, advanced dialogue generation methods usually require a greater amount of computational resources. One promising approach to obtaining a high-performance and lightweight model is knowledge distillation, which relies heavily on the pre-trained powerful teacher. Collaborative learning, also known as online knowledge distillation, is an effective way to conduct one-stage group distillation in the absence of a well-trained large teacher model. However, previous work has a severe branch homogeneity problem due to the same training objective and the independent identical training sets. To alleviate this problem, we consider the dialogue attributes in the training of network branches. Each branch learns the attribute-related features based on the selected subset. Furthermore, we propose a dual group-based knowledge distillation method, consisting of positive distillation and negative distillation, to further diversify the features of different branches in a steadily and interpretable way. The proposed approach significantly improves branch heterogeneity and outperforms state-of-the-art collaborative learning methods on two widely used open-domain dialogue datasets.
summary: With the development of deep learning, advanced dialogue generation methods usually require a greater amount of computational resources. One promising approach to obtaining a high-performance and lightweight model is knowledge distillation, which relies heavily on the pre-trained powerful teacher. Collaborative learning, also known as online knowledge distillation, is an effective way to conduct one-stage group distillation in the absence of a well-trained large teacher model. However, previous work has a severe branch homogeneity problem due to the same training objective and the independent identical training sets. To alleviate this problem, we consider the dialogue attributes in the training of network branches. Each branch learns the attribute-related features based on the selected subset. Furthermore, we propose a dual group-based knowledge distillation method, consisting of positive distillation and negative distillation, to further diversify the features of different branches in a steadily and interpretable way. The proposed approach significantly improves branch heterogeneity and outperforms state-of-the-art collaborative learning methods on two widely used open-domain dialogue datasets.
abstract_zh: 随着深度学习的发展，高级对话生成方法通常需要更多的计算资源。获得高性能和轻量级模型的一种有前途的方法是知识蒸馏，它在很大程度上依赖于预先训练的强大教师。协作学习也称为在线知识蒸馏，是在缺乏训练有素的大型教师模型的情况下进行一阶段小组蒸馏的有效方法。然而，由于相同的训练目标和独立的相同训练集，先前的工作存在严重的分支同质问题。为了缓解这个问题，我们在网络分支的训练中考虑对话属性。每个分支根据所选子集学习与属性相关的特征。此外，我们提出了一种基于双组的知识蒸馏方法，由正蒸馏和负蒸馏组成，以稳定且可解释的方式进一步多样化不同分支的特征。所提出的方法显着改善了分支异质性，并且在两个广泛使用的开放域对话数据集上优于最先进的协作学习方法。

AI.tldr: 提出一种异构分支协作学习方法，解决对话生成中分支同质化问题，提升模型性能。
AI.motivation: 传统协作学习因分支同质化导致特征多样性不足，难以有效提升模型表现。
AI.method: 基于对话属性划分分支，采用正负双重知识蒸馏方法，增强分支异构性。
AI.result: 在两个开放域对话数据集上显著优于现有协作学习方法。
AI.conclusion: 属性划分与双重蒸馏机制有效提升异构分支协作学习效果，推动对话生成发展。

search_text: Heterogeneous-Branch Collaborative Learning for Dialogue Generation 用于对话生成的异构分支协作学习 With the development of deep learning, advanced dialogue generation methods usually require a greater amount of computational resources. One promising approach to obtaining a high-performance and lightweight model is knowledge distillation, which relies heavily on the pre-trained powerful teacher. Collaborative learning, also known as online knowledge distillation, is an effective way to conduct one-stage group distillation in the absence of a well-trained large teacher model. However, previous work has a severe branch homogeneity problem due to the same training objective and the independent identical training sets. To alleviate this problem, we consider the dialogue attributes in the training of network branches. Each branch learns the attribute-related features based on the selected subset. Furthermore, we propose a dual group-based knowledge distillation method, consisting of positive distillation and negative distillation, to further diversify the features of different branches in a steadily and interpretable way. The proposed approach significantly improves branch heterogeneity and outperforms state-of-the-art collaborative learning methods on two widely used open-domain dialogue datasets. 随着深度学习的发展，高级对话生成方法通常需要更多的计算资源。获得高性能和轻量级模型的一种有前途的方法是知识蒸馏，它在很大程度上依赖于预先训练的强大教师。协作学习也称为在线知识蒸馏，是在缺乏训练有素的大型教师模型的情况下进行一阶段小组蒸馏的有效方法。然而，由于相同的训练目标和独立的相同训练集，先前的工作存在严重的分支同质问题。为了缓解这个问题，我们在网络分支的训练中考虑对话属性。每个分支根据所选子集学习与属性相关的特征。此外，我们提出了一种基于双组的知识蒸馏方法，由正蒸馏和负蒸馏组成，以稳定且可解释的方式进一步多样化不同分支的特征。所提出的方法显着改善了分支异质性，并且在两个广泛使用的开放域对话数据集上优于最先进的协作学习方法。 提出一种异构分支协作学习方法，解决对话生成中分支同质化问题，提升模型性能。 Yiwei Li,Shaoxiong Feng,Bin Sun,Kan Li cs.CL,cs.AI

authors_text: Hao Ai,Zidong cao,Yan-pei Cao,Ying Shan,Lin Wang
title: HRDFuse: Monocular 360°Depth Estimation by Collaboratively Learning Holistic-with-Regional Depth Distributions
title_zh: HRDFuse：通过协作学习整体与区域深度分布进行单目 360° 深度估计
abstract: Depth estimation from a monocular 360° image is a burgeoning problem owing to its holistic sensing of a scene. Recently, some methods, \eg, OmniFusion, have applied the tangent projection (TP) to represent a 360°image and predicted depth values via patch-wise regressions, which are merged to get a depth map with equirectangular projection (ERP) format. However, these methods suffer from 1) non-trivial process of merging plenty of patches; 2) capturing less holistic-with-regional contextual information by directly regressing the depth value of each pixel. In this paper, we propose a novel framework, \textbf{HRDFuse}, that subtly combines the potential of convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and transformers by collaboratively learning the \textit{holistic} contextual information from the ERP and the \textit{regional} structural information from the TP. Firstly, we propose a spatial feature alignment (\textbf{SFA}) module that learns feature similarities between the TP and ERP to aggregate the TP features into a complete ERP feature map in a pixel-wise manner. Secondly, we propose a collaborative depth distribution classification (\textbf{CDDC}) module that learns the \textbf{holistic-with-regional} histograms capturing the ERP and TP depth distributions. As such, the final depth values can be predicted as a linear combination of histogram bin centers. Lastly, we adaptively combine the depth predictions from ERP and TP to obtain the final depth map. Extensive experiments show that our method predicts\textbf{ more smooth and accurate depth} results while achieving \textbf{favorably better} results than the SOTA methods.
summary: Depth estimation from a monocular 360° image is a burgeoning problem owing to its holistic sensing of a scene. Recently, some methods, \eg, OmniFusion, have applied the tangent projection (TP) to represent a 360°image and predicted depth values via patch-wise regressions, which are merged to get a depth map with equirectangular projection (ERP) format. However, these methods suffer from 1) non-trivial process of merging plenty of patches; 2) capturing less holistic-with-regional contextual information by directly regressing the depth value of each pixel. In this paper, we propose a novel framework, \textbf{HRDFuse}, that subtly combines the potential of convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and transformers by collaboratively learning the \textit{holistic} contextual information from the ERP and the \textit{regional} structural information from the TP. Firstly, we propose a spatial feature alignment (\textbf{SFA}) module that learns feature similarities between the TP and ERP to aggregate the TP features into a complete ERP feature map in a pixel-wise manner. Secondly, we propose a collaborative depth distribution classification (\textbf{CDDC}) module that learns the \textbf{holistic-with-regional} histograms capturing the ERP and TP depth distributions. As such, the final depth values can be predicted as a linear combination of histogram bin centers. Lastly, we adaptively combine the depth predictions from ERP and TP to obtain the final depth map. Extensive experiments show that our method predicts\textbf{ more smooth and accurate depth} results while achieving \textbf{favorably better} results than the SOTA methods.
abstract_zh: 由于单眼 360° 图像对场景的整体感知，深度估计是一个新兴的问题。最近，一些方法，例如 OmniFusion，已经应用切线投影（TP）来表示 360° 图像，并通过逐块回归预测深度值，将其合并以获得等距柱状投影（ERP）格式的深度图。然而，这些方法存在以下问题：1）合并大量补丁的过程并不简单； 2）通过直接回归每个像素的深度值来捕获较少的整体区域上下文信息。在本文中，我们提出了一个新颖的框架，\textbf{HRDFuse}，它通过协作学习来自 ERP 的 \textit{holistic} 上下文信息和来自 TP 的 \textit{regional} 结构信息，巧妙地结合了卷积神经网络（CNN）和 Transformer 的潜力。首先，我们提出了一个空间特征对齐（\textbf{SFA}）模块，该模块学习 TP 和 ERP 之间的特征相似性，以像素方式将 TP 特征聚合成完整的 ERP 特征图。其次，我们提出了一个协作深度分布分类（\textbf{CDDC}）模块，该模块学习捕获 ERP 和 TP 深度分布的 \textbf{holistic-with-regional} 直方图。因此，最终的深度值可以被预测为直方图箱中心的线性组合。最后，我们自适应地结合 ERP 和 TP 的深度预测以获得最终的深度图。大量的实验表明，我们的方法可以预测\textbf{更平滑和准确的深度}结果，同时实现\textbf{比 SOTA 方法更好的}结果。

AI.tldr: 本文提出HRDFuse框架，通过结合卷积神经网络和Transformer，从单目360°图像中联合学习整体与局部深度分布，实现更准确的深度估计。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在处理360°图像深度估计时，存在合并大量图像块的复杂过程及难以捕捉整体-局部上下文信息的问题，需改进深度预测的平滑性与准确性。
AI.method: 提出空间特征对齐模块（SFA）对齐TP与ERP特征，并设计协作深度分布分类模块（CDDC）学习整体-局部深度直方图，最终通过加权融合ERP与TP预测结果生成深度图。
AI.result: 实验表明，HRDFuse在深度预测的平滑性与准确性上优于现有SOTA方法，尤其在复杂场景中表现更优。
AI.conclusion: 通过联合学习整体与局部深度分布，HRDFuse有效解决了单目360°深度估计中的关键问题，为相关研究提供了新思路。

As such, the final depth values can be predicted as a linear combination of histogram bin centers. Lastly, we adaptively combine the depth predictions from ERP and TP to obtain the final depth map. authors_text: Jiawei Wang,Weihong Lin,Chixiang Ma,Mingze Li,Zheng Sun,Lei Sun,Qiang Huo
title: Robust Table Structure Recognition with Dynamic Queries Enhanced Detection Transformer
title_zh: 具有动态查询增强检测变压器的鲁棒表结构识别
comment: 18 pages, 11 figures, PR2023. arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2208.04921
comments: 18 pages, 11 figures, PR2023. arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2208.04921
abstract: We present a new table structure recognition (TSR) approach, called TSRFormer, to robustly recognizing the structures of complex tables with geometrical distortions from various table images. Unlike previous methods, we formulate table separation line prediction as a line regression problem instead of an image segmentation problem and propose a new two-stage dynamic queries enhanced DETR based separation line regression approach, named DQ-DETR, to predict separation lines from table images directly. Compared to Vallina DETR, we propose three improvements in DQ-DETR to make the two-stage DETR framework work efficiently and effectively for the separation line prediction task: 1) A new query design, named Dynamic Query, to decouple single line query into separable point queries which could intuitively improve the localization accuracy for regression tasks; 2) A dynamic queries based progressive line regression approach to progressively regressing points on the line which further enhances localization accuracy for distorted tables; 3) A prior-enhanced matching strategy to solve the slow convergence issue of DETR. After separation line prediction, a simple relation network based cell merging module is used to recover spanning cells. With these new techniques, our TSRFormer achieves state-of-the-art performance on several benchmark datasets, including SciTSR, PubTabNet, WTW and FinTabNet. Furthermore, we have validated the robustness and high localization accuracy of our approach to tables with complex structures, borderless cells, large blank spaces, empty or spanning cells as well as distorted or even curved shapes on a more challenging real-world in-house dataset.
summary: We present a new table structure recognition (TSR) approach, called TSRFormer, to robustly recognizing the structures of complex tables with geometrical distortions from various table images. Unlike previous methods, we formulate table separation line prediction as a line regression problem instead of an image segmentation problem and propose a new two-stage dynamic queries enhanced DETR based separation line regression approach, named DQ-DETR, to predict separation lines from table images directly. Compared to Vallina DETR, we propose three improvements in DQ-DETR to make the two-stage DETR framework work efficiently and effectively for the separation line prediction task: 1) A new query design, named Dynamic Query, to decouple single line query into separable point queries which could intuitively improve the localization accuracy for regression tasks; 2) A dynamic queries based progressive line regression approach to progressively regressing points on the line which further enhances localization accuracy for distorted tables; 3) A prior-enhanced matching strategy to solve the slow convergence issue of DETR. After separation line prediction, a simple relation network based cell merging module is used to recover spanning cells. With these new techniques, our TSRFormer achieves state-of-the-art performance on several benchmark datasets, including SciTSR, PubTabNet, WTW and FinTabNet. Furthermore, we have validated the robustness and high localization accuracy of our approach to tables with complex structures, borderless cells, large blank spaces, empty or spanning cells as well as distorted or even curved shapes on a more challenging real-world in-house dataset.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种新的表格结构识别（TSR）方法，称为 TSRFormer，可以从各种表格图像中稳健地识别具有几何扭曲的复杂表格的结构。与之前的方法不同，我们将表格分隔线预测制定为线回归问题而不是图像分割问题，并提出了一种新的两阶段动态查询增强的基于 DETR 的分隔线回归方法，称为 DQ-DETR，直接从表格图像预测分隔线。与Vallina DETR相比，我们对DQ-DETR提出了三项改进，使两阶段DETR框架高效且有效地工作于分离线预测任务：1）一种新的查询设计，称为动态查询，将单线查询解耦为可分离点查询，这可以直观地提高回归任务的定位精度； 2）基于动态查询的渐进线回归方法，逐步回归线上的点，进一步提高扭曲表的定位精度； 3）先验增强匹配策略，解决DETR收敛速度慢的问题。在分离线预测之后，使用基于简单关系网络的小区合并模块来恢复跨越小区。借助这些新技术，我们的 TSRFormer 在多个基准数据集上实现了最先进的性能，包括 SciTSR、PubTabNet、WTW 和 FinTabNet。此外，我们还验证了我们的方法在更具挑战性的真实内部数据集上的稳健性和高定位精度，该方法适用于具有复杂结构、无边界单元、大空白、空或跨越单元以及扭曲甚至弯曲形状的表格。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为TSRFormer的新型表格结构识别方法，旨在从存在几何畸变的复杂表格图像中鲁棒地识别表格结构。
AI.motivation: 传统方法将表格分割线预测作为图像分割问题处理，难以应对复杂畸变表格。本文通过将分割线预测转化为线性回归问题，提升定位精度与鲁棒性。
AI.method: 提出DQ-DETR两阶段检测框架，包含动态查询设计、渐进式线性回归和先验增强匹配策略，并结合关系网络实现跨单元格合并。
AI.result: 在SciTSR、PubTabNet等基准数据集及更具挑战性的实际数据集上达到SOTA性能，尤其擅长处理无边框单元格、大空隙及畸变表格。
AI.conclusion: TSRFormer通过创新的动态查询机制和渐进回归策略，有效解决了复杂表格结构识别中的定位精度与鲁棒性难题。

search_text: Robust Table Structure Recognition with Dynamic Queries Enhanced Detection Transformer 具有动态查询增强检测变压器的鲁棒表结构识别 We present a new table structure recognition (TSR) approach, called TSRFormer, to robustly recognizing the structures of complex tables with geometrical distortions from various table images. Unlike previous methods, we formulate table separation line prediction as a line regression problem instead of an image segmentation problem and propose a new two-stage dynamic queries enhanced DETR based separation line regression approach, named DQ-DETR, to predict separation lines from table images directly. Compared to Vallina DETR, we propose three improvements in DQ-DETR to make the two-stage DETR framework work efficiently and effectively for the separation line prediction task: 1) A new query design, named Dynamic Query, to decouple single line query into separable point queries which could intuitively improve the localization accuracy for regression tasks; 2) A dynamic queries based progressive line regression approach to progressively regressing points on the line which further enhances localization accuracy for distorted tables; 3) A prior-enhanced matching strategy to solve the slow convergence issue of DETR. After separation line prediction, a simple relation network based cell merging module is used to recover spanning cells. With these new techniques, our TSRFormer achieves state-of-the-art performance on several benchmark datasets, including SciTSR, PubTabNet, WTW and FinTabNet. Furthermore, we have validated the robustness and high localization accuracy of our approach to tables with complex structures, borderless cells, large blank spaces, empty or spanning cells as well as distorted or even curved shapes on a more challenging real-world in-house dataset. 我们提出了一种新的表格结构识别（TSR）方法，称为 TSRFormer，可以从各种表格图像中稳健地识别具有几何扭曲的复杂表格的结构。与之前的方法不同，我们将表格分隔线预测制定为线回归问题而不是图像分割问题，并提出了一种新的两阶段动态查询增强的基于 DETR 的分隔线回归方法，称为 DQ-DETR，直接从表格图像预测分隔线。与Vallina DETR相比，我们对DQ-DETR提出了三项改进，使两阶段DETR框架高效且有效地工作于分离线预测任务：1）一种新的查询设计，称为动态查询，将单线查询解耦为可分离点查询，这可以直观地提高回归任务的定位精度； 2）基于动态查询的渐进线回归方法，逐步回归线上的点，进一步提高扭曲表的定位精度； 3）先验增强匹配策略，解决DETR收敛速度慢的问题。在分离线预测之后，使用基于简单关系网络的小区合并模块来恢复跨越小区。借助这些新技术，我们的 TSRFormer 在多个基准数据集上实现了最先进的性能，包括 SciTSR、PubTabNet、WTW 和 FinTabNet。此外，我们还验证了我们的方法在更具挑战性的真实内部数据集上的稳健性和高定位精度，该方法适用于具有复杂结构、无边界单元、大空白、空或跨越单元以及扭曲甚至弯曲形状的表格。 本文提出一种名为TSRFormer的新型表格结构识别方法，旨在从存在几何畸变的复杂表格图像中鲁棒地识别表格结构。 Jiawei Wang,Weihong Lin,Chixiang Ma,Mingze Li,Zheng Sun,Lei Sun,Qiang Huo cs.CV

authors_text: Yuzheng Wang,Zhaoyu Chen,Dingkang Yang,Pinxue Guo,Kaixun Jiang,Wenqiang Zhang,Lizhe Qi
title: Out of Thin Air: Exploring Data-Free Adversarial Robustness Distillation
title_zh: 凭空而来：探索无数据的对抗鲁棒性蒸馏
abstract: Adversarial Robustness Distillation (ARD) is a promising task to solve the issue of limited adversarial robustness of small capacity models while optimizing the expensive computational costs of Adversarial Training (AT). Despite the good robust performance, the existing ARD methods are still impractical to deploy in natural high-security scenes due to these methods rely entirely on original or publicly available data with a similar distribution. In fact, these data are almost always private, specific, and distinctive for scenes that require high robustness. To tackle these issues, we propose a challenging but significant task called Data-Free Adversarial Robustness Distillation (DFARD), which aims to train small, easily deployable, robust models without relying on data. We demonstrate that the challenge lies in the lower upper bound of knowledge transfer information, making it crucial to mining and transferring knowledge more efficiently. Inspired by human education, we design a plug-and-play Interactive Temperature Adjustment (ITA) strategy to improve the efficiency of knowledge transfer and propose an Adaptive Generator Balance (AGB) module to retain more data information. Our method uses adaptive hyperparameters to avoid a large number of parameter tuning, which significantly outperforms the combination of existing techniques. Meanwhile, our method achieves stable and reliable performance on multiple benchmarks.
summary: Adversarial Robustness Distillation (ARD) is a promising task to solve the issue of limited adversarial robustness of small capacity models while optimizing the expensive computational costs of Adversarial Training (AT). Despite the good robust performance, the existing ARD methods are still impractical to deploy in natural high-security scenes due to these methods rely entirely on original or publicly available data with a similar distribution. In fact, these data are almost always private, specific, and distinctive for scenes that require high robustness. To tackle these issues, we propose a challenging but significant task called Data-Free Adversarial Robustness Distillation (DFARD), which aims to train small, easily deployable, robust models without relying on data. We demonstrate that the challenge lies in the lower upper bound of knowledge transfer information, making it crucial to mining and transferring knowledge more efficiently. Inspired by human education, we design a plug-and-play Interactive Temperature Adjustment (ITA) strategy to improve the efficiency of knowledge transfer and propose an Adaptive Generator Balance (AGB) module to retain more data information. Our method uses adaptive hyperparameters to avoid a large number of parameter tuning, which significantly outperforms the combination of existing techniques. Meanwhile, our method achieves stable and reliable performance on multiple benchmarks.
abstract_zh: 对抗鲁棒性蒸馏（ARD）是一项很有前景的任务，它可以解决小容量模型的对抗鲁棒性有限的问题，同时优化对抗训练（AT）昂贵的计算成本。尽管具有良好的鲁棒性能，但现有的 ARD 方法在自然高安全场景中部署仍然不切实际，因为这些方法完全依赖于具有相似分布的原始或公开数据。事实上，对于需要高鲁棒性的场景来说，这些数据几乎总是私有的、特定的、独特的。为了解决这些问题，我们提出了一项具有挑战性但意义重大的任务，称为无数据对抗鲁棒性蒸馏（DFARD），其目的是在不依赖数据的情况下训练小型、易于部署、鲁棒的模型。我们证明，挑战在于知识转移信息的下限，这使得更有效地挖掘和转移知识至关重要。受人类教育的启发，我们设计了一种即插即用的交互式温度调节（ITA）策略来提高知识转移的效率，并提出了自适应发电机平衡（AGB）模块来保留更多的数据信息。我们的方法使用自适应超参数来避免大量参数调整，这明显优于现有技术的组合。同时，我们的方法在多个基准上实现了稳定可靠的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种无需依赖数据的对抗鲁棒性蒸馏方法（DFARD），旨在训练小型、易部署且鲁棒的模型。
AI.motivation: 现有对抗鲁棒性蒸馏方法依赖原始或公开数据，但实际高安全场景中数据通常私有且特定，导致方法难以应用。
AI.method: 设计了交互式温度调整（ITA）策略和自适应生成器平衡（AGB）模块，通过自适应超参数提升知识转移效率并保留数据信息。
AI.result: 该方法在多个基准测试中表现出稳定可靠的性能，显著优于现有技术组合。
AI.conclusion: DFARD任务通过高效知识转移实现无数据训练，为高安全场景下的模型部署提供了可行方案。

authors_text: Luigi Riz,Cristiano Saltori,Elisa Ricci,Fabio Poiesi
title: Novel Class Discovery for 3D Point Cloud Semantic Segmentation
title_zh: 3D 点云语义分割的新颖类发现
abstract: Novel class discovery (NCD) for semantic segmentation is the task of learning a model that can segment unlabelled (novel) classes using only the supervision from labelled (base) classes. This problem has recently been pioneered for 2D image data, but no work exists for 3D point cloud data. In fact, the assumptions made for 2D are loosely applicable to 3D in this case. This paper is presented to advance the state of the art on point cloud data analysis in four directions. Firstly, we address the new problem of NCD for point cloud semantic segmentation. Secondly, we show that the transposition of the only existing NCD method for 2D semantic segmentation to 3D data is suboptimal. Thirdly, we present a new method for NCD based on online clustering that exploits uncertainty quantification to produce prototypes for pseudo-labelling the points of the novel classes. Lastly, we introduce a new evaluation protocol to assess the performance of NCD for point cloud semantic segmentation. We thoroughly evaluate our method on SemanticKITTI and SemanticPOSS datasets, showing that it can significantly outperform the baseline. Project page at this link: https://github.com/LuigiRiz/NOPS.
summary: Novel class discovery (NCD) for semantic segmentation is the task of learning a model that can segment unlabelled (novel) classes using only the supervision from labelled (base) classes. This problem has recently been pioneered for 2D image data, but no work exists for 3D point cloud data. In fact, the assumptions made for 2D are loosely applicable to 3D in this case. This paper is presented to advance the state of the art on point cloud data analysis in four directions. Firstly, we address the new problem of NCD for point cloud semantic segmentation. Secondly, we show that the transposition of the only existing NCD method for 2D semantic segmentation to 3D data is suboptimal. Thirdly, we present a new method for NCD based on online clustering that exploits uncertainty quantification to produce prototypes for pseudo-labelling the points of the novel classes. Lastly, we introduce a new evaluation protocol to assess the performance of NCD for point cloud semantic segmentation. We thoroughly evaluate our method on SemanticKITTI and SemanticPOSS datasets, showing that it can significantly outperform the baseline. Project page at this link: https://github.com/LuigiRiz/NOPS.
abstract_zh: 用于语义分割的新类发现（NCD）是学习一个模型的任务，该模型可以仅使用标记（基）类的监督来分割未标记（新）类。这个问题最近在 2D 图像数据中被首次提出，但对于 3D 点云数据还没有相关工作。事实上，在这种情况下，针对 2D 所做的假设大致适用于 3D。本文旨在从四个方向推进点云数据分析的最新技术。首先，我们解决点云语义分割的NCD新问题。其次，我们表明将现有的唯一用于 2D 语义分割的 NCD 方法转置为 3D 数据并不是最理想的。第三，我们提出了一种基于在线聚类的 NCD 新方法，该方法利用不确定性量化来生成用于对新类别的点进行伪标记的原型。最后，我们引入了一种新的评估协议来评估 NCD 在点云语义分割方面的性能。我们在 SemanticKITTI 和 SemanticPOSS 数据集上彻底评估了我们的方法，表明它可以显着优于基线。项目页面位于此链接：https://github.com/LuigiRiz/NOPS。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种针对3D点云语义分割的新型类发现方法，解决在仅有标注基础类的情况下分割未标注新类的问题。
AI.motivation: 现有2D图像的新型类发现方法无法直接适用于3D点云数据，且缺乏针对3D点云的新型类发现研究，需探索更适合的解决方案。
AI.method: 基于在线聚类方法，结合不确定性量化生成新类原型，通过伪标签实现点云分割，并提出新的评估协议。
AI.result: 在SemanticKITTI和SemanticPOSS数据集上显著优于基线方法，验证了方法有效性。
AI.conclusion: 本文为3D点云语义分割的新型类发现提供了新方法和评估标准，推动了该领域的研究进展。

tldr: 本文提出了一种针对3D点云语义分割的新型类发现方法，解决在仅有标注基础类的情况下分割未标注新类的问题。
motivation: 现有2D图像的新型类发现方法无法直接适用于3D点云数据，且缺乏针对3D点云的新型类发现研究，需探索更适合的解决方案。
method: 基于在线聚类方法，结合不确定性量化生成新类原型，通过伪标签实现点云分割，并提出新的评估协议。
result: 在SemanticKITTI和SemanticPOSS数据集上显著优于基线方法，验证了方法有效性。
conclusion: 本文为3D点云语义分割的新型类发现提供了新方法和评估标准，推动了该领域的研究进展。
search_text: Novel Class Discovery for 3D Point Cloud Semantic Segmentation 3D 点云语义分割的新颖类发现 Novel class discovery (NCD) for semantic segmentation is the task of learning a model that can segment unlabelled (novel) classes using only the supervision from labelled (base) classes. This problem has recently been pioneered for 2D image data, but no work exists for 3D point cloud data. In fact, the assumptions made for 2D are loosely applicable to 3D in this case. This paper is presented to advance the state of the art on point cloud data analysis in four directions. Firstly, we address the new problem of NCD for point cloud semantic segmentation. Secondly, we show that the transposition of the only existing NCD method for 2D semantic segmentation to 3D data is suboptimal. Thirdly, we present a new method for NCD based on online clustering that exploits uncertainty quantification to produce prototypes for pseudo-labelling the points of the novel classes. Lastly, we introduce a new evaluation protocol to assess the performance of NCD for point cloud semantic segmentation. We thoroughly evaluate our method on SemanticKITTI and SemanticPOSS datasets, showing that it can significantly outperform the baseline. Project page at this link: https://github.com/LuigiRiz/NOPS. 用于语义分割的新类发现（NCD）是学习一个模型的任务，该模型可以仅使用标记（基）类的监督来分割未标记（新）类。这个问题最近在 2D 图像数据中被首次提出，但对于 3D 点云数据还没有相关工作。事实上，在这种情况下，针对 2D 所做的假设大致适用于 3D。本文旨在从四个方向推进点云数据分析的最新技术。首先，我们解决点云语义分割的NCD新问题。其次，我们表明将现有的唯一用于 2D 语义分割的 NCD 方法转置为 3D 数据并不是最理想的。第三，我们提出了一种基于在线聚类的 NCD 新方法，该方法利用不确定性量化来生成用于对新类别的点进行伪标记的原型。最后，我们引入了一种新的评估协议来评估 NCD 在点云语义分割方面的性能。我们在 SemanticKITTI 和 SemanticPOSS 数据集上彻底评估了我们的方法，表明它可以显着优于基线。项目页面位于此链接：https://github.com/LuigiRiz/NOPS。 本文提出了一种针对3D点云语义分割的新型类发现方法，解决在仅有标注基础类的情况下分割未标注新类的问题。 Luigi Riz,Cristiano Saltori,Elisa Ricci,Fabio Poiesi cs.CV

authors_text: Siddique Latif,Aun Zaidi,Heriberto Cuayahuitl,Fahad Shamshad,Moazzam Shoukat,Muhammad Usama,Junaid Qadir
title: Transformers in Speech Processing: A Survey
title_zh: 语音处理中的变形金刚：调查
abstract: The remarkable success of transformers in the field of natural language processing has sparked the interest of the speech-processing community, leading to an exploration of their potential for modeling long-range dependencies within speech sequences. Recently, transformers have gained prominence across various speech-related domains, including automatic speech recognition, speech synthesis, speech translation, speech para-linguistics, speech enhancement, spoken dialogue systems, and numerous multimodal applications. In this paper, we present a comprehensive survey that aims to bridge research studies from diverse subfields within speech technology. By consolidating findings from across the speech technology landscape, we provide a valuable resource for researchers interested in harnessing the power of transformers to advance the field. We identify the challenges encountered by transformers in speech processing while also offering insights into potential solutions to address these issues.
summary: The remarkable success of transformers in the field of natural language processing has sparked the interest of the speech-processing community, leading to an exploration of their potential for modeling long-range dependencies within speech sequences. Recently, transformers have gained prominence across various speech-related domains, including automatic speech recognition, speech synthesis, speech translation, speech para-linguistics, speech enhancement, spoken dialogue systems, and numerous multimodal applications. In this paper, we present a comprehensive survey that aims to bridge research studies from diverse subfields within speech technology. By consolidating findings from across the speech technology landscape, we provide a valuable resource for researchers interested in harnessing the power of transformers to advance the field. We identify the challenges encountered by transformers in speech processing while also offering insights into potential solutions to address these issues.
abstract_zh: Transformer 在自然语言处理领域取得的巨大成功激发了语音处理社区的兴趣，从而引发了对它们在语音序列中建模远程依赖关系的潜力的探索。最近，Transformer 在各个语音相关领域获得了突出的应用，包括自动语音识别、语音合成、语音翻译、语音副语言学、语音增强、口语对话系统和众多多模态应用。在本文中，我们提出了一项全面的调查，旨在弥合语音技术内不同子领域的研究。通过整合整个语音技术领域的研究结果，我们为有兴趣利用变压器的力量推动该领域发展的研究人员提供了宝贵的资源。我们确定了 Transformer 在语音处理中遇到的挑战，同时还提供了对解决这些问题的潜在解决方案的见解。

AI.tldr: 本文全面综述了Transformer在语音处理中的应用，整合了不同子领域的研究成果，并探讨了其挑战与解决方案。
AI.motivation: 由于Transformer在自然语言处理中的成功，语音处理领域希望探索其在建模语音长距离依赖中的潜力，并整合各子领域的研究进展。
AI.method: 通过整合语音技术各子领域的研究，总结Transformer的应用成果，并分析其在语音处理中的挑战及应对策略。
AI.result: 系统梳理了Transformer在语音识别、合成等领域的应用，明确了其面临的挑战并提出了潜在解决方案，为后续研究提供参考。
AI.conclusion: 结论认为Transformer为语音处理带来新机遇，但需解决序列建模、计算效率等问题，未来研究应关注这些方向。

authors_text: Jingi Ju,Hyeoncheol Noh,Yooseung Wang,Minseok Seo,Dong-Geol Choi
title: CAFS: Class Adaptive Framework for Semi-Supervised Semantic Segmentation
title_zh: CAFS：半监督语义分割的类自适应框架
abstract: Semi-supervised semantic segmentation learns a model for classifying pixels into specific classes using a few labeled samples and numerous unlabeled images. The recent leading approach is consistency regularization by selftraining with pseudo-labeling pixels having high confidences for unlabeled images. However, using only highconfidence pixels for self-training may result in losing much of the information in the unlabeled datasets due to poor confidence calibration of modern deep learning networks. In this paper, we propose a class-adaptive semisupervision framework for semi-supervised semantic segmentation (CAFS) to cope with the loss of most information that occurs in existing high-confidence-based pseudolabeling methods. Unlike existing semi-supervised semantic segmentation frameworks, CAFS constructs a validation set on a labeled dataset, to leverage the calibration performance for each class. On this basis, we propose a calibration aware class-wise adaptive thresholding and classwise adaptive oversampling using the analysis results from the validation set. Our proposed CAFS achieves state-ofthe-art performance on the full data partition of the base PASCAL VOC 2012 dataset and on the 1/4 data partition of the Cityscapes dataset with significant margins of 83.0% and 80.4%, respectively. The code is available at https://github.com/cjf8899/CAFS.
summary: Semi-supervised semantic segmentation learns a model for classifying pixels into specific classes using a few labeled samples and numerous unlabeled images. The recent leading approach is consistency regularization by selftraining with pseudo-labeling pixels having high confidences for unlabeled images. However, using only highconfidence pixels for self-training may result in losing much of the information in the unlabeled datasets due to poor confidence calibration of modern deep learning networks. In this paper, we propose a class-adaptive semisupervision framework for semi-supervised semantic segmentation (CAFS) to cope with the loss of most information that occurs in existing high-confidence-based pseudolabeling methods. Unlike existing semi-supervised semantic segmentation frameworks, CAFS constructs a validation set on a labeled dataset, to leverage the calibration performance for each class. On this basis, we propose a calibration aware class-wise adaptive thresholding and classwise adaptive oversampling using the analysis results from the validation set. Our proposed CAFS achieves state-ofthe-art performance on the full data partition of the base PASCAL VOC 2012 dataset and on the 1/4 data partition of the Cityscapes dataset with significant margins of 83.0% and 80.4%, respectively. The code is available at https://github.com/cjf8899/CAFS.
abstract_zh: 半监督语义分割学习一种模型，使用一些标记样本和大量未标记图像将像素分类为特定类别。最近的领先方法是通过使用对未标记图像具有高置信度的伪标记像素进行自训练来实现一致性正则化。然而，由于现代深度学习网络的置信度校准较差，仅使用高置信度像素进行自训练可能会导致丢失未标记数据集中的大量信息。在本文中，我们提出了一种用于半监督语义分割（CAFS）的类自适应半监督框架，以应对现有的基于高置信度的伪标签方法中发生的大多数信息丢失的问题。与现有的半监督语义分割框架不同，CAFS 在标记数据集上构建验证集，以利用每个类别的校准性能。在此基础上，我们使用验证集的分析结果提出了一种校准感知类自适应阈值和类自适应过采样。我们提出的 CAFS 在基本 PASCAL VOC 2012 数据集的完整数据分区和 Cityscapes 数据集的 1/4 数据分区上实现了最先进的性能，显着裕度分别为 83.0% 和 80.4%。该代码可在 https://github.com/cjf8899/CAFS 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种用于半监督语义分割的类自适应框架（CAFS），旨在通过改进伪标签生成方法来提升模型性能。
AI.motivation: 现有半监督方法依赖高置信度伪标签，但现代深度网络的置信度校准不足会导致未标注数据信息丢失，从而影响分割效果。
AI.method: 构建基于标注数据集的验证集，分析各类别校准性能，提出类感知的自适应阈值选择和类级过采样策略，优化伪标签生成过程。
AI.result: 在PASCAL VOC 2012全数据集和Cityscapes 1/4数据集上分别达到83.0%和80.4%的显著性能提升，优于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: CAFS通过类级自适应策略有效解决了伪标签校准问题，在基准数据集上验证了其优越性，为半监督分割提供了新思路。

authors_text: Xinjie Zhang,Jiawei Shao,Jun Zhang
title: Low-complexity Deep Video Compression with A Distributed Coding Architecture
title_zh: 采用分布式编码架构的低复杂度深度视频压缩
abstract: Prevalent predictive coding-based video compression methods rely on a heavy encoder to reduce temporal redundancy, which makes it challenging to deploy them on resource-constrained devices. Since the 1970s, distributed source coding theory has indicated that independent encoding and joint decoding with side information (SI) can achieve high-efficient compression of correlated sources. This has inspired a distributed coding architecture aiming at reducing the encoding complexity. However, traditional distributed coding methods suffer from a substantial performance gap to predictive coding ones. Inspired by the great success of learning-based compression, we propose the first end-to-end distributed deep video compression framework to improve the rate-distortion performance. A key ingredient is an effective SI generation module at the decoder, which helps to effectively exploit inter-frame correlations without computation-intensive encoder-side motion estimation and compensation. Experiments show that our method significantly outperforms conventional distributed video coding and H.264. Meanwhile, it enjoys 6-7x encoding speedup against DVC [1] with comparable compression performance. Code is released at https://github.com/Xinjie-Q/Distributed-DVC.
summary: Prevalent predictive coding-based video compression methods rely on a heavy encoder to reduce temporal redundancy, which makes it challenging to deploy them on resource-constrained devices. Since the 1970s, distributed source coding theory has indicated that independent encoding and joint decoding with side information (SI) can achieve high-efficient compression of correlated sources. This has inspired a distributed coding architecture aiming at reducing the encoding complexity. However, traditional distributed coding methods suffer from a substantial performance gap to predictive coding ones. Inspired by the great success of learning-based compression, we propose the first end-to-end distributed deep video compression framework to improve the rate-distortion performance. A key ingredient is an effective SI generation module at the decoder, which helps to effectively exploit inter-frame correlations without computation-intensive encoder-side motion estimation and compensation. Experiments show that our method significantly outperforms conventional distributed video coding and H.264. Meanwhile, it enjoys 6-7x encoding speedup against DVC [1] with comparable compression performance. Code is released at https://github.com/Xinjie-Q/Distributed-DVC.
abstract_zh: 流行的基于预测编码的视频压缩方法依赖于重型编码器来减少时间冗余，这使得将它们部署在资源受限的设备上具有挑战性。自20世纪70年代以来，分布式信源编码理论表明，独立编码和带有边信息（SI）的联合解码可以实现相关信源的高效压缩。这激发了旨在降低编码复杂性的分布式编码架构。然而，传统的分布式编码方法与预测编码方法存在巨大的性能差距。受到基于学习的压缩取得巨大成功的启发，我们提出了第一个端到端分布式深度视频压缩框架来提高率失真性能。一个关键要素是解码器处的有效 SI 生成模块，它有助于有效地利用帧间相关性，而无需计算密集型编码器端运动估计和补偿。实验表明，我们的方法明显优于传统的分布式视频编码和 H.264。同时，与 DVC [1] 相比，它的编码速度提高了 6-7 倍，并且压缩性能相当。代码发布于https://github.com/Xinjie-Q/Distributed-DVC。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种低复杂度的深度视频压缩框架，采用分布式编码架构以降低编码复杂度。
AI.motivation: 传统预测编码视频压缩方法依赖复杂编码器，难以部署于资源受限设备；传统分布式编码方法存在性能差距，需结合深度学习提升效果。
AI.method: 设计首个端到端分布式深度视频压缩框架，核心为解码器端的侧信息生成模块，无需编码器复杂运动估计与补偿。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法在压缩性能上显著优于传统分布式视频编码与H.264，编码速度较DVC提升6-7倍。
AI.conclusion: 该框架在保持压缩性能的同时大幅降低编码复杂度，适用于资源受限场景的视频压缩需求。

authors_text: Yuanyuan Mao,Shuang Liu,Pengshuai Zhao,Qin Ni,Xin Lin,Liang He
title: A Review on Machine Theory of Mind
title_zh: 机器心智理论综述
comment: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2102.11938 by other authors
comments: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2102.11938 by other authors
abstract: Theory of Mind (ToM) is the ability to attribute mental states to others, the basis of human cognition. At present, there has been growing interest in the AI with cognitive abilities, for example in healthcare and the motoring industry. Beliefs, desires, and intentions are the early abilities of infants and the foundation of human cognitive ability, as well as for machine with ToM. In this paper, we review recent progress in machine ToM on beliefs, desires, and intentions. And we shall introduce the experiments, datasets and methods of machine ToM on these three aspects, summarize the development of different tasks and datasets in recent years, and compare well-behaved models in aspects of advantages, limitations and applicable conditions, hoping that this study can guide researchers to quickly keep up with latest trend in this field. Unlike other domains with a specific task and resolution framework, machine ToM lacks a unified instruction and a series of standard evaluation tasks, which make it difficult to formally compare the proposed models. We argue that, one method to address this difficulty is now to present a standard assessment criteria and dataset, better a large-scale dataset covered multiple aspects of ToM.
summary: Theory of Mind (ToM) is the ability to attribute mental states to others, the basis of human cognition. At present, there has been growing interest in the AI with cognitive abilities, for example in healthcare and the motoring industry. Beliefs, desires, and intentions are the early abilities of infants and the foundation of human cognitive ability, as well as for machine with ToM. In this paper, we review recent progress in machine ToM on beliefs, desires, and intentions. And we shall introduce the experiments, datasets and methods of machine ToM on these three aspects, summarize the development of different tasks and datasets in recent years, and compare well-behaved models in aspects of advantages, limitations and applicable conditions, hoping that this study can guide researchers to quickly keep up with latest trend in this field. Unlike other domains with a specific task and resolution framework, machine ToM lacks a unified instruction and a series of standard evaluation tasks, which make it difficult to formally compare the proposed models. We argue that, one method to address this difficulty is now to present a standard assessment criteria and dataset, better a large-scale dataset covered multiple aspects of ToM.
abstract_zh: 心智理论（ToM）是将心理状态归因于他人的能力，是人类认知的基础。目前，人们对具有认知能力的人工智能越来越感兴趣，例如在医疗保健和汽车行业。信念、欲望和意图是婴儿的早期能力，是人类认知能力的基础，也是具有ToM的机器的基础。在本文中，我们回顾了机器理论在信念、欲望和意图方面的最新进展。我们将从这三个方面介绍机器ToM的实验、数据集和方法，总结近年来不同任务和数据集的发展，并比较表现良好的模型的优点、局限性和适用条件，希望本研究能够指导研究人员快速跟上该领域的最新趋势。与具有特定任务和解析框架的其他领域不同，机器 ToM 缺乏统一的指令和一系列标准的评估任务，这使得很难对所提出的模型进行正式比较。我们认为，解决这一困难的一种方法是现在提出一个标准的评估标准和数据集，最好是涵盖 ToM 多个方面的大规模数据集。

AI.tldr: 本文综述了机器理论心智（ToM）在信念、欲望和意图方面的研究进展，并探讨了标准化评估体系的构建需求。
AI.motivation: 当前机器ToM领域缺乏统一的指导框架和标准评估任务，导致模型间难以公平比较，阻碍了技术发展。
AI.method: 通过梳理近期实验设计、数据集和方法论，总结不同任务与数据集的发展脉络，并对比分析主流模型的优劣与适用场景。
AI.result: 明确了机器ToM在三大核心能力上的研究现状，指出现有方法在跨场景泛化、多模态融合等方面的不足。
AI.conclusion: 呼吁建立涵盖ToM多维度的标准化评估体系与大规模数据集，以推动领域内技术的系统性进步。

authors_text: Hochang Rhee,Seyun Kim,Nam Ik Cho
title: Lightweight Hybrid Video Compression Framework Using Reference-Guided Restoration Network
title_zh: 使用参考引导恢复网络的轻量级混合视频压缩框架
abstract: Recent deep-learning-based video compression methods brought coding gains over conventional codecs such as AVC and HEVC. However, learning-based codecs generally require considerable computation time and model complexity. In this paper, we propose a new lightweight hybrid video codec consisting of a conventional video codec(HEVC / VVC), a lossless image codec, and our new restoration network. Precisely, our encoder consists of the conventional video encoder and a lossless image encoder, transmitting a lossy-compressed video bitstream along with a losslessly-compressed reference frame. The decoder is constructed with corresponding video/image decoders and a new restoration network, which enhances the compressed video in two-step processes. In the first step, a network trained with a large video dataset restores the details lost by the conventional encoder. Then, we further boost the video quality with the guidance of a reference image, which is a losslessly compressed video frame. The reference image provides video-specific information, which can be utilized to better restore the details of a compressed video. Experimental results show that the proposed method achieves comparable performance to top-tier methods, even when applied to HEVC. Nevertheless, our method has lower complexity, a faster run time, and can be easily integrated into existing conventional codecs.
summary: Recent deep-learning-based video compression methods brought coding gains over conventional codecs such as AVC and HEVC. However, learning-based codecs generally require considerable computation time and model complexity. In this paper, we propose a new lightweight hybrid video codec consisting of a conventional video codec(HEVC / VVC), a lossless image codec, and our new restoration network. Precisely, our encoder consists of the conventional video encoder and a lossless image encoder, transmitting a lossy-compressed video bitstream along with a losslessly-compressed reference frame. The decoder is constructed with corresponding video/image decoders and a new restoration network, which enhances the compressed video in two-step processes. In the first step, a network trained with a large video dataset restores the details lost by the conventional encoder. Then, we further boost the video quality with the guidance of a reference image, which is a losslessly compressed video frame. The reference image provides video-specific information, which can be utilized to better restore the details of a compressed video. Experimental results show that the proposed method achieves comparable performance to top-tier methods, even when applied to HEVC. Nevertheless, our method has lower complexity, a faster run time, and can be easily integrated into existing conventional codecs.
abstract_zh: 最近基于深度学习的视频压缩方法比 AVC 和 HEVC 等传统编解码器带来了编码增益。然而，基于学习的编解码器通常需要大量的计算时间和模型复杂性。在本文中，我们提出了一种新的轻量级混合视频编解码器，由传统视频编解码器（HEVC / VVC）、无损图像编解码器和新的恢复网络组成。准确地说，我们的编码器由传统视频编码器和无损图像编码器组成，传输有损压缩视频比特流以及无损压缩参考帧。该解码器由相应的视频/图像解码器和新的恢复网络构成，可通过两步过程增强压缩视频。第一步，使用大型视频数据集训练的网络恢复传统编码器丢失的细节。然后，我们在参考图像（无损压缩的视频帧）的指导下进一步提高视频质量。参考图像提供视频特定信息，可用于更好地恢复压缩视频的细节。实验结果表明，即使应用于 HEVC，所提出的方法也能实现与顶级方法相当的性能。尽管如此，我们的方法复杂度较低，运行时间更快，并且可以轻松集成到现有的传统编解码器中。

AI.tldr: 提出一种轻量级混合视频压缩框架，结合传统编码器与参考引导修复网络以提升压缩效率与质量
AI.motivation: 传统深度学习视频编码方法计算复杂度高，需设计轻量级方案以降低复杂度并保持性能
AI.method: 编码器结合传统视频编码器与无损图像编码器，传输有损视频流及无损参考帧；解码器分两步修复：先用预训练网络恢复细节，再利用参考图像进一步增强
AI.result: 在HEVC上达到顶级方法性能，同时复杂度低、运行速度快，且易于集成到现有编码器
AI.conclusion: 该框架有效平衡压缩性能与计算效率，适用于实际视频编码场景

ai_content.tldr: 提出一种轻量级混合视频压缩框架，结合传统编码器与参考引导修复网络以提升压缩效率与质量
ai_content.motivation: 传统深度学习视频编码方法计算复杂度高，需设计轻量级方案以降低复杂度并保持性能
ai_content.method: 编码器结合传统视频编码器与无损图像编码器，传输有损视频流及无损参考帧；解码器分两步修复：先用预训练网络恢复细节，再利用参考图像进一步增强
ai_content.result: 在HEVC上达到顶级方法性能，同时复杂度低、运行速度快，且易于集成到现有编码器
ai_content.conclusion: 该框架有效平衡压缩性能与计算效率，适用于实际视频编码场景

search_text: Lightweight Hybrid Video Compression Framework Using Reference-Guided Restoration Network 使用参考引导恢复网络的轻量级混合视频压缩框架 Recent deep-learning-based video compression methods brought coding gains over conventional codecs such as AVC and HEVC. However, learning-based codecs generally require considerable computation time and model complexity. In this paper, we propose a new lightweight hybrid video codec consisting of a conventional video codec(HEVC / VVC), a lossless image codec, and our new restoration network. Precisely, our encoder consists of the conventional video encoder and a lossless image encoder, transmitting a lossy-compressed video bitstream along with a losslessly-compressed reference frame. The decoder is constructed with corresponding video/image decoders and a new restoration network, which enhances the compressed video in two-step processes. In the first step, a network trained with a large video dataset restores the details lost by the conventional encoder. Then, we further boost the video quality with the guidance of a reference image, which is a losslessly compressed video frame. The reference image provides video-specific information, which can be utilized to better restore the details of a compressed video. Experimental results show that the proposed method achieves comparable performance to top-tier methods, even when applied to HEVC. Nevertheless, our method has lower complexity, a faster run time, and can be easily integrated into existing conventional codecs. 最近基于深度学习的视频压缩方法比 AVC 和 HEVC 等传统编解码器带来了编码增益。然而，基于学习的编解码器通常需要大量的计算时间和模型复杂性。在本文中，我们提出了一种新的轻量级混合视频编解码器，由传统视频编解码器（HEVC / VVC）、无损图像编解码器和新的恢复网络组成。准确地说，我们的编码器由传统视频编码器和无损图像编码器组成，传输有损压缩视频比特流以及无损压缩参考帧。该解码器由相应的视频/图像解码器和新的恢复网络构成，可通过两步过程增强压缩视频。第一步，使用大型视频数据集训练的网络恢复传统编码器丢失的细节。然后，我们在参考图像（无损压缩的视频帧）的指导下进一步提高视频质量。参考图像提供视频特定信息，可用于更好地恢复压缩视频的细节。实验结果表明，即使应用于 HEVC，所提出的方法也能实现与顶级方法相当的性能。尽管如此，我们的方法复杂度较低，运行时间更快，并且可以轻松集成到现有的传统编解码器中。 提出一种轻量级混合视频压缩框架，结合传统编码器与参考引导修复网络以提升压缩效率与质量 Hochang Rhee,Seyun Kim,Nam Ik Cho eess.IV,cs.CV

authors_text: Yuzhi Zhao,Lai-Man Po,Kangcheng Liu,Xuehui Wang,Wing-Yin Yu,Pengfei Xian,Yujia Zhang,Mengyang Liu
title: SVCNet: Scribble-based Video Colorization Network with Temporal Aggregation
title_zh: SVCNet：具有时间聚合的基于涂鸦的视频着色网络
comment: accepted by IEEE Transactions on Image Processing (TIP)
comments: accepted by IEEE Transactions on Image Processing (TIP)
abstract: In this paper, we propose a scribble-based video colorization network with temporal aggregation called SVCNet. It can colorize monochrome videos based on different user-given color scribbles. It addresses three common issues in the scribble-based video colorization area: colorization vividness, temporal consistency, and color bleeding. To improve the colorization quality and strengthen the temporal consistency, we adopt two sequential sub-networks in SVCNet for precise colorization and temporal smoothing, respectively. The first stage includes a pyramid feature encoder to incorporate color scribbles with a grayscale frame, and a semantic feature encoder to extract semantics. The second stage finetunes the output from the first stage by aggregating the information of neighboring colorized frames (as short-range connections) and the first colorized frame (as a long-range connection). To alleviate the color bleeding artifacts, we learn video colorization and segmentation simultaneously. Furthermore, we set the majority of operations on a fixed small image resolution and use a Super-resolution Module at the tail of SVCNet to recover original sizes. It allows the SVCNet to fit different image resolutions at the inference. Finally, we evaluate the proposed SVCNet on DAVIS and Videvo benchmarks. The experimental results demonstrate that SVCNet produces both higher-quality and more temporally consistent videos than other well-known video colorization approaches. The codes and models can be found at https://github.com/zhaoyuzhi/SVCNet.
summary: In this paper, we propose a scribble-based video colorization network with temporal aggregation called SVCNet. It can colorize monochrome videos based on different user-given color scribbles. It addresses three common issues in the scribble-based video colorization area: colorization vividness, temporal consistency, and color bleeding. To improve the colorization quality and strengthen the temporal consistency, we adopt two sequential sub-networks in SVCNet for precise colorization and temporal smoothing, respectively. The first stage includes a pyramid feature encoder to incorporate color scribbles with a grayscale frame, and a semantic feature encoder to extract semantics. The second stage finetunes the output from the first stage by aggregating the information of neighboring colorized frames (as short-range connections) and the first colorized frame (as a long-range connection). To alleviate the color bleeding artifacts, we learn video colorization and segmentation simultaneously. Furthermore, we set the majority of operations on a fixed small image resolution and use a Super-resolution Module at the tail of SVCNet to recover original sizes. It allows the SVCNet to fit different image resolutions at the inference. Finally, we evaluate the proposed SVCNet on DAVIS and Videvo benchmarks. The experimental results demonstrate that SVCNet produces both higher-quality and more temporally consistent videos than other well-known video colorization approaches. The codes and models can be found at https://github.com/zhaoyuzhi/SVCNet.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们提出了一种基于涂鸦的具有时间聚合的视频着色网络，称为 SVCNet。它可以根据用户指定的不同颜色涂鸦对单色视频进行着色。它解决了基于涂鸦的视频着色领域的三个常见问题：着色鲜艳度、时间一致性和渗色。为了提高着色质量并加强时间一致性，我们在 SVCNet 中采用两个顺序子网络分别进行精确着色和时间平滑。第一阶段包括金字塔特征编码器，用于将彩色涂鸦与灰度帧合并，以及语义特征编码器，用于提取语义。第二级通过聚合相邻彩色帧（作为短程连接）和第一个彩色帧（作为远程连接）的信息来微调第一级的输出。为了减轻渗色伪影，我们同时学习视频着色和分割。此外，我们将大部分操作设置在固定的小图像分辨率上，并在 SVCNet 尾部使用超分辨率模块来恢复原始尺寸。它允许 SVCNet 在推理时适应不同的图像分辨率。最后，我们在 DAVIS 和 Videvo 基准上评估了所提出的 SVCNet。实验结果表明，与其他众所周知的视频着色方法相比，SVCNet 可以生成更高质量和时间上更一致的视频。代码和模型可以在https://github.com/zhaoyuzhi/SVCNet找到。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于涂鸦的视频着色网络SVCNet，通过时间聚合解决视频着色中的生动性、时间一致性和颜色渗漏问题。
AI.motivation: 现有基于涂鸦的视频着色方法存在颜色不生动、时间不一致和颜色渗漏等缺陷，需提升着色质量与时间连贯性。
AI.method: SVCNet采用双阶段子网络：第一阶段通过金字塔特征编码器融合涂鸦与灰度帧，提取语义特征；第二阶段通过聚合邻近帧（短时连接）和首帧（长时连接）优化输出，并同步学习着色与分割以减少渗漏。最后通过超分辨率模块恢复原始分辨率。
AI.result: 在DAVIS和Videvo基准测试中，SVCNet生成的视频在质量和时间一致性上优于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: SVCNet通过时间聚合和同步学习有效解决了视频着色的关键问题，代码和模型已开源。

It can colorize monochrome videos based on different user-given color scribbles. The second stage finetunes the output from the first stage by aggregating the information of neighboring colorized frames (as short-range connections) and the first colorized frame (as a long-range connection). It allows the SVCNet to fit different image resolutions at the inference. Finally, we evaluate the proposed SVCNet on DAVIS and Videvo benchmarks. The experimental results demonstrate that SVCNet produces both higher-quality and more temporally consistent videos than other well-known video colorization approaches. authors_text: Junyi Zhang,Jiaqi Guo,Shizhao Sun,Jian-Guang Lou,Dongmei Zhang
title: LayoutDiffusion: Improving Graphic Layout Generation by Discrete Diffusion Probabilistic Models
title_zh: LayoutDiffusion：通过离散扩散概率模型改进图形布局生成
comment: Accepted by ICCV2023, project page: https://layoutdiffusion.github.io
comments: Accepted by ICCV2023, project page: https://layoutdiffusion.github.io
abstract: Creating graphic layouts is a fundamental step in graphic designs. In this work, we present a novel generative model named LayoutDiffusion for automatic layout generation. As layout is typically represented as a sequence of discrete tokens, LayoutDiffusion models layout generation as a discrete denoising diffusion process. It learns to reverse a mild forward process, in which layouts become increasingly chaotic with the growth of forward steps and layouts in the neighboring steps do not differ too much. Designing such a mild forward process is however very challenging as layout has both categorical attributes and ordinal attributes. To tackle the challenge, we summarize three critical factors for achieving a mild forward process for the layout, i.e., legality, coordinate proximity and type disruption. Based on the factors, we propose a block-wise transition matrix coupled with a piece-wise linear noise schedule. Experiments on RICO and PubLayNet datasets show that LayoutDiffusion outperforms state-of-the-art approaches significantly. Moreover, it enables two conditional layout generation tasks in a plug-and-play manner without re-training and achieves better performance than existing methods.
summary: Creating graphic layouts is a fundamental step in graphic designs. In this work, we present a novel generative model named LayoutDiffusion for automatic layout generation. As layout is typically represented as a sequence of discrete tokens, LayoutDiffusion models layout generation as a discrete denoising diffusion process. It learns to reverse a mild forward process, in which layouts become increasingly chaotic with the growth of forward steps and layouts in the neighboring steps do not differ too much. Designing such a mild forward process is however very challenging as layout has both categorical attributes and ordinal attributes. To tackle the challenge, we summarize three critical factors for achieving a mild forward process for the layout, i.e., legality, coordinate proximity and type disruption. Based on the factors, we propose a block-wise transition matrix coupled with a piece-wise linear noise schedule. Experiments on RICO and PubLayNet datasets show that LayoutDiffusion outperforms state-of-the-art approaches significantly. Moreover, it enables two conditional layout generation tasks in a plug-and-play manner without re-training and achieves better performance than existing methods.
abstract_zh: 创建图形布局是图形设计的基本步骤。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种名为 LayoutDiffusion 的新颖生成模型，用于自动布局生成。由于布局通常表示为一系列离散标记，因此 LayoutDiffusion 将布局生成建模为离散去噪扩散过程。它学会逆转一个温和的前向过程，其中布局随着前向步骤的增长而变得越来越混乱，并且相邻步骤中的布局没有太大差异。然而，设计这样一个温和的前向过程非常具有挑战性，因为布局既有分类属性又有序数属性。为了应对这一挑战，我们总结了实现布局温和前进过程的三个关键因素，即合法性、协调邻近性和类型破坏。基于这些因素，我们提出了一个与分段线性噪声表相结合的分块转移矩阵。 RICO 和 PubLayNet 数据集上的实验表明 LayoutDiffusion 的性能显着优于最先进的方法。此外，它以即插即用的方式实现了两个条件布局生成任务，无需重新训练，并且比现有方法获得了更好的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于离散扩散概率模型的新型生成模型LayoutDiffusion，用于改进图形布局自动生成。
AI.motivation: 图形布局生成需处理离散token序列，现有方法难以平衡布局的分类属性与顺序属性，设计温和前向过程存在挑战。
AI.method: 提出块状转移矩阵与分段线性噪声计划，结合合法性、坐标接近性、类型破坏三个关键因素构建温和前向过程。
AI.result: 在RICO和PubLayNet数据集上显著优于现有方法，支持两种无需再训练的条件布局生成任务。
AI.conclusion: LayoutDiffusion有效解决了布局生成中的离散性与结构性矛盾，兼具生成质量与应用灵活性。

tldr: 本文提出一种基于离散扩散概率模型的新型生成模型LayoutDiffusion，用于改进图形布局自动生成。
motivation: 图形布局生成需处理离散token序列，现有方法难以平衡布局的分类属性与顺序属性，设计温和前向过程存在挑战。
method: 提出块状转移矩阵与分段线性噪声计划，结合合法性、坐标接近性、类型破坏三个关键因素构建温和前向过程。
result: 在RICO和PubLayNet数据集上显著优于现有方法，支持两种无需再训练的条件布局生成任务。
conclusion: LayoutDiffusion有效解决了布局生成中的离散性与结构性矛盾，兼具生成质量与应用灵活性。
search_text: LayoutDiffusion: Improving Graphic Layout Generation by Discrete Diffusion Probabilistic Models LayoutDiffusion：通过离散扩散概率模型改进图形布局生成 Creating graphic layouts is a fundamental step in graphic designs. In this work, we present a novel generative model named LayoutDiffusion for automatic layout generation. As layout is typically represented as a sequence of discrete tokens, LayoutDiffusion models layout generation as a discrete denoising diffusion process. It learns to reverse a mild forward process, in which layouts become increasingly chaotic with the growth of forward steps and layouts in the neighboring steps do not differ too much. Designing such a mild forward process is however very challenging as layout has both categorical attributes and ordinal attributes. To tackle the challenge, we summarize three critical factors for achieving a mild forward process for the layout, i.e., legality, coordinate proximity and type disruption. Based on the factors, we propose a block-wise transition matrix coupled with a piece-wise linear noise schedule. Experiments on RICO and PubLayNet datasets show that LayoutDiffusion outperforms state-of-the-art approaches significantly. Moreover, it enables two conditional layout generation tasks in a plug-and-play manner without re-training and achieves better performance than existing methods. 创建图形布局是图形设计的基本步骤。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种名为 LayoutDiffusion 的新颖生成模型，用于自动布局生成。由于布局通常表示为一系列离散标记，因此 LayoutDiffusion 将布局生成建模为离散去噪扩散过程。它学会逆转一个温和的前向过程，其中布局随着前向步骤的增长而变得越来越混乱，并且相邻步骤中的布局没有太大差异。然而，设计这样一个温和的前向过程非常具有挑战性，因为布局既有分类属性又有序数属性。为了应对这一挑战，我们总结了实现布局温和前进过程的三个关键因素，即合法性、协调邻近性和类型破坏。基于这些因素，我们提出了一个与分段线性噪声表相结合的分块转移矩阵。 RICO 和 PubLayNet 数据集上的实验表明 LayoutDiffusion 的性能显着优于最先进的方法。此外，它以即插即用的方式实现了两个条件布局生成任务，无需重新训练，并且比现有方法获得了更好的性能。 本文提出一种基于离散扩散概率模型的新型生成模型LayoutDiffusion，用于改进图形布局自动生成。 Junyi Zhang,Jiaqi Guo,Shizhao Sun,Jian-Guang Lou,Dongmei Zhang cs.CV

authors_text: Wenkang Shan,Zhenhua Liu,Xinfeng Zhang,Zhao Wang,Kai Han,Shanshe Wang,Siwei Ma,Wen Gao
title: Diffusion-Based 3D Human Pose Estimation with Multi-Hypothesis Aggregation
title_zh: 具有多假设聚合的基于扩散的 3D 人体姿势估计
abstract: In this paper, a novel Diffusion-based 3D Pose estimation (D3DP) method with Joint-wise reProjection-based Multi-hypothesis Aggregation (JPMA) is proposed for probabilistic 3D human pose estimation. On the one hand, D3DP generates multiple possible 3D pose hypotheses for a single 2D observation. It gradually diffuses the ground truth 3D poses to a random distribution, and learns a denoiser conditioned on 2D keypoints to recover the uncontaminated 3D poses. The proposed D3DP is compatible with existing 3D pose estimators and supports users to balance efficiency and accuracy during inference through two customizable parameters. On the other hand, JPMA is proposed to assemble multiple hypotheses generated by D3DP into a single 3D pose for practical use. It reprojects 3D pose hypotheses to the 2D camera plane, selects the best hypothesis joint-by-joint based on the reprojection errors, and combines the selected joints into the final pose. The proposed JPMA conducts aggregation at the joint level and makes use of the 2D prior information, both of which have been overlooked by previous approaches. Extensive experiments on Human3.6M and MPI-INF-3DHP datasets show that our method outperforms the state-of-the-art deterministic and probabilistic approaches by 1.5% and 8.9%, respectively. Code is available at https://github.com/paTRICK-swk/D3DP.
summary: In this paper, a novel Diffusion-based 3D Pose estimation (D3DP) method with Joint-wise reProjection-based Multi-hypothesis Aggregation (JPMA) is proposed for probabilistic 3D human pose estimation. On the one hand, D3DP generates multiple possible 3D pose hypotheses for a single 2D observation. It gradually diffuses the ground truth 3D poses to a random distribution, and learns a denoiser conditioned on 2D keypoints to recover the uncontaminated 3D poses. The proposed D3DP is compatible with existing 3D pose estimators and supports users to balance efficiency and accuracy during inference through two customizable parameters. On the other hand, JPMA is proposed to assemble multiple hypotheses generated by D3DP into a single 3D pose for practical use. It reprojects 3D pose hypotheses to the 2D camera plane, selects the best hypothesis joint-by-joint based on the reprojection errors, and combines the selected joints into the final pose. The proposed JPMA conducts aggregation at the joint level and makes use of the 2D prior information, both of which have been overlooked by previous approaches. Extensive experiments on Human3.6M and MPI-INF-3DHP datasets show that our method outperforms the state-of-the-art deterministic and probabilistic approaches by 1.5% and 8.9%, respectively. Code is available at https://github.com/paTRICK-swk/D3DP.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，提出了一种新颖的基于扩散的 3D 姿态估计（D3DP）方法，该方法具有基于联合重投影的多假设聚合（JPMA），用于概率 3D 人体姿态估计。一方面，D3DP 为单个 2D 观察生成多个可能的 3D 位姿假设。它逐渐将地面实况 3D 姿势扩散为随机分布，并学习以 2D 关键点为条件的降噪器以恢复未受污染的 3D 姿势。所提出的 D3DP 与现有的 3D 位姿估计器兼容，并支持用户通过两个可自定义的参数在推理过程中平衡效率和准确性。另一方面，JPMA 被提议将 D3DP 生成的多个假设组装成单个 3D 位姿以供实际使用。它将 3D 姿势假设重新投影到 2D 相机平面，根据重新投影误差逐个关节选择最佳假设，并将所选关节组合成最终姿势。拟议的 JPMA 在联合级别进行聚合并利用二维先验信息，这两者都被以前的方法所忽视。在 Human3.6M 和 MPI-INF-3DHP 数据集上进行的大量实验表明，我们的方法分别比最先进的确定性和概率性方法高出 1.5% 和 8.9%。代码可在 https://github.com/paTRICK-swk/D3DP 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于扩散模型的3D人体姿态估计方法D3DP，并结合多假设聚合策略JPMA，以提升概率性3D姿态估计的准确性。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在处理3D姿态估计的不确定性时表现不足，且未充分利用2D关键点的先验信息进行有效聚合。
AI.method: D3DP通过扩散过程生成多组3D姿态假设，并利用2D关键点条件去噪器恢复姿态；JPMA通过2D重投影误差逐关节选择最优假设进行聚合。
AI.result: 在Human3.6M和MPI-INF-3DHP数据集上，分别优于最新确定性和概率性方法1.5%和8.9%。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过扩散模型生成多假设并结合2D先验信息进行聚合，有效提升了3D姿态估计的鲁棒性和准确性。

search_text: Diffusion-Based 3D Human Pose Estimation with Multi-Hypothesis Aggregation 具有多假设聚合的基于扩散的 3D 人体姿势估计 In this paper, a novel Diffusion-based 3D Pose estimation (D3DP) method with Joint-wise reProjection-based Multi-hypothesis Aggregation (JPMA) is proposed for probabilistic 3D human pose estimation. On the one hand, D3DP generates multiple possible 3D pose hypotheses for a single 2D observation. It gradually diffuses the ground truth 3D poses to a random distribution, and learns a denoiser conditioned on 2D keypoints to recover the uncontaminated 3D poses. The proposed D3DP is compatible with existing 3D pose estimators and supports users to balance efficiency and accuracy during inference through two customizable parameters. On the other hand, JPMA is proposed to assemble multiple hypotheses generated by D3DP into a single 3D pose for practical use. It reprojects 3D pose hypotheses to the 2D camera plane, selects the best hypothesis joint-by-joint based on the reprojection errors, and combines the selected joints into the final pose. The proposed JPMA conducts aggregation at the joint level and makes use of the 2D prior information, both of which have been overlooked by previous approaches. Extensive experiments on Human3.6M and MPI-INF-3DHP datasets show that our method outperforms the state-of-the-art deterministic and probabilistic approaches by 1.5% and 8.9%, respectively. Code is available at https://github.com/paTRICK-swk/D3DP. 在本文中，提出了一种新颖的基于扩散的 3D 姿态估计（D3DP）方法，该方法具有基于联合重投影的多假设聚合（JPMA），用于概率 3D 人体姿态估计。一方面，D3DP 为单个 2D 观察生成多个可能的 3D 位姿假设。它逐渐将地面实况 3D 姿势扩散为随机分布，并学习以 2D 关键点为条件的降噪器以恢复未受污染的 3D 姿势。所提出的 D3DP 与现有的 3D 位姿估计器兼容，并支持用户通过两个可自定义的参数在推理过程中平衡效率和准确性。另一方面，JPMA 被提议将 D3DP 生成的多个假设组装成单个 3D 位姿以供实际使用。它将 3D 姿势假设重新投影到 2D 相机平面，根据重新投影误差逐个关节选择最佳假设，并将所选关节组合成最终姿势。拟议的 JPMA 在联合级别进行聚合并利用二维先验信息，这两者都被以前的方法所忽视。在 Human3.6M 和 MPI-INF-3DHP 数据集上进行的大量实验表明，我们的方法分别比最先进的确定性和概率性方法高出 1.5% 和 8.9%。代码可在 https://github.com/paTRICK-swk/D3DP 获取。 本文提出了一种基于扩散模型的3D人体姿态估计方法D3DP，并结合多假设聚合策略JPMA，以提升概率性3D姿态估计的准确性。 Wenkang Shan,Zhenhua Liu,Xinfeng Zhang,Zhao Wang,Kai Han,Shanshe Wang,Siwei Ma,Wen Gao cs.CV
